name: Coffee At Carltons
address: 36 Toronto St, Toronto, ON M5C 2C5, Canada
description: Tucked on Toronto St in the Financial District. Small, no-fuss, and consistently good -- the kind of spot you end up at every single weekday.

reviews.author: Meg
reviews.text:
Great little coffee shop tucked away in the old town neighbourhood :) (follow the sign and you’ll find Carlton and his coffee shop in the middle of the building lobby)

We got the iced latte and almond croissant  - both were great! The owner is also very kind and inviting

Will definitely be back for an iced matcha :)

reviews.author: Stefan Baerg
reviews.text:
Coffee is top notch. I had a flat white with oat milk. Flavor of the espresso shot came through nicely and the milk was silky smooth without any froth - perfect!

The chocolate croissant was also delicious - European style - light and flakey, and fresh.

Shosie also deserves a shoutout as the barista with a perfect pour!

Carlton’s is torontos best kept secret for now… not sure it will stay that way…

reviews.author: eryn stewart
reviews.text:
Wow, such kind and generous service. Thank you so much.
The flat white was also very yummy 🙏🏻

reviews.author: Julie Roy
reviews.text:
Don’t let Google fool you, the entrance is on Adelaide street.
Went there twice mornings of our short time in Toronto after my husband saw me looking at options for coffee on Google map. “The machine is a Slayer! Only super passionate people have this machine and the coffee is sure to be great”.
The coffee was indeed amazing and so was the barista. Super friendly, made me want to talk with him (which is not something I tend to do, especially in english!). One of our top “foodie” experiences in toronto!

reviews.author: Simon Roberts
reviews.text: Best coffee in the downtown core. Carlton is a wonderful owner; caring, consistent and with an eye for quality. If you’re looking for a regular spot for your downtown caffeine fix, give Carlton’s a look.

name: Moments
address: 904 Dundas St W Main Floor, Toronto, ON M6J 1W1, Canada
description: A warm, intimate cafe in downtown Toronto with a loyal local following. Great for slowing down with a well-made coffee and a quiet corner.

reviews.author: Kira S
reviews.text:
Had a good experience at Moments on Dundas St W. I ordered the matcha, which was smooth and perfectly balanced, and a flat white that was equally good. We also tried the Nutella donut and a peanut butter cookie, both were warmed up for us and absolutely delicious.

What really stood out was the customer service. They were attentive, kind, and even provided water while we waited for our drinks, which was a thoughtful touch. Overall, a great spot with excellent drinks, tasty treats, and very welcoming service. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Katerina
reviews.text:
I've heard a lot about this place and have been sincerely wanting to give it a try.

They have exceptional reviews for a reason. The food and coffee here are great.

What I had:
1.  Black Bean, Eggs & Cheddar Puff Pastry
2. Chana Ma Sala Puff Pastry (Vegan)
3. Pistachio Nuts Scone
4. Crème Brûlée Latte

Black Bean, Eggs & Cheddar Puff Pastry
It tasted delicious, costs $5.20. They refer to it as an empanada, but if you look at the picture it looks and tastes, more on the side of a puff pastry to me. It's very chewy, no crisp, but it is delicious.

Chana Ma Sala Puff Pastry (Vegan)
Very delicious, highly recommend and also costs $5.20. It's tastier, has a nice spice kick and sweet aftertaste. Has potato, onion and chickpeas. Very chewy too and the flakiness is perfect. Top favourite thing here.

Pistachio Nuts Scone
Costs $5.20 too. They serve it warm, tastes delicious by itself, but pair it with a coffee and it is mouthwatering. I had it with a latte and it's perfectly balanced. Not overly sweet or nutty, just perfect.

Crème Brûlée Lattee
Their flavoured lattes cost $5.75. Oh but again, it's perfect. Tastes smooth, balanced, even with syrup (from France, brand they use is Giffard) and the serving size, generous. Something you can definitely sit with, enjoy each sip and relax as you enjoy the atmosphere of the cafe.

Vegetarian/Vegan Options
1. Chana Ma Sala
2. For cafe drinks, milk alternatives they offer are: Soy, Coconut, Almond and Oat Milk

Atmosphere
The place is beautiful . Plenty of tables at the front and back. It has a charm, the tables, plates and cups all compliment each other. There are scenic photo shots of Toronto on the wall and as well, where I was sitting at least, beautiful artwork on display on the shelf across from me.

They have outlets for charging your devices if you're looking to get a bit of work done too.

Washrooms, huge, clean and very earthly vibes. Plenty of room for freshening up after having a coffee or bite to eat.

Conclusion
At this cafe, their vegan Chana Ma Sala pastry, as well as their Pistachio Nut Scone and Crème Brûlée Latte, are definite must-trys and incredibly delicious.

reviews.author: vivi touesnard
reviews.text:
Such a lovely place to spend some time with yourself. Latte was delicious and the pumpkin pecan scone went great with it.

Service was amazing. I felt very welcome. Shout out to Thanh who made the whole experience extra special! First cafe I've been to that  brought my order to the table and served water without asking. Just lovely~

reviews.author: Yoorim Lee
reviews.text:
It is one of the sweetest cafe in Toronto! The staff is super friendly and sweet. I ordered chestnut latte and apple caramel. Both of the orders were above average! Definitely recommend trying the bakegoods here.

It’s relatively quiet in the morning, perfect for anyone who would like to study or work (an outlet beside most of the tables).

reviews.author: fatemeh mosalla
reviews.text:
This was my little safe space in Toronto. I always enjoyed coming here for multiple reasons. They’ve got ann amazing and interesting food selection. I remember my first time coming here and I was just so intrigued by the flavor combinations. I always come back to see what new type of financier they have. The coffee always slaps too, and there are always seats available. The BEST part of this cafe is hands down their service. I am actually so impressed that every single time I step into this cafe, I’m met with nothing but kindness and just over the top service. The barista is always welcoming, sweet, and polite. He always brings the coffee to our table, and takes our plates once we’re done. It’s like restaurant level service at a cafe.

I genuinely recommend this place! The interior is minimalistic with cute photos of Toronto. It’s just such a sweet and cute spot.

Also, if you dine in they have really cute dishes.

name: 6x8 Market
address: 184 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L8, Canada
description: A tiny Kensington staple that punches above its square footage. Solid espresso, friendly faces, and a neighbourhood feel that's hard to fake.

reviews.author: Selina Yung
reviews.text: Cutest store ever! It is probably my favourite store in Kensington. So many curated goodies for souvenirs and gifts.

reviews.author: Pilar Johnson
reviews.text: Local arts, jewelry, gifts and more. A must see in Kensington market.

reviews.author: Mafer Rodriguez
reviews.text:
Awesome store with a great selection of local brands, from clothing to jewlery, accessories, artwork. The perfect spot for a birthday gift, or a self-gift 😉

The cafe inside the store sells awesome matcha. Will come again to try them all!

reviews.author: The Wandering Lamb
reviews.text: 6x8 Market is such a hidden gem! Their matcha is one of the best I’ve had — smooth, rich, and perfectly balanced. They carry a ton of unique and high-quality items, from specialty snacks to niche pantry goods and beautifully curated local products. It’s part café, part boutique grocer, and totally worth checking out if you’re into discovering cool new things. The vibe is cozy, modern, and super aesthetic. A great spot to sip and shop!

reviews.author: Pamela Johnston
reviews.text: Love this shop in Kensington Market.  They are showcasing all kinds of products created by artisans right here in Toronto.  Lots to pick from and some yummy Matcha  too!  Must see in the market

name: Sip Sip
address: 101 Yorkville Ave, Toronto, ON M5R 1C1, Canada
description: Yorkville's low-key answer to the neighbourhood's usual fanfare. Good coffee, relaxed setting, and none of the pretension you'd expect.

reviews.author: Daniela Fougere
reviews.text: Beautiful atmosphere, great food and coffee. While I was waiting for my latte; I was browsing the shop attached and the barista came right to me and gave me my drink 10/10 service!

reviews.author: Jacqueline Usher
reviews.text: Discovered this hidden gem in Yorkville while waiting for a friend. It’s tucked below Yorkville Avenue and a welcome quiet respite. I had the most wonderful matcha tea! Definitely worth a try!

reviews.author: Tara Raghunath
reviews.text: Beautifully in interior. Almost feels like you’re in Bali.

reviews.author: Laurie Herd
reviews.text: Excellent coffee, latte and shopping in a friendly, elegant and cozy neighbourhood spot

reviews.author: Robin Wang
reviews.text: Really nice coffee! Try the pistachio!!!

name: Kandl Kafé
address: 88A Avenue Rd, Toronto, ON M5R 2H2, Canada
description: A cozy Annex cafe with a warm neighbourhood-living-room feel. Great for slow mornings and catching up over a good flat white.

reviews.author: Jenny
reviews.text: Had the most incredible experience at Kandl! It was the perfect 'girls' outing'—the atmosphere is sophisticated, and the process of creating our own scents was so much fun. Everything from the staff to the aesthetic was 10/10. If you’re looking for a unique, high-quality experience with your friends, this is it. Can’t wait to go back!

reviews.author: Angelique Ulep
reviews.text: Bella was warm, friendly, and very attentive. She recommended a great drink that I really enjoyed. The cafe itself is aesthetically pleasing, with a welcoming atmosphere and a lovely scent throughout the space. Overall, it was a really pleasant experience!

reviews.author: Farah El-Tobgy
reviews.text: We did a candle workshop for our anniversary and absolutely enjoyed it. Bella was friendly and knowledgeable and made our first candle making experience fun!

reviews.author: Lia Naag
reviews.text: Kandl Kafe’s a must try! my friend and I ordered the Strawberry Matcha Latte and loved it. Bella is so friendly, makes the whole experience complete! 🫶🏻

reviews.author: Cassandra DiBiase
reviews.text: We did a candle making class and it was such an amazing experience! The whole setup was beautiful and so well organized, but what really made it special was Ri. She was hands down the best instructor — so patient, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about what she does. She explained everything clearly, helped us choose the perfect scents, and made the class feel relaxed and fun the entire time. You can tell she truly cares about creating a great experience. I would 100% recommend this class to anyone — and if you get Ri as your instructor, you’re in the best hands!

name: SOLARIS COFFEE
address: 2A St Patrick St, Toronto, ON M5T 1T9, Canada
description: Sleek and specialty-focused, SOLARIS brings serious coffee credentials to the downtown core without making it feel like a lecture.

reviews.author: May N.
reviews.text:
Very cute little coffee shop that’s mainly for take out. I’m not a coffee drinker but saw a matcha latte on their menu and had to try it.
The barista / manager (?) or owner (?) was very pleasant to speak to and wow!! I was amazed. The matcha latte (with oat milk) was absolutely delicious. I did get it with 100% sugar as it was my first time trying the matcha latte from here. Definitely would be coming back soon and will lower the sugar to see how it tastes. Also, they definitely do a great job whisking the matcha powder because it wasn’t grainy at all! 10/10!!

reviews.author: Zed Nad
reviews.text:
Saw this cafe while on the road and decided to try it. The smaller the cafe, the better the coffee. I tried their honeycomb latte with oat milk. 10/10
It was lovely meeting Ivy. Will definitely visit whenever I’m in the area.

reviews.author: Sylvie Peers
reviews.text: A little gem right here. (: their coffee is delicious and aromatic. The staff were kind and patient. Also thoughtful just incase they made my drink too sweet. But they didn't but I am happy they were considerate!

reviews.author: Janice Zhang
reviews.text: A cozy little coffee shop with friendly staff. Tried their tiramisu latte, just the right sweetness and so smooth! Definitely coming back again.

reviews.author: Haroun B
reviews.text:
Great and welcoming atmosphere, staff is very friendly and the coffee was excellent 👌

Had the Solaris Cream Latte, not too sweet, was perfect! Definitely recommend if you’re in the area!

name: Black wolf coffee
address: 717 Bay St., Toronto, ON M5G 2J9, Canada
description: A downtown espresso bar with a dark, focused aesthetic and drinks to match. Black Wolf is for people who take their coffee seriously.

reviews.author: David Y
reviews.text:
Great cozy atmosphere. Good strong expresso macciato and pretty good sea salt chocolate chip cookie. Quick service. Fair prices.

Overall, a good spot for a cup of coffee and a treat.

reviews.author: Rediet Andualem
reviews.text: Me and my cousin went here after hearing they having amazing coffee. We got dolce latte and matcha which was a bit disappointing , we had high hopes but it was mid , not bad not great. I would not recommend getting the matcha at all.

reviews.author: - IM
reviews.text:
Staff are super kind and caring and the vibes are so nice, I especially like the corner in the cafe where you can snuggle in.

I’m definitely going to come back here, I wish I could’ve eaten more but I didn’t want to be too big back 😭

The cafe is nice and quiet and I seen lots of people studying and reading and some friends just chatting as well.

reviews.author: Mat Gend
reviews.text: Nice little specialty coffee shop and perfect for a coffee on the go. There's not a lot of room to eat or drink on the spot around 6 to 8 tables, but we got lucky and found one outside. It's a good quality espresso prepared with care. We'll definitely come back during our stay. They sell a good variety of beans to bring home plus some coffee accessories like Aero Press and and pour over.

reviews.author: Louise
reviews.text: Lovely coffee in a lovely environment. But why hold the cup at the rim when there’s a perfectly good handle to use? I guarantee I won’t lick the handle. Thank goodness I had some sanitiser.  Also, no nut free snacks.

name: Ivy. Coffee Shop
address: 109 Queens Quay W, Toronto, ON M5J 2T5, Canada
description: Light-filled and inviting, Ivy is one of those downtown spots that feels like a relief. Good coffee, good energy, easy to like.

reviews.author: Evelyn Orogun
reviews.text: My husband and I stopped into Ivy Coffee Shop and it was one of the best experiences I’ve had at a cafe! The owner is very kind and made us feel very welcomed! The service was impeccable and you can tell they really care about the craft of coffee! We got the strawberry matcha cold foam and an iced biscoff latte along with a few baked goods and everything was deliciously handcrafted! They also have really high quality merch and other items to purchase! I highly recommend stopping here if you’re looking for cute vibes, great customer service, and yummy drinks! This was the best way to start our trip in Toronto!

reviews.author: Catherine Ramkhalawan
reviews.text:
I live in the area and came to check out this café after coming across them on socials. It's nestled along Harbourfront, just across Love Park. I had their iced strawberry matcha latte and brownie. Both very good and delicious. It was a beautiful day to walk my dog at Love Park and stop by for a drink.

The café itself is on the smaller side with only a few seats available inside with a small table along the wall. It is definitely more of a grab-and-go place.

I look forward to trying out their other drinks soon. I will definitely be back!

reviews.author: Bill Lakie
reviews.text: A small unassuming shop but serving delicious coffee. The seating options are limited to about 8-12 people. We were here on Christmas Eve, so it was a little chilly to be outside. But in the warmer weather this is a prime spot located across from a park where one could enjoy the sun and a really good coffee. They served the standard coffee choices of a choice coffee spot. They also offered seasonal specialty coffees. We also enjoyed a very delicious pistachio croissant.   There were people passing through the shop picking up coffee. It is an obvious favorite local spot. As I am here for work frequently I will certainly be back.

reviews.author: Eat In Ontario Blog
reviews.text:
Most people find pearls in the ocean… I found one by the lake. Tucked along Toronto’s Harbourfront, Ivy Coffee Shop is a hidden spot with European charm, chic aesthetics, and the kind of atmosphere that makes you want to stay a little longer than planned. It’s the kind of café that feels both cozy and elevated, where every detail is crafted with thought.

The almond croissant was a standout — golden and flaky on the outside, with layers that broke apart beautifully at every bite. Inside, the soft almond filling added just enough sweetness without overpowering, making it the perfect companion to a good latte. It felt like something straight out of a Parisian café, but right here by Lake Ontario.

However, the drinks were where it became interesting. While they offer all the classic coffees, it’s their specialty lattes that set them apart. The ube latte with cold foam and an espresso shot struck the perfect balance — smooth, creamy, and lightly sweet, with a caffeine kick that made it ideal for anyone settling in for a late-night grind. The strawberry matcha cold foam brought something entirely different to the table: earthy matcha rounded out with a fruity lift, a drink that was playful yet balanced and refreshing to the last sip. Both showed off the café’s knack for innovation and creativity.

Beyond the menu, Ivy leans into lifestyle with its own merch line: everything from sweaters and t-shirts to mugs, teacups, and pins. It adds a layer of personality and makes the brand feel like something you can take with you, long after the last sip of your drink.

Between the pastries, the inventive drinks, and the Italian gelato and quiches also on offer, Ivy. Coffee Shop felt like a European escape on the edge of Lake Ontario. Whether starting the day with a lakeside walk or winding down in the evening, it’s the kind of hidden pearl you’ll be glad you discovered. 🌊☕️

To hear more about this review, follow us @eatinontario on Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, & Facebook. ✨🤩🙏

reviews.author: Christina Ru
reviews.text: I live nearby and found myself coming back to this cafe whenever I get the chance. I especially love their strawberry matcha foam latte and black sesame hojicha latte which are both so tasty! Not a huge fan of the ube latte though. They also use oat milk as default I think. Love them!

name: Hailed Coffee - Elizabeth St.
address: 112 Elizabeth St Unit 2, Toronto, ON M5G 1P5, Canada
description: A compact Elizabeth St cafe with well-pulled shots and a focused menu. Hailed is a quiet alternative to the busier options nearby.

reviews.author: Aarez Hassan Brohi
reviews.text:
Easily one of my top three coffee shops in all of Toronto. The quality is consistently great, with the espresso pulled flawlessly and you can tell the beans are roasted with real care. The menu has unique drinks that still feel thoughtful and balanced.

My favourites are Hailed Latte (hot) or the Iced Cardamella (cold). If you want something to snack on, their croissants are great as well!

reviews.author: abraham nash
reviews.text:
Superb experience. The coffees are excellent—I especially enjoy the pistachio one (decaf) in the mornings. The staff are always professional and friendly.
I ordered a matcha latte with soya milk today, and having recently returned from Japan—where matcha is hard to beat for smoothness and consistency—I was genuinely impressed. Whoever made it today matched that experience, which was a very pleasant surprise.
Top rating for this place. A truly great spot.

reviews.author: Helmi Ansari
reviews.text: Very cute little coffee shop with excellent coffee and great to go breakfast items and even Canadian made chocolate and snacks. Very friendly and helpful batista as well.  Highly recommended for a quick on the go cup!

reviews.author: lesia Mykhailyshyn
reviews.text:
Amazing addition to the neighborhood! Hailed Coffee has always been my favorite coffee stop in Toronto, and everything about it is just perfect - from the moment you open the door to the warm goodbye from the great baristas.

I’m so happy for the team - huge congratulations on opening a new location! The best coffee, the best people, and the best service you can find. Always Hailed in my heart.❤️ ☕️ My highest recommendation - a must-visit spot for everyone!

reviews.author: Devyani Khosla
reviews.text:
This Hailed’s Coffee location is such a great addition to the area. It’s tucked in a quiet corner, which makes it feel relaxed and inviting. The space is cozy, and the atmosphere is perfect for enjoying a great cup of coffee.

Josephine was wonderful—so warm, friendly, and genuinely personable. She took the time to recommend a latte, and it was spot on. The coffee tasted fresh and well-made, and I’m already looking forward to trying other drinks on the menu.

If you’re nearby and want great coffee with equally great service, this is the place to go.

name: Mystik Cafe
address: 56C Kensington Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2K1, Canada
description: Kensington's most eclectic cafe, where the coffee is solid and the vibe is something you have to experience. Reliably weird in the best way.

reviews.author: Kassandra Ouimet
reviews.text:
We had a great time at Mystic Cafe in Toronto! We honestly traveled from Ottawa very excited to come visit this unique experience. My boyfriend has loved and advocated for reptiles his entire life and having this experience was truly amazing. I’m only slightly disappointed in how much we actually had the chance to touch the animals. We payed (a little expensive) to have a full hands on experience but weren’t able to hold most of them. I understand they were in shed but for the ones that weren’t the staff did not seem eager to explain or allow us to hold them and/or would tell us they can’t get them out. From videos and pictures on social media I was disappointed in the experience that we received. We had seen videos of the large lizard coming out to be fed or walk across peoples arms but when we asked if they ever take it out they told us no. When we would ask questions the answers were short or not able to be answered. What I had seen on social media was not what we received.
However, staff were very welcoming and kind just seemed a little unorganized even for a small group visiting.
When we come back to Toronto we will likely stop by again but hope for a more hands on experience like we payed for.
Thank you mystic cafe for giving us a fun and different type of outing.

reviews.author: Tom
reviews.text:
My kids brought me here to help me face my fear of reptiles. Where I grew up, these animals were considered dangerous, so I’ll admit I arrived a little tense and ready to keep my distance.

To my surprise, Mystik Cafe completely changed my perspective. The staff were calm, knowledgeable, and reassuring, and somehow managed to make reptiles feel less “run for your life” and more “actually kind of fascinating.” I felt safe the entire time, and even caught myself asking questions which my kids found very amusing.

The space itself is peaceful and well thought out, blending education with fun in a way that works for both kids and adults who need a bit more convincing. Every staff member was warm and welcoming, something you don’t see enough of these days in coffee shops.

I also enjoyed a cheddar chive savoury scone that was toasted perfectly, with butter on the side (a small detail, but a very appreciated one). The coffee was excellent.

I’d happily bring my kids back again. It’s a great way to support their love for reptiles without turning my house into one. which, at this stage in life, I consider a win.

reviews.author: Prehistoric Terminator
reviews.text: Amazing collection of unique reptiles, fish, invertebrates, and amphibians that through the VIP tour you can interact with. I truly enjoyed visiting here with my beloved girlfriend (a bio major) and we both had an unforgettable experience with these majestic creatures! They also do educational workshops as well for people interested in keeping reptiles as well as terrarium workshops. Davey, Jackie, Millie, Muri, and Sher were alll very informative on all the animals here as well as how to care for them! Staff are super friendly and accommodating. Would strongly recommend visiting whether you are a wildlife lover or trying to get your loved ones, friends, and family to love them as well!

reviews.author: Marlowe Clegg
reviews.text: A truly unique and enlightening experience! The staff are eager to welcome you and make your experience special. Every animal here has a beautifully-crafted enclosure with its welfare and comfort in mind. I came here with my boyfriend (a reptile nerd) and his excitement and enthusiasm were contagious; he was in his element and my experience was elevated because of it. Bring your friends and loved ones here -- you're bound to have an amazing experience, reptile lover or not! No matter where you stand, you'll come away having a new or enhanced appreciation for these beautiful animals. Thank you to all the amazing staff for making this possible!!

reviews.author: Peter Wang
reviews.text: This place is truly a hidden gem. Other than serving as a cafe, it has an exhibition room with around 30, if not more, species of live reptiles and amphibians, most of which are quite exotic and rare as far as I know. As an animal enthusiast, I was delighted to see such an “animal plus cafe” establishment come to Toronto. I paid for a 15-minute self-guided tour which cost $15, while there are other options that allow for longer viewing time and also interaction with the animals.

name: EPOS Cafe & Restaurant
address: 90 Yorkville Ave, Toronto, ON M5R 1B9, Canada
description: A Yorkville all-day spot that handles breakfast and lunch as well as it handles coffee. Comfortable, consistent, and a bit of a hidden gem.

reviews.author: Roya Baryole
reviews.text:
Stepping into this cafe & restaurant in Yorkville truly feels like being transported to Paris. The space is elegant, intimate, and beautifully designed — refined without feeling pretentious.

The croissants are exceptional and pair perfectly with their coffee selection. What impressed me most, however, was the elevated brunch menu. This is far more than a casual café — it feels like a fine-dining brunch experience.

The crêpes are a must-order. The Wild Mushroom Crêpe was rich and savoury, perfectly balanced in flavour. The Crêpe Suzette was equally memorable — delicate with caramelized citrus notes, finished with orange sauce and cognac. Absolutely delicious.

If you’re looking for Parisian elegance in the heart of Yorkville, this café delivers. A wonderful addition to the neighbourhood.

reviews.author: Jirayr Pambukyan
reviews.text:
Had a beautiful experience at Epos Restaurant.We went there with my wife and it was absolutely amazing!
The oysters were fresh and perfectly chilled, and the French toast was absolutely delicious  soft, balanced, and not overly sweet.
Great atmosphere and lovely service. Will definitely be back

reviews.author: Jemy Joseph
reviews.text:
A newer restaurant in the Yorkville area which has a cafe and a restaurant. Excellent customer service and really appreciated the owner checking in with us multiple times! She was very receptive to feedback. French themed decor and the nailed the theme and ambiance!

Food: I had the duck confit croissant and salad, and salad; my friend had the benedict. Both were delicious. For dessert, we closed off with mango moose cake. I wasn't a fan of the shape of the dessert but The taste was delicious.

reviews.author: Mike U
reviews.text: I had brunch with a friend at this amazing cafe, and we absolutely loved the egg benedict royal! The hollandaise sauce was so light and delicate, unlike anything else we’ve had. Plus, the smoked salmon was delicious and fresh. Then, we tried the farmhouse scramble, which was served on a delicious, crispy, airy sourdough bread, topped with a bed of onion jam that was sweet and perfectly complemented the scrambled eggs. We also tried the exclusive Epos benedict, which was a fascinating twist on a classic benedict. It was served on a classic English muffin with a delicious chicken cutlet wrapped in bacon, topped with a fried poached egg that was crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, and finished with a delicious truffle velouté at the bottom. Overall, it was an amazing place and I’d definitely visit again!

reviews.author: Alaleh - FoodyTO
reviews.text:
Epos cafe offers the authentic Parisian cafe vibes in the heart of Yorkville. The space is elegant yet cozy with a beautiful patio (summertime) and two separate indoor dining areas; one can be used for private events, too.

We visited here for a weekday brunch and enjoyed tasty and fresh meal, in a calm atmosphere with great service.

We ordered “Epos Royal Benedict”, “Burrata & Pear” and “French Waffles”. We loved the sweet and savory combination of burrata, pear and pesto sauce; very unique and delicious. Epos Royal Benedict is both beautifully served and equally delicious. We also loved the house-made cream with the waffles. Everything was fresh and tasty.

We finished on a sweet note with “Raspberry Mousse Cake” and “Coffee Bean Mousse” and paired them with Cappuccino and Matcha Latte. The desserts are creative and right amount of sweetness.

The staff are very friendly and attentive. The cafe is accessible by subway Lone 2 and there’s a Green P parking close by if you’re driving.

name: Daily Dose Coffee Bar
address: 125 John St, Toronto, ON M5V 2E2, Canada
description: A go-to downtown coffee stop with fast service and a well-executed menu. Daily Dose is the kind of reliable spot that fills up early for a reason.

reviews.author: Sandi Cole
reviews.text:
Smashing IT!☕️🥰☕️

I just found the cutest the most amazing little coffee shop in all of Toronto!

Situated inside a very historical building which adds to the charm and character of this marvelous coffee shop!

Dailey Dose Coffee Bar is nestled along trendy John Street in between King and Queen Street where all the cool hipsters hang out is serving up some of the most amazing beverages. Have it hot or have it cold either way your taste buds will be over the moon that you decided to stop by this magical place.

Dailey Dose Coffee is not only serving up some of the best coffees and teas and they also have a wide selection of sweets and desserts to satisfy anyone's sweet tooth.

So whether you're just starting your day, or looking for a cool Saturday place to hang out then I have to encourage you all to definitely check out Daily Dose Coffee Bar!

reviews.author: Tarik Radwan
reviews.text:
Loved the cozy ambiance. Stepped in on a rainy afternoon with a craving for coffee and sweets and boy did we hit the jackpot. I walked over to the counter to take a look at the sweets and couldn't resist the pecan square, the salted caramel brownie, and the butter tart. All three transported me to another dimension and now I'm just relishing in the memory of the taste from each one.
Great cappucino too. Can't miss.
Seriously blown away with the quality of the sweets.. they are award winning.

reviews.author: Daria Kurovskaya
reviews.text: Cozy little coffee shop. The cappuccino was excellent and they have a great variety of fresh pastries. Staff is friendly and welcoming. There is some seating, but not a lot since it’s a fairly small spot. My only wish is that they offered ceramic mugs for drinks if you’re staying in, instead of just to-go cups. Overall, a lovely place for a quick coffee and treat.

reviews.author: Laura Kelland-May
reviews.text:
Quiet friendly service and excellent scones. We were there Friday midmorning. There was a steady flow of people but not overly crowded.
There is indoor and outdoor seating. Our outdoor seating was cafe style and comfortable. Recommend (organic)breakfast tea and scone to keep you full until lunch.

reviews.author: Karen alves gaspar
reviews.text:
For those in downtown Toronto, near King Street, Rogers, CNTower, and others, Daily is a great cafe option for a snack, afternoon coffee, or a chat with friends.

The space is very cozy, with excellent service and a welcoming atmosphere.

The cafe has both indoor and outdoor seating, and purchasing and serving items requires descending a small staircase (unfortunately, the cafe is not accessible; assistance is required for guests with disabilities—they also do not have a Braille menu).

The shop has a wide selection of hot and cold drinks, as well as many food options. With our current summer season, a recommendation is the strawberry matcha, which is simply wonderful, and the peach cake, which is sensational.

The prices are fair, and the location is close to bus stops and a few kilometers from the subway station.

It's worth a visit.

name: Fahrenheit Coffee
address: 120 Lombard St, Toronto, ON M5C 3H5, Canada
description: Precise about its coffee and it shows. Fahrenheit brings a specialty edge to the downtown core with a clean, focused menu.

reviews.author: Shaghayegh Yas
reviews.text: There were three different coffee blends, and we tried two of them. Both were tasty and unique. On top of that, the welcoming and lovely vibe of the people in the café made the visit even more enjoyable.

reviews.author: Andromeda Vass
reviews.text: Soooo yummy, best coffee ever. I got The lemon currant scone and the chocolate babka, so pleasant. The staff is really nice too !

reviews.author: Simone Jehayem
reviews.text: Sameer was amazing, wonderful service. I came in for something small to eat. I’m not a coffee drinker in the slightest, but Sameer insisted on making me something i would love completely on him. And it was so good! I’m so happy i stopped by.

reviews.author: Aim B
reviews.text: Didn't order any food, but our drinks (see photos) were really well made and delicious. Liked the overall vibe of the place, can be a bit tight if it's busy. Otherwise, seems like a wonderful find for barista made coffee, teas and in-house baked goods.

reviews.author: Ziwei Yuan
reviews.text:
after living in toronto for 9 months finally found my fav coffee. There are a few good roasters in town that make really good espresso / black coffee but Fahrenheit does the best job balancing the creaminess of milk and the unique taste profiles of the beans.
I hope they can consider adding dirty to the menu. It’s rarely seen here in Canada.

Matcha almond cookie is moderately sweetened which I really love. Doesn’t taste super almond-y though.

name: Goldstruck Coffee
address: 133 Richmond St W #103, Toronto, ON M5H 3S5, Canada
description: Bright and well-designed, Goldstruck is the kind of cafe that makes you want to stay longer than planned. Great espresso to match the space.

reviews.author: Earth Explorer
reviews.text: This is one of our favorite coffee shops in Toronto. The halva latte is unique and a must try. The Wi-Fi is good in the seating is ergonomically correct. The ambiance is very nice and there is plenty of seating. They have pour over coffee and many drink choices. Perfect place for digital nomads.

reviews.author: Ashlee Ramjass
reviews.text: A beautiful location and lovely staff! Got their limited edition gold coffee and tiramisu pastry - ABSOLUTELY DIVINE! love how much seating is available as well, definitely recommend stopping by!

reviews.author: Daniel Miller
reviews.text: Good coffee, ok tea. We got a medium roast drip coffee and a London fog. The drip coffee was very good, I typically prefer dark but they were out so I tried the medium and it was still really good. The London fog was pretty milky and had a very weak tea flavour. It really needed a longer steep or stronger tea bag. There's a good cozy sitting area and everyone was friendly.

reviews.author: caylin macpherson
reviews.text: Was staying at the Sheraton in Toronto and wanted a quick coffee and breakfast Sunday morning prior to checking out and Goldstruck Coffee did not disappoint. It was a 1 min walk from the hotel, the atmosphere was very cool, calm and welcoming. The caramel latte was the best I’ve had and the breakfast sandwiches were 10/10 as well. Friendly and helpful service too!

reviews.author: Oksana Miroshnikova
reviews.text: I had a lovely time in this cozy place in rainy day !!! Sharon and Alina was so polite and friendly.  The best matcha and cookie in the Toronto ❤️❤️❤️ Will be back again and again.

name: El Pacho Coffee Roasters
address: 40 University Ave Unit 8, Toronto, ON M5J 1T1, Canada
description: King West's go-to for specialty coffee with Colombian roots. The beans are sourced with care and the results are consistently impressive.

reviews.author: Andrew Tan
reviews.text:
Such an amazing spot for coffee. To me this is a hidden spot and a hidden gem.

Hidden spot as there’s not anything with a flashy sign and the name is on a small butterfly on the storefront.

Hidden gem because the fresh baked goods and the coffee are superb. The coffee for my latte was a bright roast. So there is some citrusy note to it. Most likely from the seaberry which I gather is in their roasts since there is a sign by the register.

Hidden gem as well because of service. Nice and friendly with every person I interacted with and they also offer breakfast with an oatmeal that was available which I didn’t get but saw and was tempted to get.

Also like the fact that it opens early even on the weekends, so with a work conference I had at MTCC, it was nice to know I can grab a good cup of caffeine in the morning.
Won’t talk too much about parking as it is in downtown Toronto but it’s about a few mins walk from Royal York hotel.

Great job overall.

reviews.author: Sebastian Botero
reviews.text:
I visited El Pacho Coffee Roasters in Toronto on a Friday afternoon and had a genuinely pleasant experience. Although the café is relatively small inside, it’s well laid out with cozy seating and tables that make it easy to relax and enjoy your drink. One great bonus is the small outdoor terrace, which adds extra space and offers a nice spot to sit when the weather’s good, perfect for soaking up some sun with your coffee.

We ordered two lattes, one hot and one iced, and a cheese croissant. Both lattes were smooth, well-balanced, and served at just the right temperature. The croissant was fresh, flaky, and had a rich cheesy flavour that paired beautifully with the coffee. It’s clear they care about quality ingredients and preparation.

The service was efficient and straightforward. While the barista wasn’t overly chatty or expressive, she was polite and got everything right, which I appreciated. Sometimes direct service is exactly what you need when you're just looking to unwind.

Overall, El Pacho is a great spot if you're looking for a quiet, cozy café with solid coffee and tasty pastries. I’d definitely recommend stopping by if you’re in the area and want a break from the usual chains, this place has character.

reviews.author: Александра Боцул
reviews.text: This place is pure love. It has such a warm and cozy atmosphere that you want to come back again and again. Every visit feels like coming home — the baristas are genuinely friendly, welcoming, and full of positive energy. The coffee is always outstanding, rich and aromatic, and truly energizing. And the espresso is absolutely exceptional. A coffee shop that genuinely makes your day better.

reviews.author: Amanthi Wickramasinghe
reviews.text:
I just checked online for a coffee recommendation on a rainy and gloomy thanks giving Sunday in Toronto and El Pacho popped up on the list. It was located a lovely 10 minute walk from where I was and easy to find.

I am SO glad I tried this out. The space is small and cozy and if the weather is good there is a small space to sit outside too.

Service was very friendly and very fast.

The pistachio croissant was definitely the best I have had to date- generous amount of pistachio paste which was not overly sweet and also didn’t overwhelm the pastry. The pastry was huge, light and super flaky. It was just the perfect combination.

Cappucino was strong on coffee flavour and just on point. Also for both items costing around 14 dollars overall I felt was an absolute steal given the current prices of items and quality in Toronto.

I would highly recommend giving El Pacho a try. Great location close to Union Station as well.

reviews.author: Kota Okada
reviews.text:
Thank you for taking the time to read my review. I have visited countless cafés not only in Toronto, but also in New York and Tokyo, and I can confidently say that El Pacho is one of the best cafés in the world.

My family visited this café countless times during our nearly 6-month stay in Toronto. We were constantly comforted by their exceptional coffee, pastries, and weekend-only banana pudding and granola, and we were always energized by the warm, lovable staff. Thank you so much for everything.

Here are some of the many wonderful aspects of this café:

First, there is a deep sense of care and pride in everything they serve. The quality is outstanding. Everything is prepared daily with time, effort, and genuine love for their customers. I am not a professional chef, so I can’t explain it technically, but when you compare their food with other cafés, the difference in ingredient quality is immediately obvious. Some first-time visitors might feel the prices are a bit high, but once you experience the quality, you understand that it is absolutely worth it.
My top recommendation is the freshly baked croissant sandwich — it’s a must-try.

Second, the attentiveness of the staff is truly remarkable. Even when they are busy, they make an effort to check in with each customer and see if anyone needs help. Their smiles are a true treasure of Toronto. This care extends beyond customer service to cleanliness as well. Whenever they have a moment, they are cleaning and maintaining the space, showing how much they value providing a comfortable and welcoming environment.

Finally, the attention to interior design is exceptional. From seasonal decorations to stylish small details throughout the café, and even the orange-toned equipment, you can feel the owner’s and staff’s sense of style and dedication to keeping the space at its very best.

Cafés that leave such a strong impression are rare and precious. I am deeply grateful that El Pacho chose to open in the wonderful city of Toronto, and I sincerely hope that one day they will open a location in my hometown, Tokyo. I will be looking forward to that day.

name: Library Coffee
address: 917 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1G5, Canada
description: Low-key and bookish in the best way. Library Coffee is a solid spot for working, reading, or just disappearing for a quiet hour.

reviews.author: Christopher Fowler
reviews.text:
These baristas know what they are doing and do it exceptionally well. We had a couple flat whites and long black both made very well. I also have nut allergies that were very well taken care of (potential for cross contamination with milk pots).

When buying beans the type I purchased they made sure I was aware it was recently roasted and needed to rest for a few more days to get the floral notes I was looking to get.

You can tell the locals also love this spot as a steady stream of regulars who were known by name rotated through getting their coffee fix.

The shop is small and seating is limited but there is a park across the street weather permitting.

reviews.author: Philip Sywash
reviews.text:
The Library Specialty Coffee is easily one of the best spots in Toronto for anyone who actually cares about good coffee.

Their beans are always fresh, roasted just right, and every cup has that perfect balance of flavor and aroma. You can tell they know what they’re doing behind the bar.

The staff are friendly but serious about their craft. They’ll chat about origins or brewing styles if you’re into it, or just hand you an incredible flat white if you’re not.

If you’re picky about your coffee, The Library will ruin other coffee shops for you.

reviews.author: Derrick Yanford
reviews.text:
Just discovered this hidden gem on Queen Street - The Library Specialty Coffee is small in size but enormous in character and expertise.

Walking in, you're greeted by a minimalist space with limited seating, but what truly stands out is the exceptional service. I was fortunate to meet Alice, whose passion for coffee transcends mere customer service. When I inquired about their specialty beans (particularly an expensive option at $50/100g), she didn't rush to make a sale. Instead, Alice thoughtfully suggested I try it first, ensuring I'd appreciate the flavor profile before investing.

What impressed me most was her genuine concern that I make an informed decision - discussing grind options tailored to my brewing method and making sure my purchase would truly enhance my coffee experience. This wasn't transactional; it was a coffee education delivered with warmth and expertise.
When I mentioned wanting to leave a review to recognize her outstanding service, her humble response of "I'm just trying to do my best, I'm actually very shy" only added to the charm of the entire experience.

The Library Specialty Coffee embodies what specialty coffee culture should be: knowledgeable, unpretentious, and genuinely caring. I'm already planning to visit their other locations, but this Queen Street spot with its remarkable staff has secured a permanent place in my coffee routine.

If you value substance over flash and appreciate being treated like a valued guest rather than just another customer, this little sanctuary is absolutely worth your time.

reviews.author: K YOO
reviews.text: Always welcoming, always serving the freshest coffee in town. The aroma, the taste, the warmth — perfection every single time! Highly recommend this location! 🙌☕✨ A special thanks to the amazing barista, Yuji-san, making every cup feel extra special!

reviews.author: Matthew Ishii
reviews.text:
Been a while since I've been here, almost a year, and I still think about their cookie choux cream buns. I've probably had them 10 times total though.

It's like a completely hollow choux that's crunchy like the outer layer of a melon pan (it's kind of shaped like one too but smaller, a bit bigger than a tennis ball). And the inside get completely stuffed with whipped cream (hojicha or matcha and I think they might have had chocolate in the past?)

I'm not kidding it's like 80/20 whipped cream to choux ratio. Hattendo has nothing on this. It's so good. (Coffee here is good too)

name: Augusta Coffee Bar
address: 229 Augusta Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2L4, Canada
description: Right on Augusta in Kensington, this is the kind of neighbourhood bar regulars guard like a secret. Simple, good, and completely unpretentious.

reviews.author: Maryam A
reviews.text:
We just moved to the neighborhood and had our first breakfast here. Such a great choice!
The café is clean, cozy, and stylish. coffees were delicious, and the croissants were fresh, flaky, and perfectly crispy.
We’re so happy we found this place and will definitely be coming back!

reviews.author: ANNE SCHÖN
reviews.text: Augusta Coffee Bar on Kensington Market is a true Toronto gem , warm, welcoming, and perfect whether you’re solo or with friends. The pastries are irresistible , fresh, flaky, inventive and the coffee is excellent. Cozy atmosphere, friendly staff, and a relaxed vibe make it a must-visit spot to unwind, work, or catch up. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Chris L
reviews.text: Augusta Coffee is a great little surprise in Kensington. The coffee is genuinely flavorful — rich, balanced, and clearly made with care. The shop itself feels almost too nice for the area, but in the best way: polished, calm, and a step above what you’d expect on that stretch. It’s a spot that elevates the neighborhood, and I’d definitely go back.

reviews.author: Japheth Kang
reviews.text: It is a really nice space to work/study, the coffee is quite excellent. The single origin beans are great. I also got the sausage roll which was pretty good but I believe they get the baked goods somewhere else so shouldn't be appropriate to review them with it. Overall its a nice coffee shop and a great space.

reviews.author: Parin Kothari
reviews.text: Excellent space to sit, people watch and sip on amazing coffee with yummy baked goods. Most impressive is the space, the design, the roominess, the natural light and the luxurious bar+seating

name: The Harmony Cafe/Bar
address: 75 Nassau St, Toronto, ON M5T 1M6, Canada
description: Half cafe, half bar, all Kensington. Harmony has an easy-going energy and a patio that makes summer mornings here genuinely great.

reviews.author: shevya dhawan
reviews.text:
Harmony Cafe is such a cozy little gem! The atmosphere is warm and inviting — perfect whether you’re catching up with friends, studying, or just enjoying some quiet “me time.” The staff are genuinely kind and welcoming, which makes the whole experience even better.

Their coffee is rich and smooth, and you can tell they really care about quality. The food is fresh, well-presented, and comforting without feeling heavy. It’s one of those places where you can actually relax and not feel rushed.

Definitely a spot I’d keep coming back to. Highly recommend if you’re looking for great vibes, good coffee, and friendly service!

reviews.author: Shevya Dhawan
reviews.text: Absolutely loved the service by them, they made sure the experience was very enjoyable and safe space to express people their creativity. Tried the blue berry matcha absolutely loved it, I recommend everyone to try the strawberry and the blue berry matcha from here it’s phenomenal!!!! and best open mic opportunities for creative people!!!

reviews.author: Khalid Bowe
reviews.text: So many good drinks and food options. Absolutely love coming to this cafe, its my third place! They allow the community to host events all the time which brings a lot of entertainment whenever I go. definitely recommend

reviews.author: Tivrag
reviews.text: Harmony Cafe is such a gem. The ambiance is amazing and the drinks are just as good. It’s a small business that truly supports other small businesses, which makes it even more special. I host monthly events here and the owners are always so accommodating and easy to work with. They also have great events happening throughout the week and stay really active in the community. Definitely stop in and show them some love.

reviews.author: Emily Moore
reviews.text: I always get the hot chocolate here during my lunch break and it is very tasty, honestly one of the best in Toronto. The owner during the day when I go is also super nice very friendly, the service is great! Overall 10/10

name: San Cafe
address: 165 Augusta Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2L4, Canada
description: A Kensington cafe that wears its neighbourhood identity well. Relaxed, colourful, and a nice spot to watch the market come alive.

reviews.author: cheeselord
reviews.text: Such a cute cafe with so many cute trinkets available for sale. I absolutely adore the concept of monthly newspapers and the small pop-ups that occur every so often. The blue soda I had was just alright, but there was such little syrup that it was almost flavourless. I really love the details of the atmosphere, it gives a rustic vibe that’s perfect for studying. They also sell cute calendars! Overall, great cafe!

reviews.author: Yori Zhang
reviews.text: I went there once for the grand opening collab with Mugo bunni to get some merch, then I went back with my friend to try their dessert collaboration. The service was great, and the food was tasty. The atmosphere has so much attention to detail. It was a really great place to catch up. I spent more since I wanted to try out many of their collaboration items.

reviews.author: Christa Nguyen
reviews.text: Like stepping into a warm blanket of coffee and bakery, the staff lady was really soft spoken, matcha latte and pastries all tasted rich and not too sweet!

reviews.author: Patrick vandenEnden
reviews.text: A very cute little place! The iced matcha latte was amazing! I have no complaints and no notes.

reviews.author: Taia
reviews.text:
I came here on Monday 15 min to closing and wish I could have spent more time here! The ambience was perfect with so many personal handmade touches throughout the cafe. You can tell the owner(s) put a lot of thought into how the space was decorated and how they wanted it to feel.

I got an iced latte which had a smooth and rich flavour. The cafe also had a variety of baked goods with fun flavours like caramel fig and hojicha apricot that I’m excited to try on my next visit.

The cafe also sells hatch coffee which can be tricky to find for sale in cafes so that was a major plus. There are also other coffee related goods in addition to cute things like a San Cafe designed calendar!

They also have a photo booth which costs ~$11. So definitely come here to read, catch up with friends, and take a fun photo to commemorate another day on earth!

Last but not least, I asked the woman working there if we could pray in the back area 2 min to close and not only did she accommodate this request, but she offered a quieter place for us to pray. This kind gesture really cemented for me that this cafe goes beyond serving food, drinks & good vibes, but also making their customers feel at home 🥹

Can’t wait to come back and s/o to 小红书 for introducing me to this cafe!

name: Nonni Cafe & Bakery
address: 430 King St W Unit 4, Toronto, ON M5V 1K3, Canada
description: Queen West's favourite Italian-leaning cafe. The pastries are the real draw but the coffee holds its own. Expect a line on weekends.

reviews.author: Sebastian Botero
reviews.text:
I’ve visited this café several times, and it has become one of my go-to spots in the area. The interior is bright, cozy, and beautifully designed. The café is usually busy, but you can still find seating most of the time, which is a nice advantage compared to other places nearby. I also really appreciate that they stay open later than most coffee shops in the neighbourhood.

They offer a variety of pastries, baked goods, and light meals. I usually come for coffee and snacks. On my last visit, we ordered a macchiato, which my partner felt could have been prepared with a bit more care, but I tried their strawberry & kiwi tea and really enjoyed it,  refreshing, not too sweet, and full of flavour. We also shared an almond croissant that was warmed up perfectly, crunchy on the outside and sweet enough to enjoy without being overwhelming.

The service has consistently been friendly and helpful, and the café is generally clean and well-maintained. The washrooms are tidy too, which is always a plus. Despite the macchiato not being the best this time, everything else continues to impress me, so I’m still giving them 5 stars for the overall experience.

reviews.author: Daniel Hong
reviews.text:
The service was excellent, and all staff members were very friendly and attentive. The desserts are also highly recommendable.

One point I would like to mention is that during my visit, the coffee tasted quite mild, and I noticed coffee grounds settled at the bottom of the cup. I hope it was coffee grounds.

While it is understandable that fine grounds can occasionally appear, the particles I observed were relatively large, which may indicate that the espresso machine requires more thorough cleaning or that grounds were introduced during preparation.

This is a minor issue and something that can be improved moving forward.

reviews.author: Cam W
reviews.text: This is a solid café to stop by if you need a quick brunch or coffee. Their strawberry matcha latte and Nutella lattes are great! I’ve also had this smoked salmon, croissant, which comes with the side of salad. It’s on the pricer end for a café, but the food has been great every time. The seating area is always quite packed, and if you come during lunchtime, you’re probably not gonna find a table during the summer. They have outdoor seating, which is nice, and they have large windows. You can order and servers will deliver directly to your table, or you can pick up. I really do think they need to add more seating, but if you’re only here to quickly grab a drink, you won’t have an issue with that. The ambiance isn’t very relaxing, it’s definitely more of a hustle and bustle vibe. I wouldn’t recommend bringing a laptop here to study, most people who are here are here with friends to catch up or chat.

reviews.author: fatema moiyadi
reviews.text:
I recently visited Nonni on King Street, and it was such a pleasant experience from start to finish. The café itself has a warm and inviting vibe—bright, modern, and stylish without being over the top. It’s the kind of spot where you can easily sit back, enjoy good food and coffee, and lose track of time.

We ordered a cortado and an iced latte to start, and both drinks were excellent. The cortado had just the right balance of bold espresso and creamy milk, smooth and satisfying without being too strong. The iced latte was refreshing, not overly sweet, and perfect for a mid-day pick-me-up. You can really tell they care about the quality of their coffee here, which makes a big difference.

To go with our drinks, we tried a spinach croissant and a cheese croissant topped with za’atar. Both were standouts. The spinach croissant was flaky on the outside with a rich, flavorful filling inside—hearty enough to feel like a small meal but still light. The cheese croissant with za’atar was something truly special. The combination of buttery pastry, melted cheese, and the earthy, fragrant za’atar seasoning was unique and delicious. It’s not something you come across at every café, and it really made the experience memorable.

Service was quick and friendly, and while the café does get busy, the staff handle it well and the atmosphere stays relaxed. Prices are a bit on the higher side, but given the quality and creativity, it feels worth it. Overall, Nonni is a gem on King Street—great coffee, tasty food, and a cozy spot worth revisiting.

reviews.author: Modca
reviews.text: This cafe is popular and for obvious reasons, the service is incredible. The waitresses were super kind, efficient, and friendly. Even though this cafe is very busy and there's a lot of people, the customer service was excellent. They have sandwich options, and I ordered the tuna sandwich and a cappuccino. I enjoyed it a lot, and I'm definitely going back.

name: Hello Coffee
address: 800 Bloor St W, Toronto, ON M6G 1L9, Canada
description: A cheerful downtown cafe with good espresso and an easy, welcoming atmosphere. Hello Coffee is exactly what it sounds like.

reviews.author: unggoy ako
reviews.text: The service is okay, and the coffee is actually pretty good. But the pastries, especially the brownies, taste like they came straight out of the freezer — not fresh at all. And if you’re used to high-quality matcha, you’ll be disappointed — the matcha here really misses the mark.

reviews.author: Km Y
reviews.text: If you are looking for a regular spot to get your caffeine fix this should be on your list! I only come for their vanilla iced latte’s and it never disappoints. The cafe is very clean and lovely place to just sit and relax. The staff are friendly and very tidy!

reviews.author: Robyn D
reviews.text:
Came here on the weekend with some friends while exploring Toronto’s Christie Pitts area and, for some reason, there was only one barista working.

I have no idea how this one barista kept his energy up (the obvious answer would be with a lot of caffeine), but I was rooting for him!

He made a delicious iced oat latte, despite being under intense pressure to keep the line moving.

Well done sir 👍

reviews.author: Carly Bruce
reviews.text:
This place is so cute and the food and drinks are also great. It’s local to me and I love coming in for the atmosphere and tasty drinks. It’s frequented by a lot of locals and regulars, so it’s nice to come by and catch up with the neighbourhood. It’s close enough to the park that if it’s too busy inside I can go for a walk next door or find a park bench to enjoy my drink.

My favourite thing to get is a chai latte and one of their savoury scones.

Also one of the baristas is VERY HANDSOME.

reviews.author: Katia
reviews.text:
I was having a rough day and came in to have a cup of coffee and reset. But I left feeling worse than when I came in.

The service felt like they didn’t want me to be there. They looked bothered. Made 0 eye contact with me. I just felt like a nuisance, but I thought maybe just a bad day we all have those.

Got coffee and banana bread and they weren’t good.

I sat down and wanted to get some work done, had to ask for WiFi 3 times and again the person working there looks annoyed and just pointed at the board they had up as if I should’ve known.

There’s a ton of other places on bloor that are way better and you feel like you’re welcome. This one wasnt it.

Hello coffee was goodbye coffee from me.

name: Library Coffee
address: 281 Dundas St W, Toronto, ON M5T 1G1, Canada
description: Low-key and bookish in the best way. Library Coffee is a solid spot for working, reading, or just disappearing for a quiet hour.

reviews.author: Yunhan Liu
reviews.text: Amazing oat latte. Perfect ratio of espresso to oat milk, and the right beans for latte. If you like your lattes foamy, you will love this one. Barista was extremely friendly. Overall just a good experience

reviews.author: Abby Yan
reviews.text:
Recommend:
Pour-over with house vanilla syrup and milk – lovely soy-honey aftertaste, though a bit pricey at around $10.

Also good:
Cappuccino – balanced and smooth.

Skip:
Cold brew – too bitter for my taste.

reviews.author: David Pham
reviews.text:
[Matcha review only]
Veeeery underwhelming matcha latte with a disappointing price tag.

I ordered a standard iced matcha latte with less sugar at The Library and the total came to almost $9. I don't usually order matcha outside because of how pricey it is nowadays, but I was craving it and now I'm regretting it.

The matcha is so incredibly faint, it honestly just tasted like I was drinking milk. The staff were so incredibly nice, but the matcha latte was just not it. I'd maybe come back and try their coffees since that's what they're known for, but there is a Matcha Matcha across the street if you specifically want a matcha latte!

reviews.author: Karen alves gaspar
reviews.text:
For those in the Dundas area toward the AGO, The Library is a great place for a good coffee and a quick snack.

The cafe is small, but offers excellent service and a great selection of coffees and quick snacks, such as cookies, cakes, and more.

It can be accessed via the St. Patrick subway exit and several subway lines.

The location is wheelchair-accessible, allowing for entry by customers with disabilities. It also has a spring-operated door.

It's worth adding to your list of good places for quality coffee.

reviews.author: phumin kamnerdkaluem
reviews.text: I always get the coffee and matcha here, and they’re amazing! Both drinks taste great and give me the perfect boost to stay energized and focused throughout the whole day at work. Definitely one of my go-to spots 🫠

name: Nabulu Coffee
address: 6 St Joseph St, Toronto, ON M4Y 1J7, Canada
description: A downtown spot doing things a little differently. Nabulu has real character and takes its coffee seriously without being precious about it.

reviews.author: rishabh grover
reviews.text: Nabullu Coffee is a great spot if you appreciate good coffee and a calm vibe. The coffee was rich, smooth, and clearly made with care — you can tell they focus on quality. The place has a cozy, welcoming atmosphere that’s perfect for sitting down, relaxing, or even getting some work done. Staff were friendly and polite, which made the experience even better. Definitely a nice café to visit again if you’re in the area.

reviews.author: Malena
reviews.text: Oh Nabulu, you have my heart. The staff are always kind and genuine. The best Americano in the city. Atmosphere + opening hours are unmatched. Once you enter, you feel safe and grounded. It’s always busy, for a good reason. Love love love it here.

reviews.author: Shideh Motia
reviews.text: Nabulu Cafe is such a lovely discovery! What I love most about this place is the atmosphere it’s incredibly cozy, cute, and has such a warm, welcoming vibe. It feels special and truly stands out from other cafés. You can tell it has its own unique signature and charm. A perfect spot to relax and enjoy your time. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Mostafa Azizi
reviews.text: Such a cozy atmosphere with incredibly polite staff! Their Persian tea is absolutely authentic and delicious—it's a must-try. A perfect spot to relax and enjoy a quality drink.

reviews.author: Ruby Lee
reviews.text: Very cute interior. Came here at 12pm on a Wednesday and it was quite busy. Hard to find a place to sit if you don't come here alone. Cleared out around 1pm, which is when I left, so not sure how busy it gets after that. Matcha ($6.75) was sweet which is how I like it. I got the veggie grilled cheese sandwich. Pretty expensive for what it is ($10). But all is good and so was the items I got. Near uoft campus so it's mainly students in here. Workers were nice and efficient. I would come back

name: Lazy Barista
address: 157 Queens Quay E, Toronto, ON M5A 1B4, Canada
description: The name is ironic — the coffee here is anything but lazy. A downtown favourite with a focused menu and a loyal weekday crowd.

reviews.author: roussel nasser
reviews.text:
Such a lovely coffee & patisserie spot 🤍

The coffee is super fresh and they prepare it right in front of you, which makes the whole experience even nicer. The muffins are incredibly delicious and soft, and the cheese savoury are absolutely amazing — so flavorful and satisfying.

The place itself is very calm and cozy, perfectly located next to the harbor, which makes it ideal for relaxing and enjoying your coffee.

Thank you for the wonderful service and quality — highly recommended!

reviews.author: Lee Beech
reviews.text: Nice coffee in a modern, but minimalist setting. I just popped in for a flat white, which was great and competitively priced. Definitely worth a visit

reviews.author: Ok Sun
reviews.text:
Spacious coffee spot where you can lounge, get cozy, and enjoy long conversations until they close at 4 pm 😉
I had the honey cardamom latte, and the De Mello base coffee never disappoints.

reviews.author: Amir Moghisy
reviews.text:
Excellent all around, came in to test out the place, the lady taking our order was very nice, knowledgeable and gave us amazing recommendations. The coffee we had was their specialty cardamom latte and i LOVED it. So different and smooth, not like other cafes where they overcharge for the same basic coffee.
The pastry was also on point we had 2 croissants, pro tip: ask them to warm it up for you!
Overall excellent atmosphere and quality.
Will for sure come back, more specialty coffees to try and pastries to enjoy.
Also it’s a very nice place to sit down with a computer for some work too; it’s bright, music is not too loud and very spacious.

reviews.author: Ria Madan
reviews.text: This new cafe on Queens Quay just east of Lower Jarvis is a gift to the residents here. Their coffee is very good and always served with a smile. The muffins, cookies and other breakfast items truly brightens up the day. Would recommend to all living here as well as those who visit the east side of the beautiful waterfront community.

name: Hale Coffee Company
address: 1 Adelaide St E, Toronto, ON M5C 1J4, Canada
description: One of downtown Toronto's most dependable coffee stops. Hale is consistent, unpretentious, and the kind of place you always end up walking into.

reviews.author: Neda
reviews.text:
The atmosphere was really calm and pleasant, especially if you’re looking for a quiet place to study or focus. The space felt peaceful, with soft lighting and a relaxed vibe that makes it easy to sit for a while and get work done.

Unfortunately, I didn’t really enjoy the coffee or the pastry. The iced Americano tasted a bit too diluted, more like water than strong coffee. The dessert was also a little too doughy for my taste and felt heavier than I expected.

That said, if you’re mainly looking for a calm and comfortable space to spend time, read, or concentrate, this place is definitely a great option.

reviews.author: AS V
reviews.text:
This coffee shop checks all the boxes. First time to Toronto and I had a little bullet list of coffee shops and roasters I wanted to visit but with limited time in between the conference I just landed here and I am not disappointed.

I visited Hale coffee a number of times in the week and ordered the same thing every time. The word that kept coming to mind when I was enjoying my triple oat milk latte was "drinkable". This espresso is drinkable. The space was cozy. I could definitely see myself visiting here occasionally. But honestly, good or bad, the standout at Hale Coffee was the bakery case.

The bakery case carries the torch. Such tasty treats to enjoy! Occasionally of course.

I remember the dark skinned black woman had an insanely beautiful smile. A young man behind the counter with a dope hat on a different day was incredibly knowledgeable about the La Marzocco, answering all of my silly questions. Just a cool spot with top tier snacks/treats and drinkable espresso. Nothing bad about Hale Coffee. Definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Colin E
reviews.text: They make a great latte and americano! Came a few times in the last two weeks and have been impressed. There is a generous amount of seating and the baristas are very friendly. Highly recommend the halloumi scone.

reviews.author: Ping Z
reviews.text: I have been to Hale Coffee many times to meet up with good friends. The coffee is very fragrant, the cookies and scones are delicious, the space is spacious and bright, and the atmosphere is warm and comfortable. Special thanks to Mr. Wayne, the skilled barista, who always has a smile and great service!

reviews.author: Radus Senag
reviews.text:
I entered the cafe to inquire if they were still open because it appeared the staff were tidying up and putting things away. When i asked the man behind the counter it they were still open, he looked confused so I asked him 2 more times, to which he finally responded “WE CLOSE AT 5”, in a very blunt and rude manner. Did not proceed to ask to take my order or anything, turned his back and kept doing his own thing. Very disrespectful. I’m disappointed in this experience as I was looking forward to checking out Hale Coffee.

Many other places you could go to in the downtown core with kinder staff, for sure.

name: Graination Specialty Coffee
address: 204 Spadina Ave. Ground floor, Toronto, ON M5T 2C2, Canada
description: A downtown specialty cafe that takes sourcing and brewing seriously without making it intimidating. Graination is worth seeking out.

reviews.author: Winnie Xu
reviews.text: film + coffee, doesn’t get better than this. definitely a more casual place, seating is open and there’s going to be folks roaming around. but good for a quick coffee that is still high quality.

reviews.author: JAY KAY
reviews.text:
Great atmosphere. Staff very knowledgeable and informative. Lots of various film stocks available.
In house coffee beverages and deserts freshly made and brewed by Hatch coffee.
Highly recommend.

reviews.author: MrSorenaa
reviews.text: Interesting place to enjoy coffee. First time visiting and we liked it—especially great if you’re into photography.

reviews.author: Sia Wa
reviews.text: Amazing atmosphere and very good coffee with special notes. Highly recommend the rumudamo washed fruity coffee bean, goes really well with oat milk.

reviews.author: Claudia
reviews.text: Definitely one of the best film lab I’ve ever been. The staff is really helpful and nice and the price is very reasonable for quick turnaround and good quality scans! Here’s a picture so both the film lab & I can flex:

name: FIKA Cafe
address: 28 Kensington Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2J9, Canada
description: Named after the Swedish tradition of a proper coffee break. Fika in Kensington is exactly that -- a good excuse to slow down and stay awhile.

reviews.author: diksha wasal
reviews.text:
Fika Cafe

Fika Cafe is a cozy spot with a warm atmosphere and friendly staff. The coffee is rich and well-made, and the pastries are fresh and delicious. It’s a perfect place to relax, catch up with friends, or enjoy a quiet coffee break. A lovely little café with great Scandinavian-inspired vibes. ☕✨

reviews.author: Sandi Cole
reviews.text:
Smashing IT! ☕️😋☕️

What a lovely little gem of a café I discovered today while strolling through Kensington Market. Nestled right in the heart of this trendy, hipster‑friendly neighbourhood, FIKA Café is serving up some of the most delicious coffees and decadent lattes in the city.

Whether you’re starting your morning or looking for a mid‑day escape from the hustle and bustle of everyday life, this is the perfect spot to unwind. Step inside and slip into a magical space that offers comfort, warmth, and beautifully crafted drinks — served piping hot or refreshingly iced.

Bring a friend, bring a book, bring your laptop, or simply bring yourself.

Your mind, your soul, and your taste buds will be glad you did.

reviews.author: Kevin Butterfield
reviews.text: Stellar Scandinavian coffee shop that is a specialty/craft/third wave coffee shop. It hits the trifecta. 1. Great coffee- their house roast is good but also is a multi roaster and had several high end roasters available. Pretty rare these days. 2. Great aesthetics and comfortable. This place has out front and outdoor back patio seating as well as indoor dining. 3. Great place to hang out with friends and catch up. Definitely good for that. Best coffee in Toronto!!

reviews.author: Dilay Taşkaya
reviews.text:
The very definition of “cute”!
If you’re looking for a peaceful spot near Kensington, this place is a gem. The natural lighting is lovely, warm, and inviting. I tried their fika iced latte and the walnut-hazelnut brownie — both were delicious. They also offer savoury food options. There’s both a front and back patio, perfect for enjoying a quiet moment.

reviews.author: T
reviews.text:
I had their Strawberry Matcha drink back in the summer and it was great. There’s also a beautiful and spacious patio, which is perfect on a sunny day.

A minor downside for me was that they didn’t take Amex, but nothing to take away from the overall experience.

name: Moonbean Coffee Company
address: 30 St Andrew St, Toronto, ON M5T 1K6, Canada
description: A Kensington institution. Moonbean has been doing its thing for decades, and the coffee and community are both as strong as ever.

reviews.author: K W
reviews.text:
2025 December 4★
Great coffee, great London Fog at reasonable prices.
Seating for about 25 with some up near the front, some at the back.
Mocha latte 16oz $5.25
London Fog 16oz $5.40
Nice foamy and great coffee. London Fog was flavorful with vanilla and bergamot flavors.
Both were great. Cookies and Freshly baked pastries were also available.
Would recommend.

reviews.author: Angela A.
reviews.text: This is the OG café in Kensington Market and a must-visit if you’re in the area. You can always count on getting an excellent coffee here. While the patio seating fills up quickly, it’s perfect for people watching when you snag a spot. Inside, there’s plenty of comfortable seating, making it a great place to hang out year-round. Beyond coffee, they also serve a solid selection of smoothies and baked goods, which makes it an easy go-to for locals and visitors alike.

reviews.author: Alicia Dale
reviews.text: Moonbean is a cute, artsy coffee shop that has been in Kensington for thirty years. It lacks the pretentiousness of a lot of third wave coffee shops, giving it a unique 90s coffee house vibe. The service is quick and staff are friendly. Bonus points for the adorable back patio.

reviews.author: Brandon C
reviews.text:
Really lovely coffee shop with plenty of seating indoor and outdoor. There's a front patio, a back patio, and two rooms with seating inside.

This place will always have a special place in our heart because we actually had our first date on the patio out back! It's the perfect place to hang out and have conversations, whether it's a date or with friends. We've since come back a few times and the people working here are always friendly.

reviews.author: Catherine Ramkhalawan
reviews.text:
I was in Kensington with my cousins and we came across this cafe. It was a little busy at the time we went in (around lunch time), but we managed to find a table outside and sit for a bit. There was seating inside as well, so lots of options.

I had an iced mocha with a potato and mushroom pasty. Together, they were refreshing and delicious on a warm summer day. My cousins had an iced caramel almond latte and iced chocolate. They both enjoyed it as well.

name: Butter & Blue
address: 7 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L1, Canada
description: A Kensington spot that balances a great coffee program with baked goods worth coming back for. The croissants are the right level of flaky.

reviews.author: Vonny W.
reviews.text:
So happy to have found this adorable little coffee shop so close to work! I’m not sure how long it’s been around, but I absolutely love the concept of coffee shops tucked into these charming little houses downtown. The yellow and blue exterior fits the name perfectly—so cute and inviting. It was also easy to find, which is always a plus.

It was quite busy around 10:30 a.m., with a steady mix of dine-in and takeout customers. I noticed they also have a patio that would be especially lovely during warmer weather. The menu feels Asian-fusion inspired, with a wide variety of unique drinks and plenty of snack options (as you can see from the photo I posted).

I ordered the matcha mochi, which was perfect—not overly sweet with a wonderfully soft, chewy texture. I also tried the Iced Cookie Butter Latte, a winter special. It was good, though not quite amazing. The coffee itself could be stronger and the cookie butter flavor could also be more evident. I did appreciate the touch of cinnamon, which added a nice warmth. That said, the drink prices can add up quickly. The latte was $7, and there were additional charges to have it iced and served in a takeout cup. And so, I'd expect better flavor, given the price point.

Service was friendly, though not particularly memorable. Overall, it’s a charming spot with a fun menu and a cozy atmosphere—definitely worth a visit if you’re in the area.

reviews.author: Alexa Woods
reviews.text:
