This place is so cute, beautifully and uniquely curated. The photography, paintings, and handcrafted items really add to its charm. There’s also seating in the basement, if it's too busy on the main floor.

I went with a friend, and we were able to catch up and get some work done. I ordered a flat white, and it was delicious. The espresso is strong and exactly what I needed to give me a second boost for the day.

I haven’t tried the pastries/food yet, but it look so unique and tempting. I can imagine that in the summer, their outdoor space is absolutely gorgeous. I’ll definitely be coming back. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: Aayushi Jha
reviews.text:
One of my go to study/work cafes in the city! Butter and Blhe is definetly a hidden gem.

With aesthetic and cozy seating either in their outdoor patio, inside or basement, Butter and Blue has a place for everyone!

The drinks are also incredible I have always been a big fan of their various Matchas and especially their desserts.

I would highly reccomend checking this place out!

reviews.author: Khoi Pham
reviews.text: Finally got around to trying this spot. There is seating inside and outside. Inside, you have seats in the main floor but also downstairs next to the restrooms. The strawberry matcha latte was decent, and the egg and hashbrown bread was interesting. Friendly staff. It's a good neighborhood spot.

reviews.author: David L
reviews.text: Overall, a below-average coffee shop experience—feels a bit “budget” and DIY. The matcha mochi cake was bland (more sweet rice flour than matcha), the Hong Kong milk tea lacked robustness, and the iced americano was just standard. Also worth noting: the air vents appeared to have mold coming out of them, which was off-putting.

name: Wu Wei Coffee | 无为
address: 44 Kensington Ave Lower Unit, Toronto, ON M5T 2J9, Canada
description: A serene Kensington cafe inspired by the Taoist concept of effortless action. The coffee is deliberate, the atmosphere is genuinely calm.

reviews.author: Aolani Dunlap
reviews.text: Service? 10000/10 Matcha? 100000/10! I highly recommend because the vibes were unmatched and the matcha was lowkey life changing. The apple pie one made me want to do a backflip. I love strong flavors and the strong flavors in each of my drinks (white chocolate matcha, apple pie matcha, and hojicha cheesecake) were THERE! 🔥🔥🔥🔥 I will be back damn near every day of my trip!

reviews.author: Rica Camat
reviews.text:
I stopped by Wu Wei in Kensington Market and tried their blueberry matcha, and honestly, it was so good! The flavour was really unique — you still get that earthy matcha taste, but the blueberry adds this light sweetness and fruity twist that works perfectly together. It wasn’t too sweet either, which I really appreciated.

The place itself is small and tucked away underground, so it feels like a little hidden gem. The vibe matches the artsy energy of Kensington — cozy, chill, and very aesthetic. The staff were super nice too and took their time making the drink, which showed in the quality.

It’s definitely more of a grab-and-go spot (not much seating), but it’s worth the stop if you’re exploring the area. I’ll definitely be back to try their other flavours — this one totally won me over.

reviews.author: Kristin
reviews.text:
We heard the matcha here is superb 👀 tried the matcha coconut latte and black sesame latte. The coconut latte cream foam was absolutely phenomenal and had so much coconut flavour. Same with the black sesame foam; I don't like black sesame but I gave it a taste and it was really good and not too heavy.

The drinks were good but I'm not a matcha snob so I didn't think their matcha was any more exceptional than other places. It was still really good, and I'd definitely come back to try their other drinks, even the non-matcha ones (which were sold out today 😔), but I can't say whether this is the best in the city haha.

reviews.author: lily's travels
reviews.text: wu wei coffee is a cozy spot tucked away in kensington market. i got a hojicha cheesecake latte, and while I find the hojicha was strong, it wasn't anything special. the cheesecake portion was a bit too salty. my friends got matcha and also said their drinks weren't anything impressive, but still good. the service was quick and efficient, and the staff were kind and welcoming. the atmosphere is clean and modern, but isnt the best place to sit down and chat with friends as the seating was uncomfortable as well as sparse. a nice spot to grab and go :)

reviews.author: Kelly
reviews.text: The matcha is amazing here!! They use a very good quality matcha and use a matcha bowl and whisk to prepare it, giving it a pleasant creamy and foamy texture. It is a bit on the pricey side, but that is expected in Kensington Market. The actual cafe itself is quite small, however that is also to be expected. Overall, I would definitely recommend!

name: Aeset Coffee Co.
address: 24 Bellair St Suite 6, Toronto, ON M5R 2C7, Canada
description: A quiet Annex gem that puts real care into its coffee. Small, focused, and the kind of place that makes the neighbourhood better for being there.

reviews.author: Grace Jeong
reviews.text:
Great spot to stop by to get some work done after a workout. The café has many options for the health conscious, and the Real Vanilla Latte was great.

Coffee is getting expensive nowadays, so it’s not to the fault of the owners, but comparative to other places, it’s slightly more expensive. It could be the location as well or possibly the ingredients.

The space was clean and quiet, which was perfect for having a relaxing afternoon.

reviews.author: Connor Pinkney
reviews.text:
Located in the heart of yorkville, this small little coffee shop is the perfect spot to have a quick drink, eat, and work if you choose too.

Vibe is perfect, very clean restaraunt, staff are friendly and drinks are delicious.

Would recommend!

reviews.author: Rudi Spallacci
reviews.text:
I’m not gonna lie—I actually loved Aeset Coffee Co. when I happened to be in the area the other day and needed a caffeine fix.

I ordered a vanilla oat latte, and it was honestly pretty great. I’m very particular when it comes to coffee (some might call me a coffee snob—I wouldn’t say that, but people definitely would! 😂).

The reason I’m giving five stars isn’t just because the coffee was excellent, but also because the service was outstanding. It took about 45 seconds for them to make my large vanilla latte, which is no small feat. And it wasn’t as if they were cutting corners with an instant machine—they were clearly using a proper espresso setup and doing everything with precision.

You could tell the ingredients were high quality—both the coffee and the oat milk. That elevated the taste even more, and with how quick and friendly the service was, I was inclined to leave a tip that actually exceeded the cost of the latte itself. Let that be a lesson to anyone in the service industry: excellent product + fast, friendly service = great tips. Aeset Coffee Co. nailed it.

The shop itself has a really cool aesthetic, which is totally my vibe. It was busy when I went (a good sign!), and while the space could maybe be a little bigger, that’s a very minor nitpick. Most independent artisan coffee shops are smaller anyway, and this one is actually a good size compared to many others.

All in all: amazing service, excellent coffee, awesome vibe. I’ll definitely be stopping by again—whether it’s before or after a haircut at my stylists salon in the area, or when I’m visiting my publicist’s office a few doors down :)

reviews.author: M Chandler
reviews.text: The Strawberry Lemonade Hydration Cold tonic was perfect for a warm autumn afternoon. Enjoyed on their street side patio - the perfect Yorkville experience. My friend and I saw the patio first and had to ask where the cafe was because it’s not directly across from the cafe, and not clearly sign posted. Staff were helpful with my friend’s gluten allergy and the inside of the cafe was cute. Great spot!

reviews.author: Natalia Malysheva
reviews.text:
Beautiful little spot in the Yorkville Village.
Was impressed by variety and quality of drink suggestions. My choice was Red Velvet latte. It was delicious! Will be back to try other options

name: Rooms Coffee
address: 135 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Z6, Canada
description: Rooms Coffee on Ossington has one of the most beautiful cafe interiors in Toronto. High ceilings, considered design, and specialty coffee that holds up to the space. Rated 4.6★ with 585 reviews.

reviews.author: Isabel Bowers
reviews.text: I didn’t know but a lot of spots on Ossington Avenue open at 11 am, Rooms coffee was one of the only places open it seemed. I had a chai latte and it was so yummy. The atmosphere is cool, funky, and warm. When we arrived it was filled with quite a few people lounging and reading but we found a spot in the back. The service was welcoming and fast. Such a cool spot !

reviews.author: A.J.
reviews.text: It’s a nice small cafe with some seating. Can definitely feel crowded. Staff are friendly and feels like they’re integrated with the community with how they interact with guests. Had their Sweet Potato Volcano drink - definitely taste the sweet potato but it’s otherwise a pretty restrained flavour and after a bit of time it mostly resembles a dairy beverage with a hint of sweet potato.

reviews.author: Diana Mendez
reviews.text:
Very nice place with different seating options and a cozy atmosphere. I found it very good for focusing on work, although it can get quite crowded.
It has a decent variety of drinks and a few pastries to choose from, as well as some merchandise.

reviews.author: Sherry Chen
reviews.text:
I like the vibes though it can get really crowded and loud. There's almost always a line whenever I go. The cafe is very aesthetic and their basement is a unique zen garden with more seating!

The staff is always very friendly and helpful. I find their drinks very hit or miss 😔 I like the hojicha latte but a bit pricey for what it is.

I recently got their strawberry matcha and jasmine matcha cloud. I don't usually like strawberries but I find the drink really delicious! The jasmine matcha cloud was almost undrinkable because of how thick the matcha foam is. It didn't mix well with the milk and there were matcha chunks which made the taste very bitter.

reviews.author: Quincy Leung
reviews.text: Can’t speak on the service or atmosphere since I only got a matcha latte to go, but the drink really shines. The matcha isn’t masked by sugar or milk and you can actually taste its quality. I’d definitely come back to try other things or even stay for a coffee. There’s also a full patio in the back.

name: Rooms | 17 Baldwin
address: 17 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L1, Canada
description: The Baldwin St location of Rooms brings the same considered aesthetic to Kensington. A thoughtful space for a slow, deliberate coffee.

reviews.author: Shirley Du
reviews.text:
One of the most beautiful coffee shops I've ever seen! They have Nintendo figurines, a spacious seating area, and expert baristas. I ordered a hojicha latte and my partner ordered a regular latte. Both lattes were deliciously smooth and well made.

The best part of this coffee shop is again the vibes and atmosphere. It has a long narrow and deep space, but it goes really deep. As in this cafe looks bigger than than you think once you move further down the place.

I really enjoyed the overall feeling I got from being here and again the vibes here are amazing. Great place to study or hangout with friends!

reviews.author: Carson Lam
reviews.text:
Quite rare to find a coffee shop like this that opens early! Been here several times and the drinks and service have been top notch.

It's generally packed inside so YMMV if you can secure a spot. Come early enough, and there will be plenty around.

The music being played by these gigantic vintage speakers further bring out the uniqueness of this cafe too. If you prefer a quieter environment, the seats in the far back are your best bet.

reviews.author: Kyle T
reviews.text:
Rooms is one of the BEST cafes in Toronto for interior and vibes. It’s comfortable, modern, and unique it’s hard not to want to sit for a bit and get some work done, this is the best part of rooms.

I’ve been twice and gotten an iced matcha latte and they were polar opposites in flavour. The first time I went it was very grassy which was to my liking. Most recently, it was quite bad, watery tasting as the matcha was flavourless. It is a shame the drink has been inconsistent in my visits.

Staff are chill, maybe too chill? Sometimes it is a bit difficult to get their attention, one time they didn’t even talk to me just waited for me to order. I don’t mind the relaxed service and I like it at times but I’d be lying  saying it’s warm and welcoming.

3/5 flat, atmosphere carries the rating

reviews.author: Ev T.
reviews.text: Beautiful and cozy cafe with variety of sitting and relaxing atmosphere. Love that you can enjoy coffee for several hours, there’s no rush and no haste.

reviews.author: Onuma Sattayalertyanyong
reviews.text: I ordered strawberry matcha which was really good. Not too sweet. Vibe inside is also great.

name: Mercury Espresso
address: 465 King St E Unit #5, Toronto, ON M5A 1L6, Canada
description: A downtown Toronto staple that has been making consistently great espresso since before specialty coffee was trendy. Mercury has staying power.

reviews.author: Maria Leonila Barte
reviews.text: Had the best matcha latte and can’t wait to come again! The place is tiny but cozy. Place was clean and you can play a game of chess! They have wifi and allow studying but they only have a few tables and places to sit. Still a hidden gem for those looking to try a new cafe! Wasn’t that busy when me and my boyfriend went there on a Saturday afternoon so finding a table wasn’t hard.

reviews.author: Daniel Bam
reviews.text: Visiting from Connecticut and this cappuccino seriously impressed me. Excellent coffee, and Vini the barista was incredibly friendly and knowledgeable. Would absolutely come back.

reviews.author: Hannah St
reviews.text:
Local gem. Standard coffee shop drink menu and baked goods. There are large and individual tables with local artists works displayed along walls.

Ceiling fans were going a bit strong during my visit.

reviews.author: Ariette Hung
reviews.text: Mercury Espresso is a charming little café with a great summer vibe! I had an iced cold brew that was excellent—smooth, refreshing, and perfect for the heat. That said, I did notice a fair number of small fruit flies hovering around the syrup and milk station, which was a bit off-putting. It might be worth the team looking into a solution like fly traps or one of those mini fan devices to help keep the area cleaner and more inviting. It’s quite a cute cafe overall, but cleanliness matters. Still, the coffee quality and overall vibe make it worth a visit. You could always get takeout!

reviews.author: Hillary Connolly
reviews.text: So excited this Leslieville gem has opened a new location across from Sackville Playground on King. Same awesome coffee and friendly staff, calm and quiet patio with a view of the park. Great spot to sip, work, or bring the kids.

name: Varda
address: 350 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 3X2, Canada
description: A King West cafe with a strong design sensibility and a coffee program that keeps pace. Come for the space, return for the flat white.

reviews.author: Parul Gupta
reviews.text:
Varda Café has become my go-to spot whenever I need a peaceful corner in the hustle of downtown. Whether to work from here, enjoy a book, or just have an unhurried conversation, this place is one that I count on to, every single time.

It’s calm, quiet, and thoughtfully designed for people who appreciate a slower pace, culture and art.

Also, coffee here is excellent and freshly brewed everyday. And the staff are friendly and accommodating without being intrusive, which only adds to the relaxed vibe.

If you’re looking for a place to work-from-café, write in your journal, or just catch up on life with someone special, I highly recommend Varda Café. Toronto needs more spots like this!

reviews.author: Skye R-C
reviews.text:
Loved my time at Varda! It has the relaxed, chill vibe of a coffee shop, where you order at the front, combined with the upscale interior of a cocktail lounge. The space is absolutely gorgeous, with stunning views and a great ambiance!

The food is also reasonably priced! I had the ham and cheese croissant, which was good, though the server mentioned they couldn’t heat it. However, the muffaletta was incredible—so flavorful, delicious, and surprisingly filling. The chips paired perfectly with it!

Would definitely go back and highly recommend Varda for anyone looking for a beautiful, relaxed atmosphere with great food and drinks at a reasonable price!

reviews.author: A Kens
reviews.text: Lovely cafe in the Tiff Lightbox. Great ambiance. Good espresso. Good for a chill lounging on a slow weekend morning.

reviews.author: Irbaaz Syed
reviews.text: The atmosphere is what you can expect from the photos. I tried the Ginger N Fred mocktail and it was a 10/10. The price of the items on the menu are quite pricey for what it is but the vibe in varda is great! I would definitely keep coming back here.

reviews.author: Elvin Ibragimov
reviews.text: This lounge bar is the perfect place to unwind and enjoy the night. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with stylish interiors, comfortable seating, and lighting that sets the mood without being overwhelming. CN tower through the windows looks magical.

name: Versus Coffee
address: 70 Adelaide St E, Toronto, ON M5C 2R6, Canada
description: A precise downtown specialty cafe with a menu built around single origins and careful extraction. Versus is for the coffee-curious.

reviews.author: Wes Inaba
reviews.text:
Versus Coffee is an awesome java stop in downtown Toronto. A modern building facade outside with a humble cozy interior inside. They also have a patio for summer weather.

Great coffee offerings and a few very delicious foods.

A highly rated coffee shop for good reason!

5 x ☕☕☕☕☕

reviews.author: Chris Graham
reviews.text: Visited on a Wednesday lunch time to have a look.  The cafe seems quiet enough to work remotely with many patrons working away off laptops.  Greeted by friendly staff.  A good selection of drinks and interesting to observe you can customize your breakfast sandwich.  I went for a pan au chocolat and it was very nice.  Not much change from $5 so I felt it was a bit expensive, however, it was worth popping in and finding a place you can work remotely from.

reviews.author: Sachin Kumar Sherikar
reviews.text:
Not your regular cup of coffee — Versus Coffee truly serves art in a cup! ☕ The coffee is beautifully crafted and tastes just as amazing as it looks. The café has a cozy, welcoming vibe that makes it perfect for a quick stop or a slow coffee break.

Highly recommend for anyone looking for a unique coffee experience in Toronto.

reviews.author: Melissa
reviews.text: This place is amazing… firstly the colorful lattes are amazing in design and taste. The outdoor patio is so cute! The staff is friendly and efficient. A great place for a morning coffee and lite bite..

reviews.author: Shay Matters
reviews.text:
Versus Coffee is a total gem in Toronto. The beans here are absolutely on point, smooth, rich, and bursting with flavor. You can really taste the quality in every cup. Whether you’re grabbing a quick espresso or settling in with a pour-over, the coffee never misses.

The vibe is chill, the baristas know their craft, and it’s the kind of place that makes you want to come back for your daily fix. If you’re serious about good coffee, Versus delivers.

name: HotBlack Coffee
address: 245 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1Z4, Canada
description: One of the best-known specialty cafes in Toronto and the reputation is earned. HotBlack is a downtown coffee institution worth visiting.

reviews.author: Natalie Markiewicz
reviews.text:
We used to go to hot black coffee on our weekends when they were in Bloor West village. But since they've left, we've definitely missed them. The staff and owner are awesome and friendly.

The coffee is consistently amazing. I recently tried their egg and cheese on sourdough sandwich and it was great. The awesome thing is that all the ingredients are sourced locally (from Ontario farmers) and the bread is made in house!

10/10 recommend this cafe.

reviews.author: Lucas Madore
reviews.text: I absolutely LOVE this place! I recently started a new job in the area and I discovered Hot Black Coffee. You’re greeted immediately with a good morning & hello, the food is delicious (I recommend the beet chocolate loaf and egg toast) and the vibe is warm, cozy and clean! Thank you for an amazing experience every time!! ☕️

reviews.author: Glenn
reviews.text: I love this place. Located on Queen At W. The coffee selections are fantastic with very knowledgeable. It's a laid back environment with a communal table and single seats at the front window. The raspberry wheel cookie is a must have. Also a selection of quick and satisfying baked goods and sandwiches.

reviews.author: Mr Tysr
reviews.text:
HOTBLACK COFFEE is an absolute gem in Toronto! The moment you walk in, you’re greeted with a warm welcome and the friendliest host who makes you feel right at home. The atmosphere is cozy and inviting, perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends.

Their coffee is top-notch — rich, flavorful, and clearly made with care. I love that they use local products, which makes everything taste fresh and authentic. On top of the amazing beverages, they also offer delicious meals and homemade biscuits and cakes that pairs perfectly with your drink.

The service is genuinely kind and thoughtful — you can tell they really care about their customers. A sweet and gentle vibe all around. Highly recommend this place to anyone looking for great coffee, tasty food, and a welcoming experience in the city!

reviews.author: Vibë Chêck
reviews.text: Good coffee but bad services for the price. Sandwiches are served cold (was told it is supposed to be eaten like that; didn't like it). Not great for long sitting as the seats doesn't have a back. Coffee quantity was also very less.

name: Mast Coffee
address: 68 Shuter St, Toronto, ON M5B 1Y4, Canada
description: A calm, pared-back downtown cafe with a small menu done really well. Mast is the kind of place you go when you want good coffee without fuss.

reviews.author: Khaalis De Las
reviews.text: Dropped into this cafe today for a quick snack and ended up staying to dine in. Paint this picture: It’s a cold rainy day downtown and you just got splashed from a passing car. Oh wait, Mast Coffee is here. I’ll just stop in for carrot cake and hot chocolate. Wow I’m warm now and this tastes great. I like it here!

reviews.author: Sakina Dahodwala
reviews.text: A beautiful little cafe in downtown. The cafe has a pretty good vibe with different kind of seating. The customer service is excellent and the staff is very friendly. I tried their cappuccino and cheddar and chive scone which were delicious. I would say the best scone I ever had. The day I went their dishwasher was out of service so we didn’t get the ceramic which I was upset about but the coffee made everything worth. They are also cashless which most of the places are these days. Highly recommended cafe for enjoying “me time” or coffee date.

reviews.author: Harsukhman
reviews.text: Had a great cappuccino and carrot cake slice. They have a good selection of baked goods and alternative milks including macadamia milk. They have a few tables and some bar seating available.

reviews.author: Amy
reviews.text: This cute lil local coffee shop is perfect for breakfast, they have amazing quality coffee beans, a variety of baked goods, caffeine beverages both hot and cold. The service was very fast and they provide a help yourself to cream and sugar. It’s also a beautiful bright atmosphere, perfect for a coffee chat. Thank you!

reviews.author: Sarah Rugheimer
reviews.text: YOU CAN HAVE MACADAMIA NUT MILK for your espresso drink?!??? Omg it is delicious, I had no idea. The quality is top notch of their beans. Plus the staff is super friendly. They clearly know their regulars but also are just get most friendly staff I’ve seen in a Toronto cafe.

name: Vereda Central Coffee Roasters
address: 14 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M5H 3R3, Canada
description: Colombian-owned and roaster-led, Vereda Central brings warmth and coffee expertise downtown. The single origins are the move here.

reviews.author: been benn
reviews.text:
AMAZING
Whenever I go, I always have a turmeric drink with milk; I love it. I also tried the cappuccino, and it's very good. I also had their banana loaf, an excellent choice for winter.

reviews.author: Connor Pinkney
reviews.text:
Coffee was good, restaraunt was clean and service was quick!

I got the iced Americano and loved that I had the ability to pick between the light, medium and dark roast. Oftentimes I've only seen one option.

As I often walk this path, im looking forward to returning!

reviews.author: Fabiola Castañeda Gamboa
reviews.text:
I'd call this place a hidden gem tucked inside Eaton Centre. While it's technically in the mall, it feels refreshingly removed from the chaos, almost like a little sanctuary for coffee lovers.
The coffee here is genuinely excellent, and what really impressed me was being able to choose my preferred roast level. That kind of attention to detail makes all the difference! If you're looking for quality coffee and a peaceful moment to actually enjoy it, this is the spot.

reviews.author: Vivian Wright
reviews.text:
Small coffee shop located at the far end of the top floor of The Eaton Centre, by the former HBC bridge. Limited seating, self serve cream+sweetener station.

Staff was nice, however the Cherry Blossom Latte did not taste good. Very acidic, bitter with an odd aftertaste that made me feel a little sick afterward. Might stop by and try another drink again sometime as this store was highly recommended by my colleagues but definitely recommend staying away from the Cherry Latte.

reviews.author: Ajay Gohil
reviews.text:
A lot of people hand out 5 star reviews without much consideration, however, I thought this place genuinely deserves it.

I noticed this new café had recently opened in place of the former Second Cup, inside the Eaton Centre and thought I’d check it out.

I usually go for a flat white as a general indication of how good a place is. So, how was it? Silky smooth, rich in flavour and just the right acidity profile. I think I may have found my new favourite!

Added bonus was that I visited on a weeknight, which, apparently, is their happy hour (not advertised), so the price was discounted - this is a great concept that can easily convert a lot of customers of other brands to come to this one… or like Lady Gaga, switch baristas 😆

As many reviewers have pointed out, the only thing missing is more seating.

Hopefully, they open more locations. Worth a try!

name: NEO COFFEE BAR
address: 161 Frederick St Unit 100, Toronto, ON M5A 1J9, Canada
description: A downtown Toronto staple with multiple locations and consistently high standards. NEO is what good specialty coffee at scale looks like.

reviews.author: Shahida H
reviews.text: Love the food and ambience, most people go there to work quietly or enjoy some coffee and snacks. Unfortunately there was a loud group of office workers that came in around 11am and completely ruined the vibe with loud chatting and erupting laughter. There should be some code for cafe etiquette. Its not the cafe's fault, but someone should tell disruptive customers to quiet down. I love the food and coffee at Neocoffee bar, always has amazing new seasonal specials i love to try. I had a cheese croissant and cloud 9 latte.

reviews.author: S E
reviews.text: my cappuccino was made deliciously. the staff are kind and very hard working. the space is kept very clean and there’s lots of room to sit. i ordered a matcha latte to go (which was delicious) and the barista was very accommodating when i asked for it to be extra hot ☕️🎨 hehe i will definitely be back !

reviews.author: Florence Svoboda
reviews.text: Matcha was delicious, vibes were immaculate, perfect to study or work (certain hours are computer-free, music was nice piano jazz), and employees were lovely.

reviews.author: Tiffany Waardenburg
reviews.text: Delicious Brazilian coffee! Very similar to Colombian coffee in tasting notes. I had the honey oat latte. It was perfect. Third-wave wonderful. The atmosphere was modern and chic. I didn’t eat but the pastries were top-notch.

reviews.author: Andrea Manning
reviews.text: The first thing I look at when I enter a food establishment is cleanliness. If I walk in and feel like we value that, my guard comes down. Second is are u constantly busy. The 3rd is customer service. These 3 characters are apparent at the Neo Coffee Bar. Ty for a great experience 👌🏽

name: Carbonic Coffee (Baldwin)
address: 37 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L1, Canada
description: A technically precise cafe on Baldwin using carbonic maceration for a fruity, wine-like cup. Worth a visit if you're curious about process.

reviews.author: Namita Chaudhary
reviews.text:
Very nice coffee spot in Baldwin Village. We had an Americano, a long black and a cappuccino. All very fantastic. The barista was super friendly. Theh don6use single plastic forks and spoon - Fantastic 😍. It was a take out for us but the vibe of the place is very chill.
Would definitely recommend visiting.

reviews.author: Bea D.
reviews.text: Cool coffee shop in the lovely Baldwin Village. Iced coffee was nice, but sadly no straws (not even paper ones). The pig in blanket was quite different to a British one, but tasty, with beef sausage in sweet pastry. The deck out front was a great place to sit and chill.

reviews.author: sijin ouyang
reviews.text: Worst cafe in winter, it it freezing in the back. They got 2 walls exposed to winter weather, 0 heat source in the back (only heat source in the front is a hung up ac, which is the worst heat source in winter u could possibly get, cause it couldn't heat up the ground anyway). I got 200cad solution for ya,get some plug in HEATERS!!!!!!!!!

reviews.author: Coffee Bunny
reviews.text:
CARBONIC
37 Baldwin St, Toronto, ON M5T 1L1 Rating: 4/5
Drinks Ordered: Flat White - whole milk  ($5.85CAD)
⸻
First Impressions
Carbonic is a stylish cafe filled with natural light. The arched walls/wall seating, hints of coral/deep blue and cumbia playing in the background made me feel like I was temporarily transported to a Hispanic country. The atmosphere was so immersive that I completely lost track of time.
⸻
Coffee Quality
I ordered the flat white made with whole milk.
* Espresso: Floral aroma, bright acidity, honey-like sweetness with a short, crisp finish
* Milk: Medium, round and silky
* Beans: Carbonic house blend - can be purchased, it is the grey logoless bag
*
This drink met my expectations. If you love bright and naturally sweet coffees, then this is for you.
⸻
Ambience & Amenities
* Wi-Fi: Available
* Outlets: Yes
* Seating: Coffee tables with couches and indoor and outdoor conversation sets
* Crowd: Inviting

⸻
Overall
Carbonic is a great choice for anyone looking to sit back, relax and enjoy a good cup of coffee.

reviews.author: zahra vy
reviews.text:
This coffee shop takes brewing quality seriously. Quite cozy and spacious than it looks from the outside. The staff is super friendly.

The entrance area up until the barista area looked really great but I wish the aesthetic was spread throughout the back as well.
_________________
☕️ Hojicha latté[oat milk, unsweetened]: simply underrated.
> It was a smooth blend when I had it. Tasted like a hefty amount of powder was used because it had a prominent nutty and slight smokiness. I usually drink my drinks little by little especially when I am working on something. This latté got me chugging the whole thing down ☕️٩(ˊᗜˋ*)و❕️

🍵iced matcha latté [oat milk, sweetened]: it was...alright. I had high expectations because of my previous visit with the Hojicha.
> tasted too much of sugar; overpowered the matcha
> Maybe the ice melted too quickly that it tasted watered down
> 🫤 I'll try the hot one next time

☕️iced chai latté [regular milk]: it was sweet 👀 but at least tasted like chai👍🏼 but I wouldn't order it again. Maybe it will taste better as a hot drink?

☕️cortado: ✨️✨️✨️my fave go to. Could never go wrong.

The pastries I got during my visits were good too. Almond croissant was actually huge, its worth it.
__________________
📶wifi: VERY choppy. Literally had to use my data just to finish my work

🪑 seats: if you are planning to stay and do work, definitely choose the high chairs  or the sets at the front.
> most of the seats are stools with no proper tables.
> It is not ergonomically safe for a prolonged time to be stopped over or even sit on an uncomfortable seat with the laptop on ur lap.

🔌: it is a mission to find outlets. The back area has an extension cord. High chairs also have one underneath.

🚻 washroom: in the basement. Gets very cold 🥶. Well maintained.

🎵: great music taste 👌🏼. Its nice to rid of the headphones and just vibe to the music with café noise while studying.

name: Shy Coffee Co.
address: 766 King St W #3, Toronto, ON M5V 1N6, Canada
description: Quiet in name, not in quality. Shy Coffee on Queen West makes a strong case for itself with a focused menu and well-sourced beans.

reviews.author: Zeynab A
reviews.text: Went here yesterday and it was a cute little coffee shop. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and my latte came to around $8. Menu is simple selection of baked goods. I sat outside on their patio with my coloring book, and it was a nice, relaxing spot to spend some time. A cozy place I’d go back to.

reviews.author: Meryl V
reviews.text: Super cute place. I enjoyed sipping on my latte and eating a muffin when I was there. This place has been on my to go list and its nice I finally went.

reviews.author: Hank Ban
reviews.text:
Although I liked the ambience of the place and presentation of the coffee, it just didn't live up to appearances. Place was decently clean and presentable. Looks like a nice hangout place.

Their teas were very refreshing. Service was prompt and efficient.

reviews.author: Jacquelyn Thrall
reviews.text: This place advertises as being “dog friendly” online (you can see the dogs posted inside their store on their social media) but they kicked my friend and I out after we ordered our food and drinks. We didn’t have this issue in any other coffee shop in Toronto. No behavioral issues from our dogs either… so they wanted our business and then decided to kick us out. Food and drinks were mid at best. Try somewhere else in Toronto for coffee because this place isn’t it.

reviews.author: Farzan Farnaghi
reviews.text: This coffee shop is beautifully designed and has Greta ambience. The carrot & oat muffin is good especially warmed up. Drip coffee was good as well. The barista was super friendly!

name: Cafe23
address: 728 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1E8, Canada
description: A Queen West neighbourhood cafe that has built its following the old-fashioned way -- by being consistently good and genuinely welcoming.

reviews.author: Ayushi Garg
reviews.text:
Loved this cute hidden café in Bathurst! As soon as you walk in, you instantly fall in love with the architecture and all the beautiful plants around. The greenery gives it such a cozy and calming vibe.
The seating inside is a bit congested, and it does get pretty busy — but if you manage to grab a good spot, you’ll definitely enjoy the atmosphere. We initially went just for coffee and to chat, but couldn’t resist ordering their butter croissants as well.
We ordered a flat white and a cappuccino, and both were really good. The croissants were fresh and buttery — the perfect pairing with coffee. The staff were friendly and welcoming, always smiling, which made the experience even better.
I’m definitely coming back in the summer to try their patio. I’ll always have a soft spot for places decorated with plants — they just make everything feel more peaceful and inviting 🌿☕

reviews.author: Natalie B.
reviews.text: One of my all time favourite cafes in Toronto, the pastries and sandwiches are amazing, the coffee too, and the decoration/vibe of the place is just so perfect. Their patio is the cutest, doesn’t feel like you’re in the city!

reviews.author: Konstantinos Katsaris
reviews.text: This hidden gem is the perfect spot to get away from the hustle and bustle of the city. The patios in the rear lower and upper level garden feelings along with the freshly done pastries and coffee will hit the spot for you. If you’re from the city or just visiting this spot is highly recommended. Enjoy ❤️ and thank me later…

reviews.author: Enid 𓈒ׂ𓇼 ࣪ 𓈒ׂ
reviews.text: I visited on a busy day, and the place was so packed. Making it difficult to find a spot until we got outside in the backyard. regardless there’s a couple of tables 😭 Despite all that, the interior design was striking!! What stood out the most was the variety of curated drinks. (The hojicha was my favorite) The pastries are SUPER sweet btw 😖

reviews.author: Martine Brouillet
reviews.text:
I absolutely love this little oasis on Queen st west of Bathurst. We walked there because our dog was allowed on their back terrace, which is absolutely lovely.
Great place to find a bit of calm, fabulous coffee and tea menu and to top it all off, they serve St-Viateur Bagel! Being from Montreal that was a big win!
Loved it!

name: Goldstruck Coffee
address: 130 Cumberland St, Toronto, ON M5R 1A6, Canada
description: Bright and well-designed, Goldstruck is the kind of cafe that makes you want to stay longer than planned. Great espresso to match the space.

reviews.author: Saeed Moayyedi
reviews.text:
I visited Goldstruck Coffee at Cumberland on a cold day, and the warm atmosphere was perfect for enjoying a cup of coffee. The cappuccino I ordered had good quality, although it didn’t come with any foam art.
The only downside is that, compared to other cafés at the same level, the prices are a bit high. Overall, it was a pleasant experience and a cozy spot to relax.

reviews.author: Soban A
reviews.text:
This cafe cultivates a distinctly quiet vibe, making it an appealing spot for those looking to focus, read, or have an intimate conversation. The low volume is maintained with music playing quietly in the background. However, the seating arrangement strongly dictates its use: with only about ten tables, each strictly limited to two seats, it is decidedly not suitable for large groups and is clearly oriented toward individuals or pairs.

A practical note on noise is that the overall quiet atmosphere is broken up near the service counter. Guests are advised not to sit next to the cashier as the constant operation of the coffee machines is loud in that specific area.

In terms of comfort, a significant drawback was the interior temperature. The reviewer noted that the inside of the cafe was too hot, to the point of causing them to sweat, suggesting a ventilation or air conditioning issue that detracts from the experience.

Service is friendly, as the cashier was noted to be pleasant. However, it is a minimal-service establishment; they do not bring orders out to the tables, requiring customers to pick up their food and drinks at the counter. The offerings are limited, with a small selection of baked goods available to try. Finally, the coffee itself was described as tasting "ok," but "not extraordinary," indicating it is perfectly functional but not a standout feature or specialty draw for true coffee aficionados.

reviews.author: Baruch Pletner
reviews.text: This place is amazing. Pretty much the second time I was here, they remembered my order (Americano black and Remy Martin) and treated me like family. They roast their own coffee and it's very good if you like coffee that tastes like coffee should. Just a great atmosphere, great coffee, great value, and great people. Love it.

reviews.author: Akashdeep Singh
reviews.text: Goldstruck Coffee is a great spot for high-quality coffee and a relaxed atmosphere. The coffee is rich, smooth, and clearly made with care—you can taste the quality in every sip. The staff is friendly and welcoming, and the service is quick without feeling rushed. The space itself is cozy and well-designed, making it a perfect place to sit, work, or catch up with friends. Definitely a go-to café for anyone who appreciates good coffee and good vibes.

reviews.author: Himel Don Khandker
reviews.text: The ginger molasses cookie was delicious and addictive and the milk foam was very good but the espresso in the drinks themselves tasted overly bitter, and not in a “this bitter is part of the flavour profile” kind of way, more like “this shot was haphazardly extracted”. Service was good, baristas were lovely and the vibes inside were nice.

name: Jacked Up Coffee
address: 55 Avenue Rd, Toronto, ON L1N 4H4, Canada
description: An Annex staple with serious coffee credentials and a laid-back atmosphere that makes it hard to leave. Regulars are very regular here.

reviews.author: Rupert A.
reviews.text:
This location is in the Hazelton Mall near the entrance from tge Hazelton Lane side.
The location is a small "kiosk" for takeout only.
Since, at present, there are no coffee shops at the lower level which is their food court with ample comfortable seating.
The coffee is good and the pastries are uninspiring.

reviews.author: SHAY
reviews.text: Very good flat white coffee made with oat milk, went well with the chocolate chip cookie. Lots of seating down the escalator in the food court to enjoy.

reviews.author: Cory Taylor
reviews.text: I just found out about this great little coffee shop and it's definitely one of my new favorite places in the area. The little blue van was quite unexpected. Staff was friendly and they make great espresso! They sell Cut Coffee which is a great local roaster so I had to buy a bag of beans and surprisingly got a free drink of choice. I'm glad it's now a permanent location so I can enjoy it year round.

reviews.author: Megan Weiss
reviews.text: I’ve heard so much of this coffee truck and would say that I was not at all disappointed! Not only is it a unique coffee shop, the barista was really nice and showed a passion in the coffee. I ordered an iced latte and it was delicious! Would definitely go again and try their warm beverages!

reviews.author: Victoria L.T
reviews.text: Great coffee, great people, and the kind of small business experience that leaves a lasting impression. ☕️

name: Arvo Coffee
address: 17 Gristmill Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: Australian-influenced and Distillery District-located. Arvo makes excellent flat whites and has one of the better patios in the area.

reviews.author: Linus V
reviews.text:
This coffee spot has a very cozy and relaxing atmosphere, almost like a hidden little retreat.
There were a few people working on their laptops, and it felt like a comfortable place to spend time.
The coffee is on the more acidic side, so it may come down to personal preference.
I ordered the smoked salmon toast.
It was beautifully presented with plenty of vegetables, but the toppings and sauce were quite tangy, making it hard to taste the salmon itself.

reviews.author: Mike Lovas
reviews.text: Nice Aussie inspire coffee spot with lots of options including flat whites. Aussies know their espresso drinks. Nice vibes, and seating inside and out. Friendly staff. Good iced americano, I’ll be back to try the treats, the Aussie Magic drink, and the beans (they seem to have a cool selection, at least based on the packaging!).

reviews.author: Jason Lee
reviews.text: Really cool little coffee shop in the Distillery District. I came to check it out because of the reviews and it did not disappoint. The cappuccino was delicious and I had a blueberry stone as well which was pretty good!. Would definitely come back for the cappuccino. The staff were friendly as well which is always a plus.

reviews.author: Krista P
reviews.text: Nice coffee spot, cozy to sit inside and wifi available. Iced matcha latte was too sweet though.

reviews.author: Catharine Alexander
reviews.text: Delicious avocado toast and pumpkin spice latte. Nice and strong espresso flavour and not overly sweet. Totally hit the spot. And I will try for the rest of my life to make an avocado toast as good as Arvo's!

name: Jimmy's Coffee
address: 15 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Y8, Canada
description: A true Ossington original. Jimmy's has a loyal following for good reason -- the coffee is excellent and the place feels genuinely alive.

reviews.author: Justin Veiga
reviews.text: There are soooo many coffee shops in this area, so why go to Jimmy's?  Yes, they have a cozy back area for those who need a quiet place to work.  But, everyone really goes because of the staff - one of kindest and most genuine teams on the Oz strip.

reviews.author: Solongo Mandakh
reviews.text: I got the matcha, but it mostly tasted like milk. The barista didn’t seem very attentive even though it wasn’t busy, and they didn’t call us when our coffee was ready — if we had to wait, they should’ve at least told us. Wouldn’t really recommend this place, though the muffin was actually pretty good.

reviews.author: DL M (Sarge)
reviews.text: I've been to several outlets around the city and always had a good experience. Staff have been excellent, friendly and efficient. I enjoy their brewed coffee and espresso based drinks. Very consistent high quality over time and between locations. They've done a great job of making a slightly rustic decor and welcoming atmosphere in the 3 or 4 locations I've visited. Reasonable prices. I really recommend Jimmy's.

reviews.author: T McCrae
reviews.text:
Great atmosphere, Americano was good, cookie was good...
I understand younger staff sometimes have to be reminded, for the 30 seconds we are communicating, we have your attention, we don't feel like we are interrupting your fellow staff conversation.
Your back "patio" is terrible. Even if its just visual, time of year is still no excuse, small amount of time for it to look orderly at the least. Is it not an opportunity for an extension of the atmosphere? Its a focal point even just glancing back, aside from sitting beside it.

reviews.author: Joy Birch
reviews.text: LOVE love love this place! The vibe: amazing. The coffee selections unique and yet traditional. The baked goods: delicious and very fresh! Landed here with a friend from the neighbourhood and I was the total-tourist, drinking it all in (see what I did there?: "drinking" ... coffee ... ). I love the decor and just wish they'd had a branded-travel-coffee-cup: would have totally bought that as a memento of the time. Good Job Jimmy

name: Coffee Oysters Champagne
address: 214 King St W Basement Level 1, Toronto, ON M5H 3S6, Canada
description: King West's most unexpected combo. The name delivers on its promise and so does the coffee. A neighbourhood spot with a lot of personality.

reviews.author: Alexander Theodorou
reviews.text:
Coffee Oysters Champagne is a fantastic spot that nails atmosphere and drinks. The space feels like a chic, hidden hotel bar with marble tables, plush seating, and lots of character.

The cocktails and champagne were excellent – beautifully made, well balanced, and clearly crafted with care. Staff were friendly, attentive, and great at making recommendations.  Happy hour is a must @ 5$ each for espresso Martinis, classic Martini and even a seasonal drink.

The speakeasy behind the champagne wall was the highlight: a fun, eclectic hideaway that feels special without being gimmicky. An all‑around great night out and an easy place to recommend.

reviews.author: Joseph Simard
reviews.text:
The service was genuinely warm and attentive from start to finish — you feel welcomed the moment you walk in.

The champagne selection is outstanding. Whether you’re celebrating or just treating yourself, there’s an impressive range to choose from, and the staff are happy to guide you to the perfect bottle.

And the oysters? Absolutely fantastic — fresh, beautifully presented, and full of flavour. Pairing them with a great glass of champagne makes for the perfect experience.

reviews.author: Sarena Z
reviews.text: Really cool concept, the woman that led us into the bar was very expressive in her story telling. Once we were inside, there was a magician going around to different tables which was a unique experience. Great layout to the bar, and was perfect for our larger group as they had different sections depending on group size. Cocktails and food were good. Definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Sarah Rolland
reviews.text: We recently hosted a corporate event for 30 people at A Toi, and the experience was exceptional from start to finish. Victoria was incredible to work with throughout the planning process — she was there every step of the way, thought through every detail, and made everything seamless. On the night of the event, Katrina provided outstanding service; the level of care and attentiveness was truly impeccable. The food was fantastic, the cocktails were equally impressive, and our entire group had a wonderful experience. Highly recommend A Toi for corporate groups. We will most certainly be back.

reviews.author: THE URBAN MIXOLOGIST
reviews.text:
Great set up for a speakeasy, and the decor was top-notch. Staff were friendly and knowledgeable. Higher priced cocktails, but it seems to be expected based on the venue. Cocktail execution is 3 out of 5
It's a lot of shaking for show, but still a good bar solid cocktails. This place with a few tweaks can be awesome.

name: Arvo Coffee & Wine
address: 80 Atlantic Ave, Toronto, ON M6K 1X9, Canada
description: The Queen West version of Arvo brings Australian coffee culture to one of Toronto's best streets. Great for mornings and after five.

reviews.author: Meg Vaszko
reviews.text:
stopped by on my way to the archives museum next door
got a matcha with an espresso added in. the matcha was so flavourful and savoury. it wasn’t whisked enough so it was still clumpy
the espresso was strong and flavourful!
the cookie was out of this world. it was so soft and the chocolate was so gooey. added salt on top was so decadent and elevated the cookie
cute interior and decent prices
will for sure come back! thanks!

reviews.author: Calvin Duong (The Goods)
reviews.text:
I stopped by Arvo in Liberty Village and had a really pleasant experience. I ordered the smoked salmon toast, a honey lavender latte, and a vegan walnut banana muffin.

The smoked salmon toast was easily the standout – fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented. The combination of textures and the balance of seasoning made it a dish I’d come back for. The vegan muffin was moist, not overly sweet, and a nice pairing with the latte. The honey lavender latte was smooth but leaned a little too subtle for my taste – I was hoping for more of the floral and honey notes to come through.

Service was friendly and fairly quick, and the interior is bright, modern, and comfortable – a great place to get some work done or catch up with a coworker or classmate. There's even a meeting room tucked away, which adds to the space’s versatility.

Overall, Arvo Liberty Village is a great spot for a light bite and a productive coffee break. I’ll definitely be back for that salmon toast.

reviews.author: Naya Snow
reviews.text:
Drink quality: 3.6/5
Pastry Selection: 3.7/5
Price: 3.1/5
Ambiance: 4.4/5
Service: 4.6/5
Cleanliness: 4.6/5
Location: 3.9/5

The café has a pleasant ambiance with plenty of workstations and individual desks, making it a solid choice for getting work done. A spacious patio offers a great option for warm summer days.

Located inside the Universal Studios building and connected to a music studio, the space occasionally fills with loud music, which can be distracting for those trying to focus.

The washroom is clean, and staff are friendly and welcoming. Pastry selection is modest but available. A 16 oz Iced Vanilla Latte costs $7.05 (tax included).

reviews.author: Walnut Go
reviews.text: A gem coffee shop in downtown Toronto! Very cozy and nice place! The flat white tasted great! I’m not a big fan of burgers but the Brekkie Roll was yummy! The staff were very friendly! Their croissants were sold out so probably I’ll be back soon to try them! Strongly recommended!

reviews.author: Mikee Layaoen
reviews.text:
Lush cafe at Liberty Village for coworking and catch ups. Good amount of seating indoors and outdoors with outlets near most tables. Wifi is a bit on and off, Hotspot is helpful if it's an option you have.

The almond croissant was soft, flaky, and the perfect size. Also tried sandwiches from their all day food menu, specifically the smoked salmon toast and braised brisket sando with black garlic aioli (instead of horseradish as written on the menu).

The smoked salmon toast was fine. My preference would have been to taste a bit more salmon. It's a good sandwich if you like tangy flavours.

Braised brisket was nice and warm and the brioche bun was delicious and soft!

Customer service was friendly as well ☺️

name: Coffee Exchange
address: 109 McCaul St #34, Toronto, ON M5T 3K5, Canada
description: A Kensington institution that has anchored the corner for years. The Exchange is where the market gets its morning started.

reviews.author: Mohamed
reviews.text:
Amazing Coffee
What a hidden gem of Toronto
Great service
Really wonderful team
I pray for your success
Thanks Guys!

reviews.author: Zach Smith
reviews.text:
Coffee Exchange is awesome!  They should film this place in action and use the video to teach people how to run a coffee shop or businesses in general.  Coffee Exchange basically does everything right, not just right but well!
- staff, efficient and welcoming, always the right number of staff for how busy it is
- huge but organized selection, like 12 coffee roasts and types to choose from including exotics like coconut roast or Sumatran, all the espresso based drinks, hot and cold options and seasonal specials, massive but well organized tea selection, lots of pastry choices from bagels to brownies, all delicious
- quality products, everything from the coffees to the baked goods tastes excellent and is always fresh and ready
- freedom! You get to make the coffee how you want with lots of sweetener options and milk/alternatives to choose from
- the price is right on point!
Coffee Exchange might be the best coffee shop in Toronto!

reviews.author: SAZ
reviews.text:
The best cheap (not cheap tasting) coffee store. The flavored lattes are excellent, so many different flavors of drip coffee, and the seasonal drinks are always delicious.

Prices are right too.

reviews.author: kdm IZM
reviews.text:
3 stars because the chocolate peanut butter cookie I bought was pretty good (tastes very home made) however, carrot cake was too oily. It tasted good had it not been for the excess oil. Not sure what’s going on?

Didn’t try the coffee because I didn’t want to risk getting disappointed but based on the reviews, I might just try it.

reviews.author: Sumeya Abdurahman
reviews.text: They're nice but I just paid $6 for straight up milk tinged. I can't taste even the slightest bit of matcha in my drink

name: Coco Espresso Bar
address: 29 Bellair St, Toronto, ON M5R 1B8, Canada
description: A warm, polished Annex spot with strong espresso and a comfortable space. Coco is the kind of cafe that makes the neighbourhood better.

reviews.author: Ahmed Al-Gilany
reviews.text: I love Coco Espresso Bar! The coffee is always rich and delicious, the service is warm and friendly, and the atmosphere is so cozy. Every time I visit Toronto, this place is a must-stop for me. Highly recommended! ☕✨

reviews.author: Johnny T
reviews.text:
A must visit establishment.

This morning temperatures were in the negative double digits outside, but the moment we walked in the ambiance warmed us up.  Interior is super clean, brightly lit with music at just the right volume for the number of guests. Camilla was all smiles and prepared the kind of cappuccino that makes one take a momentary trip to Italy in every sip.

As I enjoyed my cappuccino, a message appeared in my mug "Tutto E'possible" What an awesome way to brighten one's day!?!? Pure genius! Gratzie

The bread of the panini is pure magic.  Even though all tables were packed and some guests having espresso at the bar in true Italian style, Rafaelle took the time to do a quick walk around to greet each of the guests.

The high energy operarion maintaining a cosy relaxed environment is exactly that winning cobination missing from any competition. :)

We will be back to try the aperatif later today. :)

reviews.author: Aurelia Singh
reviews.text:
I visited here on the last Thursday in August, they had a live band! This was the highlight for me as the band was playing a lot of Latin music.

I had a London fog and a croissant (requested the croissant to be warmed but that wasn’t an option). Inside the cafe was small and nice, bathrooms were clean but the outside setup for the patio was very nice.

People were dancing on the pavement and the live music had the entire street and nearby restaurants buzzing. Location was key!

reviews.author: Binita Patel
reviews.text: Ordered a latte — it tasted great! Nice spot to socialize, though there aren’t many indoor seating options. The person working there was friendly and really knowledgeable about the coffee. Overall, a good experience.

reviews.author: Richard F.
reviews.text: Great cafe in heart of Yorkville! We had a delicious hot cocoa & tiramisu cake. Both were excellent! Service by Eduardo was also great. Nice seating area & they serve liquor too. Highly recommend visit 💯

name: Coffee Lunar
address: 920 Yonge St #6, Toronto, ON M4W 3C7, Canada
description: A Yorkville cafe with a cool design-forward interior and a specialty coffee program that holds up. Worth the slightly elevated price point.

reviews.author: Daniel Hong
reviews.text:
Yum yum! Great service and absolutely loved their Cascara Tonic — refreshing, unique, and perfectly balanced. The staff were welcoming and attentive, making the whole experience even better. The café has a clean, modern vibe that’s both relaxing and inviting.

Highly recommend!

reviews.author: SHAY
reviews.text:
Nice, quiet café on Yonge & Davenport in Yorkville. 11/10 for the flat white I ordered, it went well with the delicious matcha cookie:) 🍪

I’m very much impressed with the quality of coffee. That’s good coffee!

reviews.author: Sevim Asikoglu
reviews.text:
We visited Coffee Lunar today and really enjoyed the mocha latte—it was perfectly balanced, not too sweet like some other places, and had a rich, smooth flavor. The little snacks were also a nice touch! The drink presentation was adorable with cute designs, making it feel extra special.

The café itself has a lovely aesthetic, but we did find it a bit tight on space, especially since we came with our small kids. There wasn’t much room for them to sit comfortably, which made it a little less family-friendly than we’d hoped.

Overall, a great spot for quality coffee and a cozy atmosphere—just keep in mind it might not be the most spacious for families with little ones. Would definitely return for the drinks alone!

reviews.author: Harsukhman
reviews.text: One of my favourite iced coffee drinks in the city! I ordered the vanilla drop latte which featured a nice sweetened cold foam with espresso underneath. The flavours melded well together and was quite refreshing. There is a decent seating area and desk space if you are interested in getting work done but the space isn’t too big.

reviews.author: Pavani Perera
reviews.text: Good cafe with some seating and great service. I got the matcha drop cold drink. It tasted good but I would have preferred if it were milk based rather than water based and a bit stronger of the matcha flavour. My friends tried the vanilla drop latte and they said it was fantastic.

name: Forget Me Not Coffee
address: 102 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Z4, Canada
description: A charming spot with a name that sticks for good reason. The lattes are beautiful, the space is small, and it fills up fast on weekends.

reviews.author: Angela A.
reviews.text:
I absolutely love their black sesame latte — both hot and cold - but this time I went for the cold one. It’s perfectly nutty, smooth, and just a tad sweet. The staff are super friendly, and the outdoor seats are the coveted ones when the weather’s nice. Otherwise, there’s plenty of cozy indoor seating too.

They offer a wide variety of specialty drinks, teas, and sweet treats, making it one of my favourite local cafés in Toronto to relax or catch up with friends. A must-try for latte lovers and anyone looking for an aesthetic coffee shop experience!

reviews.author: Catherine Ramkhalawan
reviews.text:
I came here on a Sunday afternoon and the place was really packed. It was a bit uncomfortable with the tight space, but it goes to show how popular they are!

I ordered their Iced Dalgona Latte and it was nice! Not super sweet, but definitely a sweet drink with the crunchy bits of dalgona melted throughout the drink. I would recommend if you haven't tried it yet.

reviews.author: Titka
reviews.text:
A cute coffee shop that’s clearly popular with locals and it’s easy to see why after tasting their coffee! I decided to skip the seasonal and specialty options, which offer quite a variety, and satisfied my sweet tooth with a pastry instead. I ordered a simple oat milk latte and a matcha cookie, and both were delicious.
There is a note in the front door for additional seating upstairs, but access to both the coffee shop and the upstairs area might be challenging for those with accessibility needs.
While the front of the store is cozy, the back section could use a little extra attention.

reviews.author: Bianca B.
reviews.text: I would always unknowingly pass forget me not when I was on Ossington, until it was introduced to me. I can say it’s my favourite go to cafe for meeting up with my friends or if Im stopping by as an early morning treat before I start work! Both locations have a cozy and friendly vibe and I enjoy their matchas and dalgona lattes.

reviews.author: glueee
reviews.text:
Had this a long time ago so no photo, but I absolutely love the hot black sesame latte
*guys I take it back, I got the less ice version today and it is so watery, I tried so hard to find any bit of the sesame taste but instead it just tastes like iced milk.

name: Balzac's Distillery District
address: 1 Trinity St, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: A Toronto classic in a landmark location. Balzac's in the Distillery District is a must-visit, especially when the cobblestone patio opens up.

reviews.author: Sylvia Schmidt
reviews.text:
Stopped by Balzac's in the Distillery District during a snowstorm when a lot of the other shops were closed. Glad they were open! The space is beautiful—historic brick, high ceilings, and cozy vibes.

We got the S’mores Hot Chocolate ($10), topped with whipped cream, toasted marshmallow, graham cracker, and chocolate drizzle. It’s was very sweet, and the sugary flavour overpowered the chocolate itself.

Upstairs seating was a bit distracting with someone on speakerphone FaceTiming. It’s a shame because the café itself is gorgeous, but the experience depends a lot on other customers being considerate.

Overall, nice to have a spot open during the storm, but the drink and noisy environment made it less enjoyable than it could’ve been.

reviews.author: Verdict.
reviews.text: The cheddar-and-chive scone at Balzac’s was a standout — fluffy rather than dense, and genuinely well made. The latte and salted-caramel latte were good overall, though the first sip on both leaned a bit acidic. Service was fine, but even as the next in line we waited longer than expected. Still, it’s a pleasant spot to visit, and a nice place to stop if you’re in the Distillery District, especially for a solid pastry and a classic Balzac’s atmosphere.

reviews.author: Karen alves gaspar
reviews.text:
For those visiting the Distillery, a great place for a good coffee is definitely Balzac.

The establishment boasts a rustic, stylish atmosphere with a calm and welcoming atmosphere.

The service is excellent, with a very polite and attentive staff who are always ready to help.

For customers with disabilities, unfortunately, the space does not have a Braille menu, but the staff is always ready to assist. Furthermore, the location does not have an automatic door opening system, and because it is a heavy door, assistance is required.

In addition to the dining room, the café also has a patio for the spring and summer months.

The prices are affordable, and it is worth a visit.

reviews.author: Uly Wolters
reviews.text:
Great location, but only medium coffee.
This shop could be so much more!
Friendly team, but chairs & table design without stringent concept and love to detail. Foam is poor, coffee medium, Barista should be trained as machine is good.

reviews.author: Laura Montague
reviews.text:
Beautiful historic charm throughout this commercial space offering a cool place to enjoy a delicious a beverage!

Tried the new Premier Protein Peppermint Mocha and it was delicious. Hot beverage on a cold evening at the Distillery Market!

name: Dineen Coffee Co.
address: 140 Yonge St, Toronto, ON M5C 1X6, Canada
description: Set inside a beautiful historic building downtown, Dineen is one of Toronto's most atmospheric cafes. The space is gorgeous, the coffee earns its place.

reviews.author: Paul Nazareth
reviews.text:
Is this the best Dineen location?

I’m not sure, but it is for me.

Is this the best Bay st. Adjacent spot to close the deal over a Cortado after lunch at Sud Forno?

I’m not sure, but it is for me.

In this one of the most aesthetic delight per-square-foot cafes on Yonge with Carrot Cake?

I’m not sure, but it is for me.

reviews.author: Annabelle
reviews.text: great building and coffee. free wifi, good spots for working, outlets, and standard two hour time limit. Iced americano was nice and strong. the other drinks and pastries also look really good so i’ll try them next time. staff is polite and quick. the best part is the atmosphere and interior design for sure.

reviews.author: Angela A.
reviews.text: Housed in a gorgeous historical building, this café’s design has stood the test of time and still feels as inviting as ever. It’s the perfect setting for coffee meetings - whether for business or pleasure. The outdoor patio, framed by a beautiful wrought iron fence, adds to its charm and makes it an ideal spot to linger. The coffee is consistently excellent - never burnt, never sour. I haven’t tried their pastries yet, but they always look tempting in the display case.

reviews.author: Victoria Mancuso
reviews.text: This is my daily go-to. Very friendly staff, great coffee and yummy baked goods! Also has a cozy vibe and great seasonal drinks! They also have a workspace at the back! Definitely a space I could squat for a while

reviews.author: Denise S
reviews.text:
Great coffee option for socializing, meetings or reading/getting work done. I like that the banquette tables are designated no laptop/tablet zones, so that one person with a laptop doesn't occupy a prime table for 2 for hours on end. Rather there are single seats in other areas that are designated for people doing work. There is free WiFi with a 2 hour maximum which seems fair.

Coffee drinks are consistently good. Is it the best coffee in the city? No. But it is pretty good. They have croissants, yogurt and 3 kinds of breakfast sandwiches (the Italian sausage/cheese/egg one is pretty tasty!). In the summer, there is a cute patio facing temperance street.

Definitely better than any of the big chains. Nice, bright decor and pretty floors. This is a good option if you are in the Financial district.

If it's full, you can also get coffee across the street at Sud Forno.

name: White Squirrel Coffee Shop
address: 907 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1G5, Canada
description: An underrated Queen West gem that earns its regulars through quiet consistency. Good wifi, good coffee, and a calm space to actually think.

reviews.author: Annabelle Yee
reviews.text: This is a great spot to grab a cup of coffee and squeeze some work in / have a conversation with friends. The upper level seats around 14 people comfortably, and there are a ton of outlets around. The coffee (I had a flat white) isn’t bad either - they use SJCB beans! I didn’t try them, but they sell a variety of pastries and empanadas as well!

reviews.author: Tina Stec
reviews.text: Overall I had to give this place a 2 (I’m being generous).  My daughter got her usual macha drink, which was a small and it turned out to be over $9.  For that money, you better give me a large cup.  Yeah, I get it, you’re on Queen West (congratulations!).  The price for a cappuccino was ridiculous too and looked just a little bigger than an en espresso, so I said forget it.  As we were walking, my daughter took a sip and said did she even put a vanilla in this thing (no flavour)?  She just threw it out.  Obviously the girl was having a bad day or needed a coffee herself to wake up (no personality).  All I know is that there are tons of other places on Queen to get a great cup of coffee and you’ll have great staff and a great drink.  This is the first and last time I’ll go to the White Squirrel.  It’s right across from Bellwoods Park for those who are interested, but keep walking.  You’ll find another coffee shop in a few steps.

reviews.author: Joaquin Villalobos
reviews.text: This coffee haven pairs expertly brewed espresso with the warm, sweet soul of Chilean pastries — available fresh Friday through Sunday. The vibe is laid-back and welcoming, perfect for slow weekend mornings or casual catch-ups. Every sip and bite feels like a gentle hug, with pastries that shine in authentic flavour and homely charm. Friendly service and a comfy atmosphere make it a must-visit for coffee lovers and curious taste-seekers alike. A delightful blend of caffeine and culture right in your cup and on your plate.

reviews.author: Kara Kirk
reviews.text: Stopped by this cozy coffee shop before our walk through Trinity over the long Thanksgiving weekend and had such a great experience! I ordered a hot matcha latte and a large coffee, both were delicious. I loved that they have an outdoor order window, and the person working was super friendly, helpful, and made great conversation. Such a nice start to the day!

reviews.author: Diana
reviews.text: I love their butter croissant, it has a strong butter flavor and it’s on the softer side. I don’t like giant crunchy super flaky croissants where half the pastry crumbles and flakes everywhere. Absolutely top one or two croissants I’ve had in my life so far. So if you have a preference for buttery softer croissants I recommend! I just prefer softer textures overall and this is my go-to croissant spot. (Soft isn’t soggy!!!) I also love their cold brew, it’s mild and easy to drink but flavorful. It’s not sour or bitter and it’s pleasant to drink. I haven’t tried a lot of things on the menu but I always order these two items.

name: The Coffee
address: 150 Bloor St W, Toronto, ON M5S 1M4, Canada
description: A tucked-away Annex cafe that earns its regulars through reliability. Fast wifi, usually quiet, and a room that makes it easy to stay.

reviews.author: yawen zheng
reviews.text:
A lovely Japanese-style café with a calm Muji-inspired design — clean, minimal, and beautifully simple. The space is small but feels very cozy and well thought out. I had an iced oat latte, which was smooth, rich, and delicious.

There are no power outlets, so it’s not for working or studying, but it’s perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends. The seating is slightly firm but very comfortable. Such a peaceful spot — I really loved the vibe here.

reviews.author: H H
reviews.text:
The coffee here is absolutely delicious, and the service is outstanding!
Everyone is so kind and welcoming that you instantly feel at home. The atmosphere is cozy and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a slow morning or catching up with friends. I highly recommend trying their grilled ham & cheese — it’s amazing! They also have such a great selection of drinks with generous portions. Can’t wait to come back again! ☕🥪✨

reviews.author: Fabiano Daher
reviews.text:
The Coffee is a fantastic shop that truly stands out, thanks to both a wonderful team of baristas and an incredible product. From the moment I arrived, I felt welcome, and I even had the opportunity to have a great chat with the staff, which made the experience even better.

The coffee itself is the real star here. You can taste that the beans are of excellent quality and origin; they are roasted to near perfection, resulting in a cup that is rich and full of flavor. To top it off, the food selection is satisfying and pairs beautifully with the beverages.

I had a great time relaxing here and will definitely be coming back soon to taste other items on the menu. If you are looking for a wonderful place to unwind with a quality drink, this is definitely a recommended spot!

reviews.author: Fighter Fu
reviews.text:
Hidden gem in the town!

It’s lowkey hard to find this place cuz you have to go between buildings (kindof.. there’s like a dent beside louis vuitton) but it’s worth a visit!

Clean, japanese aesthetic
Lots of options for drinks
I got matcha latte and tbh it’s kindof mid
But the staffs seemed professional and know what they’re doing

Not a lot of seating area, and there’s no plug so I recommend fully charging ur stuff if you’re planning to work here

Overall, cute cute cafe😍

reviews.author: Charlotte Chau
reviews.text:
I got a Matcha Vanilla Latte and a classic chocolate chip cookie. The matcha is smaller than I expected but flavour and texture is great. The art was pretty too. Perfect temperature.

The cookie is a great cookie. It is on the nuttier, crumbier side, but has a nice milk chocolate to cookie ratio, and is a perfect amount of thin with specks of salt within. It’s crumby but still chewy and not crispy.

I liked taking my time looking at the menu on the device, and still got a smile from the barista.

name: WFM Coffee Bar
address: 87 Avenue Rd, Toronto, ON M5R 3R9, Canada
description: The Annex's no-frills coffee stop on Bloor. WFM keeps the menu short, the line moving, and the espresso consistently good.

reviews.author: Victoria Procunier
reviews.text: A consistently good latte and at $4.20 it is also a more reasonable option in the area. I also like that they’re open in the morning for a post-workout drink! They also sell some baked goods, snacks and drinks like Greenhouse juices. The have places to sit with USB charging ports.

reviews.author: Upkar Arora
reviews.text: Had a cardamom latte which was delicious, and the order for my high maintenance guest was made to perfection. The best part however was the barista who was warm and patient. When a homeless woman came in, she treated her with compassion and dignity as she gave her a coffee - as if she was an regular, affluent Yorkville client. Heartwarming to see.

reviews.author: T M
reviews.text: Got a cold brew there. It tasted alright. But the cup leaked everytime I took a sip. Their iced cup lids don't fit well. Would've preferred not to wear half my coffee. Don't recommend it unless you're wearing a waterproof poncho you don't mind getting coffee all over. Really made that afternoon less pleasant.

reviews.author: Nicky
reviews.text: As a huge juice/smoothie fan, having worked at a similar bar back in Australia for a number of years with epic enthusiasm I was a bit underwhelmed by my experience here. Sure the place was clean and the Apple Ginger Zip would have put a spring in my step had the bloke in the purple beanie serving me not been a real wet blanket. From the moment I entered, to having my order taken/presented and to the moment I left, he did not smile once. Thought maybe it was me, but the other 5 customers who came in after me didn’t get a smile either. He just looked like even hell would have been a better place to be on a Saturday afternoon. Sure, the guy might have some personal stuff going on or whatever, I don’t know, but it brought the vibe in the crashing down around my ankles and in a Canadian winter that’s the opposite of what I want buying an innocent refreshment. Cheer up son, life’s not that bad.

reviews.author: Coffee and cactus
reviews.text: Great coffee and wonderful muffins. Staff were helpful and attentive.

name: 5 Elements Espresso Bar
address: 131 Avenue Rd, Toronto, ON M5R 2H7, Canada
description: A focused Annex spot with a tasting-menu approach to espresso. 5 Elements is for the genuinely curious coffee drinker in Toronto.

reviews.author: FAB B.
reviews.text: I went today for my first time and me and my friend ordered a small coffee a cappuccino and a look like an almond croissant, total cost $ 14 and change, just the croissant was over $6 and it was awful, no trace of any almond flavour, on top it was baked wrong since i could still taste flour, so the person who baked not a baker at all, the cappuccino was made with either 1 or 2 % milk , unfortunately real italians use homo milk or nut juice ( milk come from animals, veg is not milk but nothing else than 5 nuts and 1 lt of filtered water) so in the end i would not recommend this place unless you are a peasant italian who is willing to pay for less!If you charge me high prices, well your Quality must be excellent and not this crap!!! As authentic Roman, 3rd gen who live in Canada since 1999 ,knows about food( im a food blogger and also an italian chef/baker/pastry maker) i want only the best for my money! Unfortunately in Canada 99.99% of food IS NOT ITALIAN AT ALL, and i am very disappointed by people who abuse it, specially restaurants and pizzerias owned from not Romans!!

reviews.author: Imo Love
reviews.text: Everything about the café itself was really nice. It seems like they have a lot of regulars, which is great, but as a first-time visitor, I did notice that some customers were a bit territorial about the space. For example, when I tried to get by to use the restroom (which is downstairs near a few tables), people didn’t really move and just stood and blocked the way, even when I politely said “excuse me.” Other than that, I have no complaints — the café itself was lovely.

reviews.author: Konstantinos Katsaris
reviews.text: Great place to grab a quick bite in Yorkville area without the hustle and bustle. Consistently making good coffee and pastries are always fresh. Sandwiches are prepared in house and done generously and are worth the price to fill you up. Very comfortable cozy feeling upstairs and downstairs with a fireplace and plenty of outlets to charge your phone or pro on your laptop. When the weather is tolerable the outdoor patio has tables and chairs with outdoor cushions and Muskoka chairs to unwind in. If you’re visiting Toronto or live in Toronto great place to stop by and unwind. Free wi fi for those require it also.

reviews.author: RG
