reviews.text: The coolest morning cafe I've visited. Its charming in its unique way, there's dedicated spots to bring a laptop or a newspaper and grab a coffee. A Lil breakfast great I enjoyed was there steaming boiled egg. Highly recommend checking it out on a walk.

reviews.author: tom cameron
reviews.text:
Expresso Coffee was fine.
Atmosphere inside and outside was great.
No idea about service because my friend ordered for me.
Pictures are deceiving - what use is a ramp on the outside door for one small step when the service counter is up a large staircase inside?
This location, although very nice, is totally inaccessible for everyone with mobility issues.

name: Pilot Coffee Roasters
address: 117 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Z2, Canada
description: One of Toronto's best roasters with a flagship on Ossington that lives up to the reputation. Come for the pour-over, stay for the beans.

reviews.author: Jonathan Prias
reviews.text: We visited this café during the LEGO Botanicals pop-up bar. It was a fantastic event with beautiful decorations. On top of that, the coffee was excellent, so it’s definitely worth visiting even without the LEGO event just to enjoy a great drink.

reviews.author: 김원희
reviews.text:
I had such a wonderful experience at this cafe! The atmosphere is so cozy and the barista was incredibly kind and professional. I’m quite sensitive to caffeine and have a milk allergy, but the barista wearing the black hat went above and beyond to double-check the ingredients for me.
He recommended the Miso Hojicha Latte, and it was absolutely delicious. It has a beautiful, deep nutty flavor—almost like black sesame—with a hint of saltiness that balances everything perfectly. The latte art was stunning and the texture was so smooth. I really appreciated the extra care taken for my dietary needs.  I’ll definitely be back soon! ❤️

reviews.author: Ngoc Linh
reviews.text:
Mocha is great but matcha latte is not.
The place looks nice and fresh but drink is very basic.

reviews.author: Soban A
reviews.text: I’d heard about this coffee roaster before but had never actually tried their coffee. If you search for coffee roasters in Toronto, this place usually pops up near the top. I happened to be walking down Ossington and stumbled across it, so I figured it was a good time to finally give it a shot. The vibe of the café is kinda industrial, high ceilings, a wide open layout, and multiple espresso machines built right into the counter, which I’ve honestly never seen anywhere else. There’s also a little retail corner where you can buy their beans. I’ve heard their “Heritage” blend is what they’re known for, and that’s what they use for most of their drinks in the café. The menu has a good range of both hot and cold options. They also have a small pastry selection, though I don’t think they bake them in-house. They had a couple of cold brew towers, which are pretty rare to see in Toronto. As for the coffee itself, it was really good. Smooth, earthy, and topped with a nice layer of espresso foam. I’ll definitely be back, and next time I’m grabbing a bag of beans to take home.

reviews.author: Cindy Hum
reviews.text:
This location is a lovely cafe spot with plenty of seating and aesthetic atmosphere. Have only tried the Mango sticky rice matcha here - unfortunately too sweet and not enough matcha flavour.

Generally, have tried various Pilot Coffee Roasters locations and the coffee is great, but the matcha is lacking. Have not tried the coffee at this location personally but I’d assume consistent quality.

name: I DEAL COFFEE & WINE
address: 162 Ossington Ave, Toronto, ON M6J 2Z7, Canada
description: Coffee and wine done right on Ossington. I DEAL handles both with equal care and zero fussiness. One of the strip's best kept secrets.

reviews.author: Conner Jolly
reviews.text: this was probably our favorite coffee place close by.  It has kind of a rustic atmosphere with splotched paint, nice natural lighting and plants all around.  We stopped in for a cold brew but stuck around for a breakfast sandwitch as well.  The cold brew was light and fruity which is just like we like it.  I think they even mentioned that if we got beens then it comes with a coffee which is cool.  The staff was pretty friendly as well.  We talked to them for a while and they had some good recomendations they shared with us.  Overall a good spot for a coffee.

reviews.author: Suzan A
reviews.text: Great coffee and pastries! I usually go in during the morning when it’s less busy— it’s nice, clam and relaxing! Perfect way to easy into your day 🙂

reviews.author: Ryan Foster
reviews.text: Being newly teetotal, but still social, I found this place on Ossington and came in for the tea and WiFi.  I had the Verbena Blues, and the tea was blue, warm, and sweetened with honey.  Great experience, lots of great food on the menu, market fresh, wonderful staff. Lots of beer options on Ossington, you can get something different in here.

reviews.author: Bella Mur
reviews.text: What’s great about this place is that they have great coffee, wine and snacks. The patio is full of sun when the weather is good. They offer delicious, hot breakfast sandwiches. Happy hour is 5-7pm with $10 for a glass of wine. It’s a great place to work at, or read, or catch up with some friends.

reviews.author: Allie S
reviews.text:
5 stars for atmosphere. Ideal to settle into work mode. Comfy yet studious, with actual pew benches to sit at.

Perfectly adequate drip coffee, a little watery. Ginger cookie could be less sweet and more gingery for my taste.

There is a no laptops rule applicable on weekends after 11am. Seems fair.

Lots of single and double seating indoors. Larger patio tables on side street. Also offers wine, like a Côtes du rhône

name: Daily Ritual Cafe
address: 1 King St W, Toronto, ON M5H 1A1, Canada
description: A downtown cafe that takes the word ritual seriously. Consistent, well-made coffee in a calm setting that makes the morning feel worthwhile.

reviews.author: Amisha A
reviews.text:
Daily Ritual cafe is a cute little cafe. The place is such a vibe. Everything was fresh and looked so tempting.

The have a variety of croissants, juices and other bakery stuff along with amazing coffee. The café is well maintained and cute.

The service was good overall. It is definitely worth a try.

reviews.author: Béni Mathias
reviews.text: The food was decent, the service was okay, and we had a good time. Since I was staying at the hotel, it was convenient and worked well. I’m not really a coffee person myself, but my girlfriend really enjoyed her coffee. I’d definitely recommend it if you’re staying nearby.

reviews.author: Chris Graham
reviews.text: Visited on a Saturday lunch time, greeted by friendly and efficient staff.  I went for the 12oz coffee and it was pretty good.  Strong and with flavour and reasonably priced.  As it was a Saturday there was seating available.  The establishment appears to be well maintained.

reviews.author: Faiz H
reviews.text: For being in the financial district, you would expect a little better coffee and treats. My usual latte order was water down and felt like a cheap bean more than the usual beans you find nearby. Unfortunately the croissant didn't hit the spot either. Too dense and tough, made me feel like it may have been a day old.

reviews.author: layali jassim
reviews.text: I rarely write reviews, but I feel like this experience deserves one. The barista who served me was surprisingly rude and gave me so much sass for absolutely no reason. Which was disappointing because the pastry was great and the coffee itself was decent. I was the only person in line, so it wasn’t even a busy moment. I simply asked if they offered a caramel macchiato, and the barista responded with unnecessary attitude, saying it was “a Starbucks exclusive item.” That’s not accurate — plenty of cafés make macchiatos, and the difference between a latte and a macchiato is common knowledge. She could’ve easily offered to make something similar since they clearly had the ingredients for one. A little courtesy and correct information would’ve made the experience much better. Unfortunately, after this interaction, I won’t be coming here again.

name: FILM CAFE
address: 230 Augusta Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2L6, Canada
description: A Kensington cafe for film lovers and those who wish they were. The programming is fun and the coffee is good enough to sit through it.

reviews.author: Headlight Fluid
reviews.text: Such a cozy, festive and adorable spot! Cancelled our dinner reservations to stay after finding this place 🥰 My amazing boyfriend and I ended up getting a cocktail and charcuterie board. It was our favorite part to our perfect Valentine's Day night 🖤💙🩵💜 Monica was great!

reviews.author: Raveena Rani
reviews.text: The café had a beautiful, cozy atmosphere. We enjoyed the strawberry matcha, though the mango smoothie was average.

reviews.author: Asmita Sadul
reviews.text:
Absolutely loved this cafe! The Smoked Marshmallow Mocha & Cappuccino were amazing. For breakfast, we had the Rancheros & All Day Breakfast both fresh & delicious.

The overall vibe around 10am was just perfect — cozy & full of good energy. It’s a great spot to start your day, get some work done, or catch up with friends over breakfast or brunch. The music and service add to the cozy, welcoming atmosphere. Definitely a place I’ll be coming back to!

reviews.author: Vivian I
reviews.text: Excellent study spot! We got the spiced apple cider chai and pumpkin mocha, both were delicious and we also got the carrot cake cheesecake which was very yummy. Would definitely come back!

reviews.author: David Rios
reviews.text: I had a hot chocolate and croissant, the restaurant it’s so nice, the food is so delicious, the people that work in the cafe are very nice, but with all this set up and, looking so nice and with the delicious products I was hoping to get more jazzy, bolero tango for music; I heard some bangers, usher and stuff but I drinking hot chocolate, hope the music selection is better the next time I come around. Great food, great service, music wasn’t it. Music is a very important element.

name: LADURÉE Toronto - Yorkville Café
address: 162 Cumberland St, Toronto, ON M5R 3N5, Canada
description: The Toronto home of the legendary Parisian patisserie. Laduree in Yorkville is as beautiful as you'd expect and worth every dollar.

reviews.author: Kristina Iskandar
reviews.text: The staff were really friendly and pleasant, I enjoyed hot chocolate. One small suggestion about the service; it would be great if you could ask customers whether they prefer a regular cup or a takeaway cup, and whether they’d like the whipped cream on the side. With hot chocolate season coming up, this little touch would make a big difference. I’m attaching a video of how it was served at Ladurée abroad; it would be lovely to see something similar here in Toronto too.

reviews.author: Celine.S
reviews.text: Service in this location was absolutely awful from one of the employees👇this gentleman was so unhappy to serve us I’m not sure what the issue was but don’t work in a beautiful aesthetically pleasing place if you don’t want people to take pictures period. This was absolutely beautiful designed cafe and definitely felt the need to do a review however he killed my experience. Ended up ordering a coffee and few macarons  and left unfortunately I would have ordered more and stayed but I couldn’t look at his miserable face any longer. Coffee with raspberry syrup, Rose petals and whipped cream however the coffee was flavourless but I’m not surprised cause they’re miserable man made it but Macaron were delicious. The other young gentlemen working was very pleasant and had a smile. Will no be back to this location.

reviews.author: Rose Dalrymple
reviews.text:
Walking into Ladurée feels like stepping into a pastel-painted daydream where even your worries get politely asked to wait outside. 💫

The latte with oat milk + cinnamon shot? Let’s just say my taste buds did a backflip, high-fived, and then whispered “never settle for less again.” The whole vibe is calm, cozy, and sprinkled with the friendliest service you could hope for.

If you’re in the area, this little slice of heaven is worth every sip ☕️

reviews.author: Jim Sairoglou
reviews.text:
The food is good but not a huge selection.  A really good stop if you're looking for a really tasty snack and a coffee.
The space and tables are very small so only good for groups of 2 - 4 max.  The coffee was really good 👍

reviews.author: Nadia
reviews.text: i ordered a marie antoinette tea and cotton candy macaroon. i took a bit of the macaroon and noticed it tasted like a different flavour. i went up to the employees and said this tastes different then i thought as the cotton candy one looked like it had sprinkles and the employee i ordered with goes its cotton candy. and the one next to her said no it’s rose….. lol ,they were very nice, so i just paid for a new one

name: Cafe X Bica
address: 160 Pears Ave. Unit 100, Toronto, ON M5R 1S9, Canada
description: A downtown cafe with Portuguese roots and a strong espresso tradition. Cafe X Bica is a neighbourhood spot with real character.

reviews.author: Nathalie Prézeau
reviews.text:
I'm giving 5 stars to this café because it is unique! (My latte and the few items we tried out of the menu were also quite good!) It is actually set in a sort of gazebo built within the elegant showroom of design firm Atriani.

We loved all the details in this café: the repeated curved pattern, the soft palette of the room with matching covers. They do their own bakery. We loved the Bolognese crepe, the chicken sandwich and the orange cake. And left with decadent cookies to eat at home.

We found free street parking on Roxborough (good for 3 hours) and explored Yonge Street (a 10-minute walk away) where the fun store Putti awaited. On the way back, we walked through lovely Ramsden Park.

reviews.author: Bino
reviews.text: Great experience at this cafe. It’s super aesthetically pleasing and their staff is very friendly and helpful with recommendations. The matcha isn’t so strong, it tastes more like milk than matcha, their iced latte is good and you can really tell that the quality of the espresso they use it’s top notch. Their cookies were delicious but a bit too oily. The gluten free orange cake was my favourite. The sandwiches were a bit dry too. I’d visit again and order the iced latte instead. Something that caught my eye was the Black Forest hot chocolate, might give it a try next time.

reviews.author: Jessica M
reviews.text:
It’s such a beautiful spot, cozy and inviting. The oat milk latte was so delicious! I also enjoyed one of their baked goods. The staff was amazing and very helpful.

I look forward to my next visit.

reviews.author: Emily Lashkhia
reviews.text:
I work at a place similar to this (food establishment connected to furniture store lol), so it was fun to see another one.

I LOVE pao de queijo so I was pleasantly surprised to see it here! It was very yummy, and the price wasn’t the worst.

The sandwich on the other hand is…what you pay for. It’s 8$, so I didn’t expect much, and it’s literally just turkey and cheese on meh sourdough.

Coffee was good and the barista was kind to explain any questions I had. It’s a good place to catch up with friends or work.

Thank you for having me :)

reviews.author: Alisha Khan
reviews.text: Such a lovely café! The coffee was smooth and perfectly made, the cake was moist and rich, and the sandwich tasted fresh and flavorful. Cozy atmosphere with beautiful presentation — a great spot to relax. Would definitely visit again ☕️

name: ZAZA Espresso Bar
address: 162 Cumberland St, Toronto, ON M5R 3N5, Canada
description: A compact Yorkville espresso bar open late into the evening. ZAZA punches above its size with precise espresso and a quick-moving counter.

reviews.author: Kenia Rodriguez
reviews.text:
ZAZA Espresso Bar is a nice option for a quick snack stop. They have a good variety of flavours. I got 2 scoops, mango and watermelon. The mango gelato was my favourite as it had a creamy texture and sweet taste. The watermelon on the other hand wasn’t really my cup of tea, since I found the taste similar to a jolly rancher.
The interior was small but spacious, and it has 2 entrances/exits, so it doesn’t really feel crowded.
The service was quick and clean, but the staff had a cold and rushed attitude, even though it wasn’t busy.

reviews.author: G D
reviews.text: A good spot in Yorkville to have a coffee break. Great menu with hot and cold drinks. The pastries look potent and deadly 😁😍. Plenty of seats inside or outside. On a good day, it's ideal to chill and fun to people watch. Good tea selection and the staff happily refills hot water anytime. Fair price for Yorkville.

reviews.author: Food lover
reviews.text:
The next stop on my Yorkville coffee tour was Zaza. What I can say is that the cappuccino was prepared perfectly – the flavor of the coffee was much better here compared to Coco.

The staff this time wasn’t as friendly with me, but to be fair it was a very busy period, so I can understand. I asked quite a lot of questions, and they answered them easily, though a bit rushed – it felt like they were just trying to serve as many people as possible.

In summary: the taste of the coffee was excellent, and the cappuccino foam was perfect. The interaction with the staff was okay but slightly lacking warmth. Overall, I would also give 3 out of 5 stars to Zaza.

reviews.author: Pavanndeep Kaur
reviews.text:
**Zaza Cafe - Cumberland, Toronto**

I visited Zaza Cafe last week and had a great experience! I tried their latte, which was delicious, and the pistachio loaf bar was equally good. The vibe of the cafe is amazing – it’s a nice, cute spot with a lot of charm. It was pretty busy when I went, so it was hard to find a seat inside, but they do have a little patio, which is a nice alternative. If you're in the area and looking for a cozy place to grab a coffee, Zaza is definitely worth checking out!

reviews.author: Glenn
reviews.text:
I have walked by this place numerous times but never had tried it until today. It was about half full when I arrived and I was second in line. Only 1 server but she was friendly and efficient. My Americano was very good. I added a blueberry scone and it was fresh tasting and very light. I kind of prefer a more sense scone but it was very good nonetheless. The atmosphere is quite good and not too loud.
#coffee #Yorkville #cafe

name: Amadeus Patisserie
address: 1235 Bay St., Toronto, ON M5R 3K4, Canada
description: An Annex patisserie where the coffee holds its own against the pastries. The baked goods are the real story but the espresso keeps up.

reviews.author: A.J.
reviews.text:
Their dining room feels bright and clean, and has a good amount of tables and seating. The display cases are also kept clean and their pastries are lined up and look pretty inviting.

I tried a chocolate and raspberry tart and I liked it. The ingredients tasted like they were good quality and fresh. The textures were nice and it wasn’t too sweet. I had a cortado with it which was decent as well. Would be interested to try their other pastries.

reviews.author: hassan Al Gailani
reviews.text:
We were walking in Yorkville and happened to pass by Amadeus Patisserie — and honestly, we couldn’t resist going in. The décor and the display alone were enough to pull us inside.

The staff were very friendly and knowledgeable about their products. They recommended a few pastries, and we decided to take some items home with us. Everything overall felt like a solid 5/5 experience — the service, the presentation, the atmosphere.

The only thing I’d rate slightly lower was the flavor of the cake we tried, which for me was more of a 4/5. Still good, but next time I’d love to try their pastries, which I believe might be even better.

It ended up being one of those spontaneous stops that turns into a great find. I’ll definitely stop by again the next time I’m in town.

reviews.author: Michele Mitchell
reviews.text:
So glad I stumbled across this bakery! As I was walking to an event, I saw this beautiful bakery and vowed to return. Luckily, the event fell through and I found myself in front of the bakery. I selected the macrons (rose, mango and salted caramel) and also the pastry with goat cheese and spices. Everything tasted exquisite.

My only complaints on my second visit were that there was no clear line (people were walking in front of you), there was no place to sit down and enjoy your food. Additionally, the prices for the mini cakes were quite steep. I wanted to basically taste test things for my upcoming birthday and my parents' anniversary but didn't want to pay an arm and leg for it. Everything else was reasonably priced. In the end, I got the EXOTIC vegan cake which was very tasty (mango, coconut with other tropical flavours) for my birthday.

On my first trip, I got the raspberry cake/tarte which was not too sweet, just perfect! I would highly recommend this bakery!

reviews.author: 04mitche
reviews.text: Excellent bakery!👌🏾The desserts I have  tasted from the savory goat cheese and zatar croissant to the delicious 😋 raspberry filled mini cake 🍰 with it's tangy sweetness to the exotic tropical, vegan cake- all were excellent. The prices for the smaller cake were a bit steep (I believe $9) which would be less than 2 slices. I think the macrons were great- selection was top tier. It was always busy whenever I went so I could never get a seat! Otherwise, I highly recommend!

reviews.author: VJ VEL
reviews.text:
Such a charming spot in Yorkville! The interior has a cozy European café vibe — elegant yet welcoming. The display of pastries, croissants, and macarons looks absolutely stunning and everything feels freshly made with care & lovely presentation. Prices are a little steep though.

Definitely looking forward to coming back to try their mushroom truffle croissant next time. A lovely find for anyone who enjoys great pastries and a relaxed café atmosphere. ☕🥐✨

name: A-OK CAFE
address: 50 Bloor St W, Toronto, ON M4W 3L8, Canada
description: A reliable Annex cafe with friendly service and solid coffee. A-OK Cafe has over 300 Google reviews and is a go-to for locals in the neighbourhood. See hours and photos.

reviews.author: Chris Graham
reviews.text: Upscale coffee shop.  Greeted by friendly staff, I stopped by for a black coffee.  Strong coffee, yet, with a nice taste and aroma.  Several tables are available and there is a nice large window so you can see the passings in the street.  Prices are a bit on the high side, yet, this is downtown Toronto.  Well maintained coffee shop.  Does not accept cash.

reviews.author: Sergio Ballesteros Jr
reviews.text:
Finally a café that gets it right! A-OK Café has a warm, inviting vibe and a refreshing “laptop-free” atmosphere that makes it the perfect spot to actually relax and enjoy your drink. The baristas are genuinely friendly and welcoming, and you can feel that good energy as soon as you walk in.

I had both the café latte and matcha latte, and they were absolutely amazing, and perfectly balanced, smooth, and beautifully presented. It’s such a rare find in Toronto to have a place where you can actually sit down, unwind, and not compete with a sea of open laptops.

If you’re looking for a cozy, stylish spot to meet a friend or just take a break from the bustle of Bloor Street, A-OK Café is your place

reviews.author: Marysol Martin
reviews.text: Always my favourite go-to coffee/matcha place. Especially when I’m just shopping and want a drink to walk around with. Plus, the matcha here is one of the best I have ever had. 10/10

reviews.author: Jordana
reviews.text: Great to have a laptop-free cafe for a change and be around people having actual conversations! The space is pretty small/tight, so best for one-on-one hangouts or solo outings. I ordered the vanilla latte and it tasted great. Also, you get a complimentary drink token to use at the cafe if you buy something at Aritzia, which I thought was super cute. Makes for a memorable experience:)

reviews.author: Irteza Ahmed
reviews.text:
Very cute cafe connected to Aritzia! Staff were very kind and quick to deliver our orders. The tomato and basil danish is really good, I highly recommend it! We also got an espresso (alright quality) and a matcha latte (a bit fishy) but still giving 5/5 to endorse this cute boutique cafe. The cups and colour scheme (dark green) are very tasteful.

Seating is limited so keep that in mind! You will likely not find a seat here during busy hours. Also there is no bathroom in the cafe, but there is a bathroom in Aritzia itself. Overall a great experience and would definitely come again!

name: Fungo Cafe
address: 800 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1N6, Canada
description: A King West spot inspired by Japanese kissaten culture. Fungo is calm, considered, and serves some of the most interesting coffee on the strip.

reviews.author: Jasdeep Grewal
reviews.text:
I recently visited Fungo Cafe, a specialty coffee shop that serves organic coffee blended with premium mushroom powders, and had a great experience.

The cafe has a relaxed, cozy atmosphere with lots of intimate seating, making it a comfortable spot to catch up with friends, get some work done, or simply unwind. It’s also dog-friendly, which adds to the welcoming vibe.

We ordered a lavender London Fog and a turmeric chai latte. The turmeric chai was warm, comforting, and full of flavor, while the London Fog was smooth and well balanced with a subtle hint of vanilla. For food, we tried the chive cheddar scone, which had a nicely crumbly exterior and a soft interior, along with a classic chocolate croissant and a pistachio chocolate croissant. The pistachio croissant was the standout and is especially good when warmed up.

Portion sizes were generous, and everything we tried felt high quality and thoughtfully prepared. Overall, this is a great option if you’re looking for something a little different from your usual coffee stop. I would definitely recommend checking it out.

reviews.author: Daria Kurovskaya
reviews.text: Cozy and quiet coffee shop with a really nice design and comfortable seating. The croissant I had was very tasty. My cappuccino wasn’t quite to my taste (a bit on the bitter side with a slight aftertaste), but everything else about the place was great. Overall, I enjoyed my visit and would be happy to come back and give it another try.

reviews.author: Gautam Suri
reviews.text:
Lovely cozy cafe that serves a great Hot Chocolate! 👌Vegan Approved

The lady at the counter was very courteous, helped me tweak my order based on my preferences. Their hot chocolate is really dark and yummy.

The cafe has a nice cozy vibe with a lot of plants. They added a few plants as we were seated there. The music seemed a touch loud but nevertheless great place to work or just chill with a book.

reviews.author: Amir Moghisy
reviews.text:
We came in and were greeted with a very nice and cozy atmosphere, warm smiles and smell of fresh coffee, so from the start the expectations were set high and I’m happy they did not disappoint.
We had a quick chat with the barista and she kindly explained what makes their special coffee different and I knew I had to try it, if you like pumpkin and feeling relaxed with subtle notes of mushroom that should be your pick. We also tried the the dark nut latte and that was also very nice. For a better reference I have included a one pager of the effect of the super add one they put in the drinks as well, come try it and see if it works for you!
The only average item on the menu was the almond croissant we tried and that was due it being a bit dry and old so my advice is not to get that but the coffee more than made up for the pastry.

reviews.author: Nyx Muse
reviews.text: The café is wonderful! I was on vacation with my family in Toronto, and I went there specifically to try the pistachio cream coffee. My mom and I absolutely loved it! I don’t remember the name of the man who was working there, but he was incredibly kind and polite. I really hope that if I ever come back to Toronto, I can visit this café again. ✨🙏🏻

name: The Theatre Centre Café/Bar
address: 1115 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1J1, Canada
description: The cafe inside the Theatre Centre on Queen West. A creative crowd, good coffee, and a space that feels genuinely different from everything around it.

reviews.author: Explorer O
reviews.text:
One of my favorite hidden spots to work or relax. 😇.
It’s a bit off the beaten path, which keeps it peaceful, and totally worth the trip.
👍The space is cozy and full of natural light, with plenty of outlets for laptops and phones.
Staff are lovely, drinks are solid (especially the hot chocolate — best I’ve had, and under $5 with oat milk!).
Some seating isn’t the most ergonomic, and the music can be a touch loud, but overall it’s a charming, affordable café I’ll keep coming back to.
🤗I definitely recommend it

reviews.author: Elizabeth T.
reviews.text: I came here for a private event and it was nice! The space is cozy and the staff were helpful. I got the vanilla latte with lactose free milk and the spicy tofu sandwich. They were expensive but they tasted so good I’m not complaining :)

reviews.author: Alberto Anez
reviews.text:
What a cozy & beautiful hidden gem. I write this because it deserves it— whilst lowkey selfishly afraid too many people will find it & it’ll get packed.

Plenty of daylight, desks for remote work & fantastic coffee.

Get an americano from James. He’s an absolute legend; serves it right to your table & your preference.

Xanath is lovely. Sets up a dope and welcoming atmosphere.

I found this place a little over a month ago— and have been here 20+ times. That says it.

They have reward system, every 5 coffee’s your 6th is free. Every 10th, you get a free ticket to a theatre show. Pretty sick.

And they got happy hour everyday from 3-6pm too, for any peeps interested ✌🏼

That’s it. Come see it for yourself.

reviews.author: Sophie J
reviews.text:
Lovely café at the Theatre Centre, the team makes the food there.
There are several vegan savoury options which is great. A tofu and pepper sandwich, another tofu sandwich can be made vegan and savoury potato and pea patties with tamarind sauce.

reviews.author: Josh Marchesini
reviews.text: Cozy café on Queen West. They have great coffee (I recommend the iced vanilla latte in the summer!). They also have alcoholic beverages for those who want to chill and vibe. Their food selection is great, they have rotating snacks including muffins, chicken pot pie, sandwiches and their most famous item: their scones! Would order their vegan breakfast sandwich again. Various options for seating (their couch is great for those Instagram posts), free wifi AND they have a Little Free Library so you can read/take/leave as many books as you want. I also like that they’re open 7 days a week (even later when they have shows in the theatre). Will definitely come back.

name: Louie Coffee Shop
address: 1187 King St W, Toronto, ON M6K 3C5, Canada
description: Liberty Village's neighbourhood living room. Louie is friendly, reliable, and the kind of spot locals are glad exists on their block.

reviews.author: Katerina
reviews.text:
Louie is a quaint, very cute, tucked away coffee shop with a warm atmosphere, wonderful staff and a great café.

Food
I went out for some errands but as it’s been very cold these days, I went looking for somewhere to have a rest, a warm drink and catch up on a bit of reading. I found Louie tucked away on the northern boundary of Liberty Village on King Street and ordered their London Fog for $4.75. I’d give it 5/5.

It was great and to top it off, their teabags are sourced from Pluck. This café knows what they are doing. Pluck uses plastic-free tea bags that are compostable, petroleum free and green bin friendly.

Service
I’d give it 5/5 stars.
The staff here are incredibly nice, helpful and warm. Drink arrived quickly, and they also had a washroom with a hook.

Atmosphere
I love it here, 5/5 again. It has this cozy feel, the energy of this place is very relaxed, it’s not loud, and it’s a great place to get comfortable and do a bit of reading or catch-up on a bit of work.

What I absolutely love about places like this in Toronto is the love that they have for our community. Right behind where I was sitting, they have a Free Community DVD Movie Library where you can pick a DVD to borrow and share one of yours. It’s a small but beautiful gesture to see in the community, circulating that that warmth and love in small ways, while also giving the locals a peaceful, unique and nice place to have a warm drink and enjoy a good book.

reviews.author: Sneha Abraham
reviews.text: Tried matcha latte for the first time and it wasn’t a good one sadly ! It’s a cozy place and the staff were friendly too.

reviews.author: Jordan Longmuir
reviews.text:
These guys make the best coffee in liberty village, no doubt. Service is always friendly too, unlike Arvo and Brodflour.

I appreciate that when ordering a larger sized drink, the amount of espresso you get increases, not just the volume of milk/water.

reviews.author: Daniel Francavilla
reviews.text:
A hidden gem. Easily some of the best matcha in the city, using the best alternative milk (Milkadamia Barista Macadamia).

Friendly staff. Great service.

Lots of space to get work done. Big tables with great power outlets and nice window nooks.

reviews.author: Mehak
reviews.text: Great coffee also tried onion pinwheel loved it. Barista was super friendly. Great Service.100% recommend.

name: TonTon Matcha + Coffee
address: 100 Western Battery Rd #1, Toronto, ON M6K 3S2, Canada
description: TonTon is Liberty Village's only dedicated matcha cafe, serving high-quality matcha lattes, espresso, and seasonal drinks in a minimal, well-designed space. Rated 4.6★ with real Google reviews.

reviews.author: Agape Fernandez
reviews.text: Had such a great experience at Tonton. Loved the pandan matcha! As someone who loves matcha, I could tell right away their quality was top tier. Honestly, best matcha I’ve had downtown. Also had the pleasure speaking with the owners mom, who was so lovely & gave great recommendations for the next time! :) will definitely return.

reviews.author: Renata C.
reviews.text:
My fiancé and I got Matchas today from TonTon and we’re both pleasantly surprised!! I’m picky with my Matcha and I was skeptical of the flavours, but the coconut one was such a unique taste!! The girl behind the counter was so cute and friendly!! I think her name was Kim.

100% recommend !!

reviews.author: Emilie Wan
reviews.text: Came here with my boyfriend and tried their most popular drink, the Iced Coconut Pandan Matcha Latte, while he got the Hot Brown Sugar Hojicha Latte. Both were good, but I have to say I liked the Brown Sugar Hojicha Latte more — the taste reminded me of tapioca in bubble tea.

reviews.author: Nguyen Thu
reviews.text:
Hojicha and Matcha is good. But I have to keep going back for the brown sugar pandan matcha and brown sugar Hojicha.

Tip: Order less sugar, you can always add more.

reviews.author: Nell
reviews.text: Very much the best matcha I’ve had in so long. I ordered on uber to work and it took a while and I also forgot it outside (whoops) and somehow it STILL tasted delicious. Matcha is strong, fresh and not grassy or fishy like other lower grade matchas. The coconut pandan one changed my life. Highly recommend.

name: Thindi Cafe
address: 153 E Liberty St, Toronto, ON M6K 3G3, Canada
description: A Liberty Village cafe with South Asian-inspired flavours and a warm, welcoming space. Something genuinely different from the usual coffee shop formula.

reviews.author: Manpreet Singh
reviews.text:
Absolutely blown away by Thindi! 🤍
We had the ghee podi thatte idli and ghee podi masala dosa, and honestly everything was outstanding. The idli and dosa were incredibly soft and literally melted in the mouth, while still having that perfect texture. The taste was fresh, authentic, and so flavourful.

The dosa was perfectly crisp on the outside with generous ghee, the podi had just the right spice, and the masala was rich without being heavy. If you’re a fan of true South Indian food, this is hands down the best dosa place in Toronto and the GTA.

A must visit. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Sayaka Y
reviews.text: Visited this lovely little indie café for the first time today and absolutely loved it! The staff member, Unmana, was so kind and helpful, taking the time to explain everything. I had the masala dosa and chai, and both were so delicious — the dosa was crispy, slightly spicy, and full of flavor. Such a cozy spot with great food and warm service. I left feeling so happy — can’t wait to visit again. ☺️🩵

reviews.author: Tanya Arora
reviews.text:
If you’re in Liberty Village and craving consistently amazing food, Thindi Cafe - Liberty Village is an absolute must-visit. This has easily become one of my go-to spots, and I always find myself coming back.

The food is excellent every single time — flavorful, authentic, and incredibly satisfying. What I especially appreciate is that the dishes are always served hot and fresh, which makes such a difference in the overall experience. You can truly taste the quality and care that goes into every meal.

Whether it’s a quick bite or a relaxed meal, this place never disappoints. The consistency, delicious flavors, and welcoming vibe keep me returning again and again. Highly recommend!

reviews.author: akanksha yeola
reviews.text:
Had the pleasure of serving Thindi Cafe’s Paneer Puffs, Pineapple Grilled Sandwiches, and Green Chutney Sandwiches at my Diwali party for about 55 guests this year, and it was such a hit! Everyone loved how flavourful everything was and how beautifully it was presented, especially for such a large order.

The Paneer Puffs were perfectly crispy yet moist on the inside.

Sandwiches often tend to get soggy, but Thindi Cafe did an incredible job keeping everything fresh, warm, and absolutely delicious.

Great for catering orders, quick and hassle free pick up! Will be getting more items for future events 🫶🪔✨

reviews.author: Sachin Kumar Sherikar
reviews.text:
So glad we stumbled upon this gem while casually strolling near Humber Bay — I only wish I had discovered it earlier!

The food was incredible and honestly took me straight back to Bangalore. 🇮🇳✨

We tried Thate Idli, Bangalore Benne Dosa, and Dalgona Coffee — and every single thing was amazing.
•	The idli was perfectly cooked, with just the right amount of podi and ghee.
•	The Bangalore Benne Dosa with garlic chutney was next-level — super crispy, buttery, and absolutely addictive. Hard to believe I missed this place despite living in the GTA for over 4 years!
•	The surprise highlight of the day was the Dalgona Coffee — rich, frothy, and bursting with that nostalgic Indian coffee flavor.

Highly recommend this spot, especially the Bangalore Benne Dosa and Dalgona Coffee — you won’t regret it!

name: Balzac's Liberty Village
address: 43 Hanna Ave #123, Toronto, ON M6K 1X1, Canada
description: The Liberty Village outpost of Toronto's beloved roaster. Balzac's brings its signature warmth and excellent coffee to the neighbourhood.

reviews.author: Kaitlyn Jung
reviews.text: Coffee is their standard Balzac quality but I care more about the location and if you can actually work there! And yes this place does have an outlet! There aren’t many but there’s at least 1. It’s shockingly underused so there is plenty of room and spaces.

reviews.author: Wes Inaba
reviews.text:
Balzac's Liberty Village is a coffee shop on Hannah and Liberty in Toronto.  The shop itself has a cool darker  ambience...long narrow space.

The service, as noted by others, could be a little more friendly. Just taking orders and ringing them in.

The food and coffee was very good.

4 x ☕☕☕☕

reviews.author: Naya Snow
reviews.text:
Drink Quality: 3.0/5
Drink Selection: 4.1/5
Pastry Quality: N/A
Pastry Selection: 4.0/5
Price: 3.3/5
Ambiance: 3.3/5
Service: 3.0/5
Cleanliness: 3.5/5
Location: 4.0/5

A 16oz iced caramel latte costs $7.40 (tax included, no tip), which is on the pricier side. Drink quality at this location is inconsistent and seems to depend heavily on the barista's skill. I've tried the iced vanilla latte four times here--two were excellent, and two were disappointing.

The inconsistency appears to stem from differences in drink preparation. One barista mixed the vanilla syrup with the espresso before adding it to the iced milk, resulting in a well-balanced drink. The other added syrup directly to the iced milk, which prevented it from fully dissolving and led to a weak, unsweetened taste.

Unfortunately, this location doesn't offer a patio, despite the outdoor space being well-suited for one. The interior is narrow, dimly lit, and awkwardly arranged. Seating consists of stools lined up against high-set windows, limiting both natural light and any real view outside.

Service tends to be quick but impersonal--more of a transactional experience than a welcoming one

reviews.author: Ata
reviews.text: great coffee! the customer service could be more welcoming, simple gestures like saying hello, thank you, and being a bit more patient would make the experience even better.

reviews.author: GS MANAGE
reviews.text: On the hunt for best mocha on the city my journey brings me to fine location , the form right away hit me and it was really creamy a perfect fit for the authentic mocha so far its up there

name: Liberty Village Market & Cafe
address: 65 Jefferson Ave, Toronto, ON M6K 1X8, Canada
description: A neighbourhood staple in Liberty Village with a market layout and a loyal local crowd. Good coffee, good energy, and usually a seat to be found.

reviews.author: Irena S
reviews.text: Cozy. Warm. Tasty. What more could you ask for? I love their soup selection and the service is good. Feels very cozy and comforting.

reviews.author: Neg
reviews.text: Food is delicious and comforting, the vegan lentil soup is amazing. Prices are not necessarily cheap, but given the portions and the fact that you're eating out in Toronto, still pretty good. Service is fast and friendly. This place is so cozy, it feels like eating at home or a friend's!

reviews.author: Connor Brunt
reviews.text:
One of the few remaining down to earth spots in LV, everything in here feels like a classic Toronto hole in the wall. Wouldn't say the prices are cheap, but I imagine commercial rent here isn't either.

Bagels could be a little higher quality for what you're paying, but overall nice place to grab a bite on a night out. Certainly beats the prices at whatever venue you're headed to.

reviews.author: Noor Sa
reviews.text:
I ordered on Uber eat this morning.  This was absolutely delicious ! I wasn’t feeling well I just wanted a yummy soup and sandwich. This was so fresh, comforting, soothing, and deliciously filling. Good quantity. I have some soup left over for later. Incredible!  I got egg Sandwich pumpernickel bread & toppings I chose, we’re loaded. The tomato soup…. Just WOW that’s going on my fave list.
It’s also the small touches for me, writing “enjoy” on the bag. For some of us… it means something.  Thank you. I’ll be visiting soon, and ordering again.

reviews.author: Fam90 Records
reviews.text: This place is trash. I ordered a blt and it looks like the bacon is old. I even called the store to complain and the manager Cirus did not care about the poor quality of their food. Don’t go here find somewhere better

name: Highly Likely Coffee
address: 1189 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6J 1J4, Canada
description: Liberty Village's most optimistic cafe. Highly Likely earns its name with consistently good coffee and a space that's easy to spend time in.

reviews.author: Ritwika
reviews.text:
This place is such a hidden gem!

It’s an Indonesian cafe and the decor is like a breath of fresh air. Very pleasing and calm. Staff is very friendly and will explain you everything in detail if you have questions about the menu.

I tried their barong dance - an Indonesian coffee flight that was a lot like a cortado. Great for people who like their coffee strong.

Also got their chocolate chip cookie which was delicious! Just the right amount of sweetness. And finally their pandan croissant which was filled with pandan cream and tasted so good. The croissant itself is fresh and flaky and goes very well with the pandan cream inside.

We also got their vanilla latte and it was good too - just nothing exceptional.

If you’re in the area, do pay them a visit, you won’t regret it.

reviews.author: Amir Moghisy
reviews.text: The most amazing and unique coffee you can find around. I was speechless and started writing this review after the first sip, ok so our orders were baron dance (exceptional choice if you love espresso) and the original Bajigur which has coffee inside of it. If you truly love coffee then Indonesian coffee is a no brainer but above all their execution and price points take this even further. If you are in doubt and don’t know which cafe is one of the best in town, look no further.

reviews.author: Zai R
reviews.text:
This is also another cafe that's been on my bucket list, and I think I have to rate this as one of my top cafes to go to! It's an Indonesian Cafe (halal too), and has a beautifully crafted interior!

We ordered the: Latte, Pandan Croissant (the best croissant I've tasted), Decaf Oat Latte (finally a Decaf that actually tastes like regular coffee!!!), and chocolate chip cookie (soft and chewy).

Customer service was amazing, they walked us through the different coffee brews and their top selling items.

Would recommend everyone to come visit, we went for dine-in, but ended up taking another latte to go!

reviews.author: Christian Felix Nugraha H.
reviews.text:
Great coffee shop, great atmosphere, great friendly staff.

I tried their signature Indonesian pour over and barong dance. This coffee shop has a goal to be specialized in Indonesian coffee varieties.

I also bought their pandan cream croissant, which I loved it.. the creamy texture of pandan cream combined with crispiness of croissant, perfect!!!

reviews.author: Oksana Gryvnak
reviews.text:
I can’t believe there is a place in Toronto taking me back in time to Bali… Not only with the atmosphere of the temple blended with modern architecture but with outstanding coffee from Indonesia.
I choose to drink coffee rarely but quality and taste is what matters most when I decide to treat myself. You have to try it! You’ll save on travel cost;)

name: Aroma Espresso Bar
address: 120 Lynn Williams St #101, Toronto, ON M6K 3N6, Canada
description: A Liberty Village classic with a broad menu and a comfortable, lived-in atmosphere. Aroma is reliable, familiar, and always busy for good reason.

reviews.author: Discovered Souls
reviews.text:
🌿 A Guided Stop in Liberty Village

We set out on a little adventure — one not led by plans, but by intuition. We chose to let God be in the driver’s seat and simply follow the nudge, trusting the journey to unfold. That’s how we found ourselves getting off the train one stop earlier at Exhibition, strolling into Liberty Village, and making our first stop at Aroma Espresso Bar. And are we ever glad we did.

Super cozy and nestled right in the heart of the Village, Aroma felt like the epicentre of good energy. The smell of fresh coffee filled our lungs, wrapping us in warmth. There was a tangible sense of expansion — that quiet joy that arises when you follow divine guidance and end up exactly where you’re meant to be.

Although there were no outlets to charge our devices, we easily found a comfortable spot to settle in and do our “work,” accompanied by delicious food and perfectly brewed coffee.

But honestly, the menu details aren’t the highlight here. What stood out was the atmosphere: expansive, alive, and quietly affirming. A reminder that when you let go of control, life often meets you with unexpected goodness.

✨ We’ll definitely be back!

— Alex & Sackeria
✨ Discovered Souls

reviews.author: ana maria Mejia
reviews.text:
Aroma Liberty Village is just perfect. The moment you walk in, you’re greeted with warm smiles and a welcoming atmosphere that feels just like home. Perfect spot for catching up with friends, working on your laptop, or just enjoying some quiet time
The quality of the food is top-notch — everything tastes fresh and delicious.  The kale avocado power is sublime! Their salads and bowls stand out from the crowd! And the pastries are sooo tasty.
Most importantly, the staff and managements are incredible!
Highly recommend this coffee shop!

reviews.author: Nilibeth Sanchez
reviews.text: Great place to have breakfast, its just simple and tasty! Nothing too elaborated, nice flavor and options, tried the egg, cheese and turkey bacon croissant and really liked it, would have had a second 😂 not too big if you are hungry! Also aroma breakfast and it was good, capuccino is also decent and tasty!

reviews.author: Anastasiia Sukhovetska
reviews.text: I really enjoy the croissants here 🤤 especially the ones with salmon, tuna, or chicken. They’re fresh, delicious, and definitely worth trying. The coffee isn’t always perfect, but it’s fine )) I’d still come back just for the croissants.

reviews.author: Amrita Singh
reviews.text:
Ordered a coffee and grilled chicken and avocado sandwich today. Opens the sandwitch, took a bite and inside the wrapper of the sandwich cover was a green insect tucked in there.
Callled uber eats, called the restaurant - a back of the house worker by the name is Bouker said “no worries, I can send you another, next he said, are you sure that’s not Arugula?” And next he says there was no issue when he sent it out.
No accounatability taken. None at all. So appalling.
I think I understand the difference between an insect and vegetables, not sure the staff at the restaurant does though. Don’t trust this kitchen
Uber of course doesn’t care

name: Bold Brew Coffee
address: 219 Dufferin St #105B, Toronto, ON M6K 3J1, Canada
description: Liberty Village's specialty-forward option. Bold Brew sources carefully and brews with intention — a strong choice if you want more than a standard latte.

reviews.author: Sandi Cole
reviews.text:
Smashing IT! 🥳☕️🥳

Bold Brew Coffee as the name implies is serving up some of the most delicious cups of coffee, teas and delectable breakfast and afternoon sandwiches that help to bring an extra joy to your busy life.

Nestled in the heart of Dufferin Street just feet away from Liberty Village within a cozy historical building is where you will find the great masters of coffee and tea of BOLD BREW COFFEE!

So whether you're starting a day, needing a break or just a passer byer make sure you drop into Bold Brew Coffee, have a seat and enjoy.

Love my LONDON FOG!

reviews.author: ERIC LALONDE
reviews.text: Very rude employees. Also I ordered a turkey avocado bacon wrap. They did put bacon or avocado and only put half of the toppings I asked for. Would not recommend.

reviews.author: Kayla Duplantis
reviews.text: Best chicken pesto wrap/sandwich. High quality ingredients and great bang for your buck.

reviews.author: Kate Smith
reviews.text: My Go-to breakfast and lunch spot in Toronto. The owners are super friendly, the food is delicious and fresh, and the coffee is perfect! Love everything about this shop!

reviews.author: Ronan Garrison
reviews.text: Ash and Hamid are two of the nicest people I’ve met. They serve great food and their hospitality is beyond amazing. Unfortunately I’ve moved away from Toronto, but I will miss this great little deli!

name: The Common
address: 36 Lisgar St, Toronto, ON M6J 0A9, Canada
description: A Liberty Village cafe that functions as the neighbourhood's living room. The Common is relaxed, consistent, and good for staying a while.

reviews.author: aheffner89
reviews.text: Stopped here for the first time.  The barista, Star, was extremely pleasant and helpful.  Shr made me feel welcomed, and even gave me a suggestion to throw the ham and cheese croissant in the oven for a few minutes if I really wanted to make it pop.  I did as suggested, and it made for a warm, flaky, delicious snack paired with a great cappuccino.  Will go out of my way to stop by here again.

reviews.author: William Khalil
reviews.text: 2022.02.06 Coffee, croissant, and scones to go. The Hot Americano was very good as well as the scones. The choice was Apple and Blueberry scones. They have multiple places across the city.

reviews.author: Rita Barbosa
reviews.text: Such a great place to pick up a coffee! The barista was so friendly and recommended a scone, which they have only on weekends. I got the apple scone, it was so fresh and so good!

reviews.author: Abdulkhader
reviews.text:
A cozy little window serving the best coffee at Abell street.
Loved the coffee, although I’m yet to explore their menu.
Will update accordingly.

reviews.author: Игорь Максименко
reviews.text: It’s feels like -7 today , ordered 2 cappuccino, took5-6 minutes to make it + she don’t know where sugar is , + when I take it , it wasn’t  even hot , I live in the same building. I got home and coffee like it was made 20 minutes ago , almost cold .

name: Larry's Place
address: 1390 Queen St W, Toronto, ON M6K 1L7, Canada
description: A Liberty Village spot with a personal feel and a loyal local following. Larry's is the kind of cafe that makes a neighbourhood feel like a community.

reviews.author: Gabi G
reviews.text:
What a wonderful place! I’m a true coffee addicted and I love going to different coffee shops every week. I was super excited about going to Larry’s after reading the reviews and boy I am so glad I did.

☕️ Coffee: I tried their special Maple Latte and it was AMAZING. Usually I only order Americano or a regular Latte because I hate when the coffee is filled with syrups that make it too sweet, but this one was fantastic.

🥐 Food: Butter tart is not my first choice of baked goods, but I had to try it after reading the comments and OMG I might start ordering butter tarts from now on. The best one I’ve ever eaten. I’ll be dreaming about it. I didn’t try their croissants, so I definitely need to go back because they looked delicious.

👩‍🍳Service: The baristas were very kind and working super fast to make sure everyone was happy and satisfied.

✨ Vibes: it is a very cozy place, I only wish they had more seats available, that’s the only downside. I would have loved to stay there and enjoyed my coffee and butter tart, but it was very busy, as expected for a Sunday morning.

reviews.author: JT Interior Construction Co.
reviews.text:
Larry’s Place is one of those gems you’re happy to discover. The moment you walk in, the atmosphere feels warm and inviting, with a cozy setup that makes you want to stay a while. The staff are friendly and attentive, always making sure you feel taken care of without being overbearing.
The highlight for me is definitely the coffee—it’s amazing! You can tell they take pride in how they brew it. The flavor is rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced, easily one of the best cups I’ve had in a long time. It’s the kind of coffee you look forward to, whether it’s your morning boost or an afternoon pick-me-up.
On top of that, the food is fresh and flavorful, with generous portions that feel like real value for money. Everything we tried tasted homemade and carefully prepared.
Larry’s Place is more than just a café—it’s the kind of spot that makes you want to come back again and again, whether for a coffee break, a casual meal, or to spend some time with friends. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Buck
reviews.text: Larry's place is a warm, clean, cozy spot and everything you need if you want a quick bite to eat or want to sit in and do work. Baristas were warm and welcoming. I had the ginger, lemon, cinnamon hot drink especially on this cold, icy, snowy day which helps warm the body. If you are in the parksdale neighborhood you have to try it.

reviews.author: Alex Redinger
reviews.text:
I'm searching for the best butter tart in Ontario - see some of my previous reviews for other options. One name that repeatedly came up was Larry's Place, so this weekend I visited to check them out.

First, it's worth noting that Larry's Place is a great coffee shop. There's a fun vibe, the place is clearly popular in the neighbourhood, and the barista was very friendly. I would gladly return for a coffee in the future.

But my visit was strictly for business: I needed to taste their butter tart. I'm pleased to confirm that they have an excellent iteration, among the best I've tried.

The crust is an interesting mix between flaky and soft, whereas most butter tarts tend to be one or the other. It's sort of the best of both worlds, and with a pleasant sticky texture, too, from the filling caramelizing against the tart.

In terms of the filling, the tarts are not particularly stuffed. Based on comments I've heard from friends and family, I would view this as a bonus: it seems that most people don't want a deluge of the filling. The texture of the filling is also pleasing: it's gooey, but not too liquidy.

Overall, Larry's Place is a strong contender among the best butter tarts in the province. It's definitely worth checking out - and maybe grab a coffee while you're there!

reviews.author: Jacquie Y
reviews.text: I love Larry's. The service is exceptional. Everyone is always so friendly. They're baked goods are really good especially if you go in the morning when the cheese croissant is still warm it is out of this world. And I love their walnut croissants with the various fruit pairings. It can be hard to find seating because the secret is out about this gem of a place.

name: Found Coffee | Bellwoods
address: 189 Shaw St, Toronto, ON M6J 2W7, Canada
description: A Bellwoods-adjacent spot worth seeking out. Found Coffee is exactly that -- a good thing to stumble across, and even better to return to.

reviews.author: Heidi Mittal
reviews.text: Found is a place you feel welcomed, heard, and truly appreciated. You might not realize the impact a kind stranger has on you, and part of the act of self-care might be indulging in a handcrafted beverage and scone to tackle the hard days. Jadyn, Adrian, and the entire team create such a warm atmosphere, that as a local tennis coach and non-profit girl, they may not realize makes such a difference brightening my day! I’ve spent a few mornings there regularly after dropping off the kiddos and teaching, and I’ve been astounded (not surprised) and how consistently intentional the whole team is about caring for the community. I can only imagine how that positive culture spreads across all locations. Places like Found are safe spaces and hubs for the community for a reason - and I’m always going to ride for that breakfast spread, hot chai cider, and charming little chats with my favourite crew!!

reviews.author: Mari Loewen
reviews.text: Love this lovely place I stumbled upon with my family. Gorgeous brand that flows through everything, and best cappuccino and great breakfast/lunch toasts. We had the avocado and smoked salmon toasts. Unexpected and Excellent spot!

reviews.author: Anastasiia Sukhovetska
reviews.text:
This is a really great coffee shop! 😍😍

The patio is incredibly atmospheric, making you want to sit there in any weather.

The staff is super friendly and welcoming, always greeting guests with a smile.

The interior is beautiful, with every detail carefully thought out—from the tableware to the decor, creating a cozy and stylish vibe.

The coffee is absolutely delicious, probably one of the best in the area🤌🏻🤌🏻🤌🏻I really enjoyed the avocado toast — fresh and rich in flavor, with a perfect combination of ingredients.

This is the kind of place you want to keep coming back to again and again. Definitely one of the best coffee shops around!!!!!!🤍🤍

reviews.author: Wishmae A
reviews.text: I’m always looking forward to my trips to this coffee shop because of how consistent they make the drinks, it’s just so good! I always order the mocha or iced oat milk latte (sometimes both!) and cheesymite scroll! The staff are always so kind and helpful - they really know their coffee!

reviews.author: Jo Collins
reviews.text: Fantastic coffee! My partner and I were visiting Toronto from London UK, we really enjoy coffee and did some research before our trip on potential spots to hit up & one of our results was Found. We visited the Bellwoods branch on our first day as we needed caffeine for our jet lag. The coffee was amazing, really smooth and flavourful. During our stay we visited 2 other branches and the coffee was consistent and delicious across all spots. Staff were so friendly and helpful. Also really knowledgeable on their coffee. If you like coffee and are in Toronto definitely check them out.

name: Milky's Cloud Room
address: 28 Bathurst St #1107, Toronto, ON M5V 0R4, Canada
description: A dreamy Liberty Village cafe that takes its aesthetic as seriously as its coffee. Milky's is a genuinely fun place to spend an afternoon.

reviews.author: salve mindanao
reviews.text: The drinks were well made and the atmosphere is simple & peaceful. Although, the place needs to be more tidy because I empty milk box is really visible to customers. It looks unpleasant. The guy who took are order as well is not the most hospitable as he could not explain a promotion for valentine that we inquired about. Also, we could not even them when they called for our order because the music is too loud.

reviews.author: Michael Viveros
reviews.text: I have become a regular here, partly due to the coffee, partly due to the vibe. Hideki made my decaf oat cortado this morning and it was delightful - smooth, creamy with a hint of bitterness so that you could still taste the coffee. The vibe is chill, minimal, unique. A good third place.

reviews.author: Anaëlle Molinet
reviews.text:
We took a matcha and a Abanaurnaur. The Abanaurnaur was too sweet and simply like a latte but with too much sugar. I really like their matcha with oat milk or almond milk without any sweet. Comparing to Matcha haus, it’s better in Milky’s.
You will not find much places and it’s bad. It’s not comfortable at all to seat there.
I have been there three times and the personal wasn’t good-natured. If you go there, don’t expect any smile neither welcoming. Expect one time when I ordered the wrong drink, the employee gave me a free pastry because I had just paid 2$ more 👍🏻

reviews.author: Daniel Hvr
reviews.text:
My go to place in the area!
Great atmosphere, good coffee and snacks.
Hideki is always welcoming on weekends and impressing with latte art!
On weekdays, (don’t know the name of the guy) is great barista and can customize drinks

reviews.author: Miguel Santos
reviews.text:
I absolutely love the vibe of this cafe and their staff!! Super chill environment + kind staff!

Love their drinks also, definitely worth passing by if you’re in the area!

I want to send my thanks to Hideki for this amazing latte art!

name: The Abbott
address: 99 Spencer Ave, Toronto, ON M6K 1G6, Canada
description: A solid Liberty Village option with a clean, modern feel and a coffee program that keeps the neighbourhood well-caffeinated. Dependable and good.

reviews.author: Tyrone
reviews.text:
The Reality of "Neighborhood" Service: I lived just two streets away from this shop for many years. I was a regular, a neighbor, and a consistent supporter. But after my experience here recently, I will never return.

The Experience: I came here after being discharged from the hospital, looking for a moment of stability. I was visibly in distress, nearly in tears, and just needed to charge my phone so I could call a taxi home. I purchased my tea and sat quietly, trying to ground myself.

The Betrayal: Despite my history as a local and being a paying customer, the staff member on shift spent the duration of my visit on her phone, speaking about me as if I didn’t understand her. When a police officer coincidentally entered to use the washroom, she attempted to weaponize his presence by telling him I was being "disruptive"—despite the fact that I hadn't moved, spoken, or even used the facilities.

The Verdict: Even the officer saw through the situation and was kind to me, but the damage was done. To be treated with such calculated cruelty by a shop I supported for years—while in a vulnerable state—is a disgrace to the Parkdale community spirit. There are plenty of other spots in the neighborhood that actually value human decency. This is no longer one of them.

reviews.author: M T
reviews.text: Cute local coffee shop in Parkdale. Has a few tables and outdoor red benches for a hot day. Rustic decor with open brick. Old hard cover books and coffee paraphernalia  decorates the bookshelves. Two leaves and a bud is the tea brand. Organic coffee beans. Had a latte which was smooth, perhaps I would ask for more espresso shots next time because I like my latte strong. Also had a orange pecan cookie which was delicious, sweet but not too sweet and had the texture of a shortbread cookie. Staff were very friendly and even offered to replace a spilled coffee for minimal cost. Very reasonable priced. Latte for under $5! Overall: Nice place to try locally with a homey feel. Probably really 4.5*

reviews.author: Angie h
reviews.text: My cappuccino was terrible (was more like a latte in a large cup) and found the server to be rude. Space is far from cozy (would say kinda danky ) not a great place to work from. If you get the green tea latte I’d reco getting half or no sweetener. Only has oat milk and not the barista kind, aka not friendly for those that are dairy free. Won’t be making a trip back any time soon.

reviews.author: Amy
reviews.text: Love the coffee here it is an awesome cup of coffee. The baked goods are delicious. The staff are amazing, like catching up with friends. The doggies can get a free treat too.

reviews.author: Arcee
reviews.text:
I really like this cafe and wish I could go here. But they’re discriminatory towards brown people. It’s not one person and not the first time I had that feeling. They do it very subtly so it’s not very apparent or in your face. Maybe they don’t get a lot of brown people at their cafe. But they will ask everyone else how they’re doing but won’t ask you, or reply to your questions freely if you ask how they are they’ll skip over small talk like talking to a wall - they’re visibly uncomfortable with my presence as a brown person. They got my order wrong and I didn’t want to say anything to them because I didn’t want to talk to the person serving me at the counter, given they were already uncomfortable serving me. This time it was a male person whom I’d not seen before. Earlier it was a lady. (PS: I went there a few times over the last 4
years each time I go I remember why I stopped going)

Only 1 girl is non biased there.
I live in the next street yet I don’t prefer to stop by here because of how they make you feel, so I go to another cafe that’s farther away.

I wish you served everybody equally and attempt to connect regardless of my color or how I look.

PS: just looked at their reviews not 1 brown person reviewed it so makes sense they don’t see a lot of people who look like me, yet it would be nice to be seen as a human and not a visible minority.

name: Project Seoul
address: 355 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2G3, Canada
description: A Chinatown cafe with Korean-inspired touches and a thoughtful approach to both coffee and the space itself. Worth a detour.

reviews.author: Angela A.
reviews.text:
It’s surprisingly hard to find a café that’s open past 5pm, still serves caffeinated drinks and desserts, and has enough space to sit comfortably with a date or friends. That’s why I was so happy to discover this place—it checks all the boxes.

You don’t realize how big it is until you head upstairs. The second floor runs almost the entire length of the building, so there’s plenty of seating, which makes it great for longer catch-ups or relaxed evenings.

They offer a wide variety of hot and cold coffee drinks, and even though we went later in the evening, there were still a few pastries available, along with bingsoo, which was a nice surprise. The crowd was a mix of people working on laptops, couples on dates, small meetings, and others stopping in for a coffee or dessert after dinner.

Service is simple and efficient—you place your order, receive a buzzer, and when it vibrates you pick up your drinks and desserts at the counter.

Overall, it’s a great late-night café option with lots of space and a relaxed atmosphere. Definitely a good spot if you’re looking for coffee and dessert in the evening.

reviews.author: Sally N
reviews.text:
Cute cafe that’s light and airy, has lots of seating and interesting drinks!

I’ve tried the iced einspanner and sweet corn latte here. Both good but neither stood out. The Einspanner is a nice and strong coffee so I’d recommend that on if you’re after caffeine.

However, I think their matcha drinks are superb!!! The blueberry, strawberry and banana matcha drinks were all amazing 😻

reviews.author: Ssina
reviews.text: The cafe is bigger on the inside than I expected, with plenty of space. I noticed a lot of people studying or working on their laptops. The Basque cheesecake was okay, but nothing special (tastes like what you'd get at a store). Overall, a good spot if you need a place to sit, but the food is average.

reviews.author: Rica Camat
reviews.text:
We stopped by Project Seoul on Spadina and tried the strawberry matcha and lavender matcha, and both were so good! The drinks were super aesthetic — the kind you have to take a photo of — and the flavours were really well-balanced.

The strawberry matcha was refreshing and slightly sweet, with that perfect mix of fruity and earthy matcha taste. The lavender matcha, on the other hand, was smooth, floral, and calming — such a nice twist on the usual matcha latte. You can tell they use good-quality matcha and put care into how they make everything.

The café itself is really cute and cozy, with clean minimalist décor and that soft K-café vibe. The staff were friendly, and the atmosphere made it such a relaxing stop.

Definitely one of my favourite matcha spots in Toronto so far — can’t wait to go back and try more flavours! 🍓💜🍵

reviews.author: Emily Lashkhia
reviews.text:
I loooove this place. As many other reviews say, it’s a very popular place to study. There are a lot of places to sit, but a limited amount of chargers. They also have a time limit on the weekends I believe for how long you can stay seated. It’s good for both solo studying as well as with friends.

But, as a popular spot, it fills up in the afternoon, so get there early, especially for more than 2 people! They also have a pretty strict no outside drinks/food, even if you buy something from there, so be careful about that.

I don’t think anywhere else does the Matcha like they do. They create a foam using the Matcha, which eventually blends into the drink. It’s extremely tasty and feels light. I normally go with the mango Matcha, I highly recommend it! Their cakes are soft and tasty as well, I recommend the blueberry one.

I’ll definitely be back- thank you for having me! :)

name: NEO COFFEE BAR KING X SPADINA
address: 61 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5V 1K1, Canada
description: The King x Spadina outpost of Toronto's favourite specialty bar. NEO delivers the same high standards in a great corner location.

reviews.author: Selina Duong
reviews.text:
Such a cozy spot, especially in the evening! One of my favourite cafes in Toronto and I always find myself coming back. The staff are super friendly, the vanilla latte was great, and the matcha tastes really high quality 🍵

They also have live music on Friday and Saturday evenings!

reviews.author: Uly Wolters
reviews.text:
A great place to relax and soak up some ‘urban city life’.

NEO serves a large varieties of coffee and matcha drinks. Hot and cold. Really good foam and good coffee.

The interior is very modern and bright, with lots of wood and dark stone, complemented by concrete elements.
The overall atmosphere feels airy and full of light, with great indirect lighting. Service is extremely fast and very friendly.

What I especially liked: many small details, lots of single- tables, combined with large community tables in the center.

reviews.author: Mel Do
reviews.text:
Fantastic espresso here.  I purchased 2 Cortado’s with 2% organic milk. They were delicious.  I got one that was big ass and one regular. Both were amazing. The decaf was not quite as strong flavor as the regular, which is pretty typical, but it was still delicious and smooth.
The presentation was perfect. The barista complained he didn’t do a very nice heart, but I was being complicated on how much milk I wanted in my Cortado. I wanted less than typical & I told him midstream.  Cashier and barista were very personable and friendly. They were welcome to my wanting altered a little bit and not being a typical Cortado. wonderful and jazz music in the background. They have a lot of individual seats, which is nice along with giant tables for groups.

reviews.author: Daria Kurovskaya
reviews.text:
Recently moved to the neighborhood and decided to give this place a try, but I left pretty underwhelmed. The space is large, but it doesn’t feel cozy. What really put me off was the staff. There was no greeting, no eye contact, and the overall vibe just felt off and unwelcoming. My cappuccino was below average and not something I’d come back for. The croissant was also not very tasty. The tables are also quite small and not very comfortable to sit at for long.
Overall, this won’t be my first choice for a morning coffee.

reviews.author: DI
reviews.text:
Neo coffee is great! I love the vibe, they have good coffee & matcha as well as alcoholic drinks! good spot to study/get work done & also good spot to chat. No laptops after 5 on weekdays.

They have some decent GF options for baked goods as well.

name: Cafe La_On
address: 453 Dundas St W Main Floor, Toronto, ON M5T 1G8, Canada
description: Cafe La_On is consistently one of Chinatown's highest-rated cafes, with a warm atmosphere, standout specialty coffee, and a loyal following that returns daily. Rated 4.7★ with 168 reviews.

reviews.author: Ivy_E
reviews.text:
Went here last week with a friend, she recommended this cafe after having brunch around the area. The space is pretty roomy, with a few seats near the entrance and several tables in the back, so it didn’t feel cramped at all. The drinks were really good, and the desserts were too! The dessert I got was very sweet but goes well with the bitter drinks!

My friend ordered a matcha, and I got a coffee drink (lol I forgot the name), but I loved it. I’m usually not a big foam person, but this one was perfect. I was worried it might be too sweet or too bitter, but the bottom was slightly bitter while the foam was sweet, and they balanced each other so well without mixing it! I’d definitely come back and order the same drink again.

reviews.author: Kasemsit “KZ” Watt
reviews.text:
Matcha drink was lighter than expected, but nicely balanced.
Cream foam on top was smooth and creamy, with hot matcha underneath that stayed well rounded and comforting.
The real star, though, was the cake(Matcha terrine).
Dense, rich, and deeply matcha-forward  bold, intense, and incredibly satisfying.
10/10. Finished every bite.
Highly recommended, especially for the matcha cake.
The café itself is quiet and calm, though a bit too dim for me.
Cozy, but slightly sleep-inducing perfect for those who enjoy a peaceful, low-key atmosphere.

reviews.author: Tran Nguyen
reviews.text:
The menu at La_On is fantastic, but the service experience was a bit stressful. I ordered a matcha latte with oat milk, a Nutty Coffee (which had a really nice foam and was very easy to drink), and the Grey It Matcha. I absolutely loved the Earl Grey foam on the Grey It Matcha—it’s a standout! For dessert, the tiramisu is highly recommended; it’s light and not too sweet.
Unfortunately, the barista/cashier was very impatient. Despite there being no long line, he kept rushing me and asking “That’s it?” every time I tried to add another item to my order. It’s a shame because the quality of the drinks is 5-star, but the hospitality made me feel like an inconvenience.

reviews.author: Peyun K
reviews.text:
My friend and I both really enjoyed this café! We shared three desserts (earl grey terrine, sesame Basque cheesecake, and original tiramisu ball) and each got a matcha cream latte.
We loved everything. The terrine was a bit sweet for my taste but I loved the strong earl grey flavor. My friend had previously said she didn't think she liked matcha, but has a completely different view on it after trying the latte here!
Also feel like the prices are very reasonable for the level of quality. Definitely recommend.

reviews.author: Tiffany Nguyen
reviews.text: Cafe La_On is one of my favourite cafes in Toronto! It's cozy, welcoming, & perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends. The drinks were delicious & well made, and the desserts were just as good. The staff were friendly & attentive, too, which made the experience even better. My friends & I will definitely be back! (:

name: Dark Horse Espresso Bar
address: 215 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2C7, Canada
description: A Toronto institution with real staying power. Dark Horse in Chinatown is a reliable, atmospheric spot that earns its reputation every single day.

reviews.author: Sebastian Botero
reviews.text:
I’ve visited this Dark Horse Espresso Bar twice, and it’s quickly becoming one of my favorite spots for a quiet break. It’s a great place to work or study, they have plenty of tables and even a cozy space at the back that feels a bit more private. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, with beautiful chandeliers adding a nice touch of charm.

They also have a small market section where you can pick up chips, wine, and other goodies, plus some merchandise. The coffee is high quality, I really enjoy the flavor and consistency here.

One area for improvement would be the pastries. It would be nice if they could warm up or toast items like croissants, as that would make them even more enjoyable. That said, the overall food and drink experience is still very good.

The staff is kind and professional, some are a bit more serious, but they do make an effort to provide good service. The washroom requires a bit of a “mission” to get to (you’ll need a key and a short walk), but it’s not a big deal once you know the process.

Prices are standard for Toronto coffee shops. Cleanliness is generally fine, though sometimes tables take a while to get cleared, especially if people leave without tidying up.

Overall, I recommend visiting. I’m giving it 5 stars because the experience is good and consistent, and I hope the small areas for improvement will be considered.

reviews.author: Adam Sly
reviews.text:
I came to Dark Horse many moons ago and found myself here again this morning. It opens at 7am some days, which makes it one of the better options for early morning.

It’s a bright open cafe with big windows and clean, comfortable tables to bust out your laptop and do a few things. Coffee is good and they have a bunch of other stuff for sale too - which I didn’t look at but may be appealing to someone else - such as pastries, sandwiches, snacks, wine and coffee/tea supplies.

Also to note, the cashier was very friendly and welcoming.
