reviews.author: Jiwon Chun
reviews.text:
I have been going to Dark Horse on Spadina for many years now, for about a decade, and as I was seeing recent reviews on decline in the quality of service, I doubted so and told those around me that it is still a good cafe to go. The last few visits were fine, as well, except for the last visit on January 21, after which I will likely not return to the cafe again, and the below is the reason why.

I have a ~14lbs dog (not a service dog), and I took him to Dark Horse on Spadina about 2 times before this last visit and sat in and dined. What I do as a non-service dog owner when I visit cafes in Toronto with my dog is first check if there is a sign on the door that explicitly bars animals besides service animals and if not, then ask the staff if they are allowed in (whether it is for take out or sit in, whatever the situation is at that time). The first time I brought my dog to this cafe, I asked the staff, and they said it was fine to bring the dog. In fact, there was another dog when I sat in one of the tables, not sure if this was a service dog or not, but did not have any visible tag of it being a service dog in any event.

This time, I ordered food and drink, went to the table to leave my belongings and came down to grab the sandwhich I ordered that was being heated up. Then, a person claiming to be the manager of the cafe who was not there before asked "is that a service dog?" I looked at the barista, looked at her, and said "no," and she asked the same question again for some reason, and I replied "I said no." She proceeded to explain that they are going to be in trouble if a health inspector comes in and sees a dog, so I asked then if she was asking me to leave, and she said no, you already ordered food and what not, but "I am just saying if someone comes and inspects, it will be on us." There was some discussion about how there are indeed cafes that are dog-friendly and you can dine in, and she kept asking if that is true, that sure, for a take out but not for dine in. It was not in her place to doubt me on this point, and what even was the point on disputing this with me in any event? I ended up leaving voluntarily and said, as a lawyer myself, I suggest they have better protocols and signs for the cafe because this could be a ground for dispute for someone who may not want to leave voluntarily, unlike myself.

For a manager, I did not find her to communicate well, because what is the point of complaining to a customer that they may be responsible for food safety violations and then still say, as if they are doing the customer a favour, that the customer can stay despite all of what she has already said? I would think it should be either don't say it at all or just tell the customer to leave.

I always found the staff to be otherwise attentive and cordial, but if she was indeed a manager, this manager has ruined the reputation of the cafe for me that all other staff there that I had interacted with over the last decade built, so that is unfortunate. Especially now that I am a dog owner, I respect if a cafe is not dog-friendly, then I just go to these places when I am not with my dog, but the way this manager handled things is not the way to communicate their policy to a customer, which was equivocal at best.

The attached photo is from Jan 7 when I brought the dog to the cafe and dined in.

reviews.author: Ignazio Johnnatan Ibarra Juárez
reviews.text: Lovely cafe! Lots of space and natural light. They have two levels for sitting. Staff is friendly. Enjoy their coffee and delicious pastries.

reviews.author: Pablo Rodas
reviews.text: Had a long Monday morning and wasn’t able to have breakfast, walking on the street I found Dark Horse just looking for a coffee and quick bite and was greeted by Dua and her energy is amazing. Such a friendly vibe and recommended one of her favourite coffees and pastries. Made my day better, coming back for sure!

name: 10 DEAN | Waverley
address: The Lobby Of The Waverley, Apartment Bldg, 484 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5S 0E4, Canada
description: A Chinatown gem at the corner of Dean and Waverley. Small, focused, and one of those spots that feels like a genuine neighbourhood discovery.

reviews.author: Andrew B
reviews.text:
We tried the Whisk It Up matcha making class. It was super interesting and informative! We sampled culinary, latte, and ceremonial grade matcha! At the end of the class, we made our own strawberry and ube matcha lattes! Chelsea was very knowledgable and passionate about matcha and the process!

We booked a couple of weeks in advanced through their website. Highly recommend if you enjoy matcha. The cafe itself has a very nice atmosphere with lots of variety in their menu!

reviews.author: Salma Dualle
reviews.text:
We visited this café this morning looking for a quiet place to study. We ordered two drinks, including the iced latte shown in the picture. Unfortunately, the latte was very disappointing. The color was unusual for a latte, and it tasted extremely watered down with very little coffee flavor.
It also did not include any sweetener, which we were not aware of, and the cashier did not inform us at the time of ordering. We would have happily paid for it if we had known.
Each drink cost $7.35, which made the experience even more disappointing given the quality. We did ask if more espresso could be added, and a very kind staff member did try to fix it, which we appreciated. However, the drink still did not improve much and we ended up leaving half of it.
Unfortunately, this was our first visit and it will likely be our last. We were very disappointed with the experience.

reviews.author: Crystal Y
reviews.text: The cafe had a cozy atmosphere. I ordered the ube matcha latte, and it tasted delightful, very matcha-y and had wonderful latte art. One issue I had was the lack of seating. I would recommend either going earlier to secure spots or getting food/drinks to go. They also a rather small selection of drinks and food. I would have appreciated more variety. But overall, nice and cozy, tasty, and it felt like I was romantisizing my academic life. The music wasn't overbearing so great study space!

reviews.author: Nice Chan
reviews.text: Did the Art of Matcha making workshop with Chelsea and she was phenomenal. She was so knowledgable and provided helpful info on the different grades of matcha, the regions, how matcha is made etc. We got to try different types of matcha at the different grades, and then make our own strawberry or ube matcha latte. It was such a fun class and I learned a lot!

reviews.author: Ruby Lee
reviews.text: Very nice cafe, however, it is located on uoft campus which makes it a busy study spot for students(bring headphones). I liked it here, the matcha wasnt as sweet as I liked, but that's a personal opinion.  The staff that served me was very helpful and accommodating. I came here on a Wednesday around 1pm and it was busy. Didn't try the food, would come again 👍🏼

name: Cosmo Cafe | Bar
address: 398 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2G5, Canada
description: A Chinatown cafe and bar that transitions smoothly throughout the day. Cosmo is a comfortable spot for a coffee, a meal, or something stronger.

reviews.author: Simon Thompson
reviews.text: Excellent coffee and high quality baked goods.  It’s a great spot to meet up before whatever activity you’re partaking in that day.  It’s a great corner location on a big intersection where a very dynamic foot traffic crowd moves and saunters and stops presented in view of big, unobstructed windows.  It’s a great in and out neighborhood spot.

reviews.author: Cassie HW
reviews.text:
Cute shop!
Quick and nice service. I ordered a cortado and I liked it (I’m by no means an expert)
Cute store but better for takeout, not much seating.
Prices are on par for Toronto

reviews.author: KidWeaBoo
reviews.text: Not much of a cafe person but I had a hot matcha latte. It was well prepped and taste smooth and creamy without any bitterness or astringency. Would consider going again if I'm in the area.

reviews.author: Harsh Patel
reviews.text:
Cosmo Cafe Bar is such a gem in Toronto! I had coffee and matcha, and both were absolutely amazing. The coffee was rich, smooth, and perfectly balanced - you can really tell they care about quality. The matcha was just as impressive: vibrant, creamy, and not overly sweet, with that perfect earthy flavour.

The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, making it a great spot to relax or catch up with friends. I’ll definitely be coming back to try more from their menu. Highly recommend checking this place out!

reviews.author: ZHANG LARRY
reviews.text: Great coffee and a lovely atmosphere. The space is comfortable and relaxing, perfect for hanging out or taking a break. I tried the Hojicha and the Mocha, and both were excellent—rich, well-balanced, and clearly made with care. Definitely a place I’d come back to again!

name: HANBINGO 한빙고
address: 397 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2G6, Canada
description: A Chinatown spot specialising in Korean shaved ice and quality drinks. HANBINGO is a fun, colourful destination that does something genuinely unique.

reviews.author: Chi Tang
reviews.text:
Very nice staff and order was quick.   Had the blueberry bingsu for 3 people and the serving size was perfect.  There were a lot of toppings, blueberry, cheesecake, cornflake, marshmallow and cookie.  Would definitely come back.  Bonus having fresh sweet potato / yam aroma while they make a fresh batch.

They even show the last bean roasting date for the coffee lovers

reviews.author: Liz N.
reviews.text:
A great place to hangout with friends with some sweet bingsoo.
I had the matcha and the mango. It wasn't bad, but compared to other bingsoo places like the cups or snowday, it does lack in flavor. It does taste like they used relatively cheap ingredients and were not so generous with the fruit contents. But still pretty good.

reviews.author: vivek gupta
reviews.text: This was my first experience trying the Korean dessert, but it was awesome, i will definitely gonna try again during the summer time. It could be the best dessert for the summer to beat the heat❤️

reviews.author: Kiran Chawda
reviews.text:
I recently visited Hanbingo in Toronto and had such a wonderful experience! The place is cozy, clean, and perfect for a late-night dessert craving. Their bingsu is incredibly soft, fluffy, and full of flavour — it literally melts in your mouth. The fruit toppings were super fresh, and the portion size was generous for the price.

The staff were also very friendly and patient while we decided what to order, and the service was quick even though the place was busy. It’s a great spot to go with friends, family, or even for a cute dessert date.

If you love Korean shaved ice or want to try something refreshing and unique, Hanbingo is definitely worth it! I’ll happily go back again. 🍓🍧

reviews.author: Jerry Xie
reviews.text: This place looks good and has good ratings because they give you free drinks for a 5 star review. It’s also quite pricey. We got the mango bingsu and I don’t think I’ve ever had a mango so unripe. The ice was also terrible. I don’t even know how it’s so bad tbh. We actually sent the bingsu back (which I’ve never done before even at nice restaurants) and they came out with something that was the same so it wasn’t a fluke. To be fair, this was in January so mangoes weren’t in season but I’ve definitely had good mango bingsu at other establishments in January. Upon further tasting, I think the issue with their ice was it was left in the freezer for too long and had a faint freezer burn aftertaste which was really disturbing. I gave this feedback to the workers there so hopefully they can improve.

name: De Mello Coffee
address: 8 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5V 0S8, Canada
description: A downtown institution known for its serious approach to sourcing and roasting. De Mello is where Toronto's coffee culture has long had a home.

reviews.author: Pariya Abadeh
reviews.text: Loved my visit to De Mello Coffee. The coffee was excellent, smooth and flavorful, and the almond croissant was a standout: crispy and flaky on the outside, soft inside, and not overly sweet. The prices feel very reasonable for the quality you get. Highly recommended.

reviews.author: Angela A.
reviews.text:
⸻

I really enjoyed my Americano here — smooth, rich, and perfectly balanced. I’m a sucker for great design, and the tiger on the cup totally won me over (tigers are my favorite!).

The café is conveniently located in the lobby of the office tower at The Well, making it an easy stop for your morning coffee or mid-day caffeine fix. Seating is limited, but I was lucky enough to grab a table and enjoy my drink in peace.

A stylish little coffee spot with great energy and attention to detail — definitely worth checking out if you’re in the area!

reviews.author: Claudine Lee
reviews.text:
Got the Hojicha and my friend got their hot chocolate. Drinks are not too bad but I tasted the milk more than the Hojicha itself. The hot chocolate was just okay as well.

Drinks were made fairly quickly but they do get pretty busy. I would try the coffee next time I'm around the area.

reviews.author: Kath Hinoguin
reviews.text: Very nice cafe to stop by whenever I'm visiting The Well. Their location is very spacious and has some seats but not a lot. I love their coffee variety! Tried their Blueberry latte and it tastes so clean and smooth. Servers are all nice and greeted us with a smile.

reviews.author: Isabelle Cho
reviews.text: Got the matcha latte and it was pretty good. I wish it was mixed a bit more since there were a few clumps. Staff was friendly and efficient. Love the interior design, the place was clean and there were a few tables inside as well.

name: ICHA TEA
address: 235 Spadina Ave. Unit 4, Toronto, ON M5T 2E2, Canada
description: A Chinatown tea and coffee shop that takes both seriously. ICHA TEA is a calm, considered space for anyone who wants something beyond the usual.

reviews.author: Susan Hoshowski
reviews.text: Came here with friends. We were all a little confused about which part of the tea bar/sit down area we should be in, but our server was so very patient and helpful, we felt at ease right away. We three ladies had the Sesame tofu cheesecake and it was amazing! I also enjoyed my tea bar of Crimson Glory Rose - so delicious (and I'm not a real tea drinker). That was on the Traditional menu, which is what the seating area in the back of the place is for. If you're getting something to go, you order and pay for it in the front part of the place. Overall, loved the place. Only quibble would be that one of the two washrooms was out of order, so everyone had to use only other one that was available.

reviews.author: Jasmine Le
reviews.text: I come here all the time!! Best matcha spot ever! I will literally bring all my friends and recommend everyone to come here ❤️ the staff are so lovely! Wendy is amazing love her, always puts a smile on my face! That is my girl! ✨ The space is super clean, good spot to hang with friends or study! Overall, come and enjoy the matcha, the peoples and environment! 🥰 1000/10!! ⭐️

reviews.author: Rica Camat
reviews.text: If you’re in the Spadina/Chinatown area and looking for more than just a quick tea time and chill — ICHA TEA is a solid choice. I’ll definitely go back. Super love their strawberry matcha. 🍵🍓

reviews.author: Ken Greene (刘志林)
reviews.text: We had the hot brown sugar ginger milk tea w/ oat milk and pearls (25% sugar). It was good, but very ginger heavy. It's good that they offer other things, like cake and treats. I'll have to come back and try it again. 😏😋

reviews.author: Rev Ms
reviews.text:
“It’s just Colored Water, Not Tea!”
I’m so disappointed despite their high rating. They promote themselves as a tea house, so I expected quality brews. But whatever you order, they just grab tea leaves from the back, steep for barely a minute, and serve colored water with zero flavor. You could do better brewing at home by yourself!

name: Lait Night
address: 81 Huron St Main Floor, Toronto, ON M5T 2A8, Canada
description: Open until 3am on weekdays and 4am on weekends, Lait Night is one of the only specialty cafes in Toronto still serving past midnight. The go-to late night coffee stop in Chinatown.

reviews.author: Emily Lashkhia
reviews.text:
I think every student loves this place, I’ve been coming here for years. When you’re a student, the day you’re living tends to get shifted, but there’s limited cafes that accommodates that. I really do wish they could expand to other places, as they can become quite packed.

This place not only stays open late but their drinks are delicious! I highly recommend their iced hojicha, and my favourite mitarashi dango!

I’ll be back as usual, thank you for having me!

reviews.author: Shafee Rashidi
reviews.text:
Had an excellent time yesterday swinging by this lovely cafe! The barista who served us, AJ, was so awesome! Such a sociable, fun, awesome guy! I asked him for a drink recommendation, and he suggested the Hojicha. It was delicious, and the roasted flavour was really comforting, especially on such a cold night.

The vibes were immaculate in the cafe, really chill and felt like the perfect place to host those last night conversations with friends. Also, this spot is open until 4 AM, making it an excellent spot for a late night study session with treat from the cafe.

Super grateful for the kindness and conversations that AJ provided. I can't wait to come back :)

reviews.author: kaltrina sejdo
reviews.text: I came here today for Wendy’s birthday pop up and oh my god the drinks amazing and the staff are chill and sweet

reviews.author: N T
reviews.text: Friendly staff, handcrafted drinks, quiet café great for conversations or studying. They also have a small selection of desserts!

reviews.author: S
reviews.text:
The menu is a bit basic, but the servers were excellent.

The vibe is quite chill. When I went just after 9pm on a Saturday, every seat was taken. So it's clearly a popular spot to work.

name: Le Gourmand
address: 152 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5V 3S6, Canada
description: A downtown spot with a European sensibility and a menu that goes well beyond coffee. Le Gourmand is worth a visit for the pastries alone.

reviews.author: nomnomnom
reviews.text:
i can definitely see the appeal behind le gourmand’s chocolate chip cookie — it’s simple but it’s done sO well, and it pairs really nicely with a warm, steamed cup of milk. the outside is baked nice and crisp but the cookie itself was SOOO soft and chewy, and the inside was gooey with rich, sweet, melted chocolate that made it taste super decadent and indulgent. i warmed it up a bit and it felt like it was fresh out of the oven!

the bread pudding was a little bit too mushy for me honestly, i would’ve liked it to have a firmer, denser feeling and maybe a bit of a baked crisp on top. it tasted super tart-tasting and fruity from the banana and raisins(?), and i also liked how there was a bit of melted chocolate in it too.

reviews.author: Mckayla Ouellette
reviews.text: You know that cookie good. The cookies here always get hyped up and they are worth the hype I would say. They are soft and buttery on the inside but still have a crispy outer rim. I got the iced matcha and this was nothing to call home about but I don’t think matcha is their speciality by any means.

reviews.author: G P
reviews.text:
Had to come here for their famous chocolate chip cookies.  When I travel I like to try the areas best chocolate cookies.  This place did not disappoint.

Chocolate chip cookie was crispy and the outside and soft and chewy in the middle.  Perfect thickness and not too sweet.  Of course had to enjoy it with a glass of milk.  Lots of seating in the cafe.  Tables were kind of close together and was hard to navigate.  Will come back to try the other goodies they have.

reviews.author: Norbert Szabo
reviews.text: Stopped by Le Gourmand for coffee and dessert, and it hit the mark completely. The iced Americano was strong but smooth — exactly what you want on a slow afternoon — and the Basque cheesecake was rich, creamy, and perfectly balanced. Great vibe overall: relaxed but busy enough to feel alive, with friendly staff who actually seem to care. It’s the kind of café that makes you linger a little longer than planned.

reviews.author: saipattra phithakkittikhun
reviews.text:
"My 5th time here!"
Still just as good as the first time. The pancakes are legendary and the quality is always 10/10. Highly recommend! 🥞🍓

name: LITTLE PEBBLES CAFE
address: 160 Baldwin St #8, Toronto, ON M5T 3K7, Canada
description: A small Kensington cafe with a big personality. Little Pebbles is the kind of spot you feel lucky to find and happy to keep coming back to.

reviews.author: Sandi Cole
reviews.text:
Smashing IT!🥰

I stumbled upon this little gem while wandering through Kensington Market, and I’m so glad I did.

Nestled among an eclectic mix of food spots and cafés, Little Pebbles Café stands out with its unique desserts and specialty matcha drinks that satisfy every kind of craving.

Whether you’re starting your morning or unwinding after a long day, this café offers the perfect escape.

The atmosphere is cool, calm, and effortlessly relaxing — ideal for savoring your favorite matcha creation, catching up with a friend, or settling in with your laptop to get some work done.

There’s something inspiring about enjoying one of their beautifully crafted desserts while the world hums quietly around you.

Morning, noon, or evening, Little Pebbles Café is a delightful spot to spend time solo or with company, all while indulging in delicious matcha and thoughtfully made sweets.

Coupled with excellent and friendly customer service!

reviews.author: Salony Widjaja
reviews.text:
I came here on daughter's friend recommendation. Though small the place is easy to find.
Tried the matcha strawberry and matcha drink. Matcha is good and rich. The black sesame cake hs rich strong sesame taste. Love it. The matcha rasberry cookie is well balanced. Sourness and sweetness combination and not too sweet.
Place is quiet. Good for relaxing morning or afternoon tea time.

reviews.author: To Datenfood
reviews.text: Amazing experience for unique types of croissants! Especially the pizza croissants is amazing!! Azzy is amazing at the hospitality & Navv made the best pastries!

reviews.author: Prehistoric Terminator
reviews.text: Decided to try the croissant pizza due to my adorable girlfriend being curious to try it. The croissant crust was buttery and light while the mozzarella had an amazing cheese pull that is complimented by the herbs & tomato sauce. Would recommend trying this unique fusion!

reviews.author: Rachel K
reviews.text: Got their tiramisu croissant and coconut matcha cloud drink, really enjoyed both! The croissant is delicious, not too sweet and perfect as a snack. The drink was surprisingly good, the matcha foam was thick and flavourful, mixed really well with the coconut water.

name: Crimson Teas
address: 415 Spadina Ave., Toronto, ON M5T 2G6, Canada
description: A Chinatown tea house that approaches its craft with real care. Crimson Teas is a calm alternative to the neighbourhood's busier coffee spots.

reviews.author: Ivy_E
reviews.text: Met up with a close friend who recommended black tea noodles since I’m a huge tea lover and they did not disappoint. The space is great for groups, and the owner was super friendly, explaining how the noodles are made differently and how filling they are (very true cuz I wasn’t hungry at all later!). Portions are huge for the price. I got the pork chop noodles with milk tea, and my friend had the braised beef. The pork chop was juicy and tender, the noodles were perfectly cooked, and the soup had a slight sweetness that surprised me in a good way since most noodle soups I’ve had are overly salty and I’m a soup lover, but this one was perfectly balanced. Small menu but amazing quality and they also sell some small cute accessories to check it out! Definitely coming back!

reviews.author: Gohar Zakaryan
reviews.text: Came here for dinner and had a great time! The service was very fast and the owner sat with us to explain the menu. We tried the BBQ pork and the braised beef which both came with tea. Everything was delicious and very reasonably priced. The portions are very big and the meals are really balanced so you leave feeling satisfied. Had a great experience and really enjoyed the service as well. Will definitely be coming back!

reviews.author: Eliz w
reviews.text: lovely place! their satay sauce is amazing . creative noodles made with tea, lower GI index that regular noodles. their meal comes with a milk tea and that did not disappoint as well! highly recommend :) the owners r super friendly and nice people.

reviews.author: Symon Oliver
reviews.text:
Amazing noodles made with tea! This unique noodle recipe delivers a distinctive taste and texture that I've never experienced before.

The broth was perfectly balanced with light aromatics that allowed the richness to come through. Also, fresh pineapples were an interesting addition that added a hit of acid to the richness.

We spoke to the owner, and learned that the tea based noodle recipe is more than just the flavor and texture. It has additional benefits! If you want to learn more, I highly recommend you ask the owner.

reviews.author: Richard Wilson
reviews.text: Went in for lunch.  Food was cooked fresh.  Was very tasty.   Had the pineapple satay.  Sweet and spicy. So good.  Stools not the greatest to sit on but the rest of the place had a cool vibe.  Great service.  Will go again.

name: Arena Coffee Bar
address: 15 Tank House Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: A Distillery District cafe with an industrial edge and a strong coffee program. Arena is a solid spot before or after exploring the historic district.

reviews.author: Man Sang Tang
reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
I seldom drink coffee, but the barista kindly introduced different kinds of coffee to me and even offered a low-caffeine option, which was very considerate. The latte art was beautiful, and the atmosphere was cozy and relaxing — a great place to enjoy a quiet moment in the Distillery District.

reviews.author: Michael Stevens
reviews.text:
This was such a fantastic place to stop by and warm up in. It has a pretty cool atmosphere with a lot of sports jerseys and memorabilia for sale. When you walk in tucked away on the back left side you'll see a really nice coffee bar with some seats and wall tables.

I tried the cortado and the flavorful taste was so good. I tried a sweet creamy coffee mixed with an espresso. I ended up asking and purchasing a bag of the coffee included in my review pictures that I ordered.  They have a pretty diverse range of beans and types of coffee which goes a long way in coffee bars. The staff explained that they get their coffees from around the world.

The prices were quite fair and I'm always happy to pay for a good taste or flavor. The service was quick and efficient and quite friendly.  A great place to stop anytime but more so on a cool winter evening.

reviews.author: Mello
reviews.text:
Located inside the Sport Gallery shop in the distillery is a small cafe.
The flat whites are consistently great flavour and customer service has been good.
I haven’t see any washrooms.

reviews.author: Nata Li
reviews.text:
Unfortunately, some items from the menu were not available, but otherwise - a great place!

A very atmospheric store, where sports fans will find souvenirs and themed gifts and something unique for themselves!

I would like to separately note the delicious coffee!

reviews.author: Dmitry Romanenko
reviews.text: Arena is comfy coffee shop in maple leaf merchandise store. Environment is nice, staff is friendly. Sadly, my order was done with mistake, but barista without hesitation solved the problem. Btw, They serve rich pilot coffee beans.

name: Brick Street Bakery
address: 27 Trinity St, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: A Distillery District institution famous for its breads and pastries. The coffee is good but it's the baked goods that make this a must-visit.

reviews.author: James L
reviews.text: We stumbled upon this little gem while wandering through the Distillery District this Sunday morning looking for a place to sit and grab some breakfast. A lot of places did not seem to be open despite it being after 10am. Entered and were immediately greeted and after a short look around proceeded to order two breakfast sandwiches and a couple hot beverages. Service was super friendly and quick and the breakfast sandwich was over the top! FRESH, Tasty and a generous portion! Served on delicious whole wheat bread this was really a great food experience! The bakery has a cool vintage vibe and feels like a local bistro should. They do have a good selection of take out which we will definitely try at a later visit. There are stools and a small area on each side to enjoy your food or drink on site which we took advantage of. Thanks Brick St. ! Excellent Espresso too! ☕️

reviews.author: Oliver Pe
reviews.text:
Nov2024  Casual, no frills, and rustic bakery at the Historical Distillery complex that serves a wide range of baked goods and dishes.

The sandwiches are huge and the listed menus are varied.  Tastes are diverse to appeal to plenty of diners.  The brewed coffee is typical drip type strong and cloudy.

Service is counter takeaway style.  Downside is that you need request for napkins despite them having a diy sermvice takeaway counter.  No driniking water as well.

Dining nooks are limited to small stalls and counters.

Ideal for takeaways and very quick bites.

reviews.author: Brendan L
reviews.text: We stopped by again today like we do every year when we visit the Christmas market at the Distillery District and it never disappoints. Our favourites are the Cornish pasty, sausage rolls and the butter pecan tarts. So warm and delicious!

reviews.author: Carolina G. Morini
reviews.text: A bit disappointed. I got a ham and cheese croissant and it only had one tiny piece of ham. They didn’t warm it up or at least ask if I wanted it warmed up. A bit overpriced, and the tip options start at 18%… I mean, I’m just taking out a cold ham and cheese croissant.

reviews.author: Ok Sun
reviews.text:
Never had any disappointing products from this bakery and this is my go to sweets pick up place whenever I am in distillary district.

This time I got minced meat tart, pecan butter tart, two different kinds of brownies. All of them were like homemade excellent comforting dessert.

name: Berkeley Cafe
address: 262 The Esplanade, Toronto, ON M5A 4J6, Canada
description: A top-rated independent coffee shop in Downtown Toronto with a 4.9★ Google rating. Berkeley Cafe is known for consistently great coffee and a comfortable, unpretentious atmosphere.

reviews.author: Linus V
reviews.text:
I got the ham and cheese sandwich. It was around $14 including tax, which felt a bit pricey at first, but it was packed with fillings and absolutely satisfying.
Unlike some sandwiches that only have a token piece of lettuce, this one had plenty of fresh, crisp lettuce—just like in the photo. Personally, I really liked that the pickles had a strong flavor, and the bread was soft and easy to eat, not dry at all.
The cafe has a more casual, down-to-earth atmosphere rather than a fancy, trendy vibe. It was lively and seemed quite popular, even though it’s a small place.
I’d definitely love to visit again!

reviews.author: Lindsey Tran
reviews.text:
My office always order breakfast croissants and pastries from here. Freshly made and tasty. Shawn, the owner, is very kind. We always feel welcomed here!
(In the photo is sausage & egg sandwich)

reviews.author: Sophie Tremblay
reviews.text: I’d been craving a good Chopped Cheese since my trip to New York, and Berkeley Cafe absolutely delivered! The sandwich was spot on, and their bagels are a must-try too — simple, fresh, and delicious. The service was excellent, and I loved relaxing on the clean outdoor patio surrounded by trees. Definitely looking forward to coming back!

reviews.author: Amir Moghisy
reviews.text: Amazing little spot, when you walk in they great you with a smile and through amazing customer service take the order. We had the feta egg croissant and sides of hash-browns. The food, matcha and the price were great. However, the normal drip coffee was AMAZING, excellent beans and phenomenal results at the end. Highly recommend this place for a breakfast or lunch as it hey have a nice patio and indoor sitting area for you to enjoy your food.

reviews.author: Ariel Banner
reviews.text: I’ve been craving a good Chopped Cheese sandwich since my trip to New York last year and let me tell you, Berkeley Cafe does not disappoint. For those of you who haven’t tried it, next time you’re craving a Big Mac, don’t go to McDonalds, come here. Also for those bagel lovers out there- they have wonderful bagels for breakfast- they’re simple and delicious. Service is wonderful here and I greatly enjoyed the outdoor patio that was clean and surrounded by trees. Can’t wait to go back!

name: Wildly Delicious Fine Foods
address: 11 Tank House Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: A Distillery District spot that lives up to its name. Wildly Delicious pairs quality coffee with exceptional food in a beautiful historic setting.

reviews.author: Linus V
reviews.text:
This place has a really cute selection of stylish goods, foods, and seasonings.
The store has a fun, slightly cluttered vibe (in a good way!) and it’s enjoyable just to look around. You can tell every product is carefully chosen.

I tried the turkey sandwich, and it was excellent. The turkey wasn’t just thin slices but nicely thick, the cheese had a rich flavor, and the bread was really tasty. There was also a subtle fruity sweetness in the sauce, almost like a hidden touch, which really elevated the whole sandwich. Outstanding! Since the shop sells many different sauces, I’d say they really put effort into them.

The price was around $20, which is a little on the high side, but the portion was generous and I felt satisfied. Packaging was also neat and careful.

The only downside was the wait time. Even though no one else was waiting for food when I ordered, my sandwich still took a bit to come out. Honestly though, the taste made it worth the wait. Just keep in mind it might not be ideal if you’re in a rush.

Lots of other menu items looked tempting too—can’t wait to come back and try more!

reviews.author: Avy-Loren Cohen
reviews.text:
Atmosphere.  Excellent. Clean. Beautifully merchandising and great variety and not rip off pricing.
Service. Lovely and friendly.
Food. Great assortment and all fresh.  Pricing is reasonable to high.
To come again - if in the area. It. It go out of one’s way. Not a destination place.

reviews.author: ML G
reviews.text: This little café is truly a hidden gem. The vibe is warm and welcoming, with rustic décor that feels both nostalgic and refreshing. The food is healthy, clean, and thoughtfully prepared, making every bite a delight. The staff are kind, attentive, and quietly supportive, adding to the calm and cozy atmosphere. It’s the kind of place that takes you down memory lane—where time slows down and comfort takes over.

reviews.author: Phyllis Tee
reviews.text: Their chimichurri steak salad was fresh and delicious, and the salad dressing was impressive and nutritious. 10/10 would definitely come back again.

reviews.author: Mello
reviews.text:
A Hidden Gem in the Distillery – Wildly Delicious Lives Up to Its Name!

Wildly Delicious is exactly what its name promises—a delightful culinary experience in the heart of the Distillery District. Every visit here feels like a little escape, with its charming ambiance, welcoming atmosphere, and a menu that celebrates bold flavors and quality ingredients.

This time, we indulged in the chimichurri steak sandwiches, and they were absolutely fantastic. The steak was perfectly cooked—tender, juicy, and packed with flavor—while the chimichurri added the perfect bright and zesty touch. Pairing that with a rich, expertly brewed coffee and a crisp, delicate biscotti made for a truly satisfying meal from start to finish.

One of the things we love most about Wildly Delicious is the consistently great service. The staff is always friendly, attentive, and genuinely passionate about what they do, making every visit feel special. Whether you’re stopping by for a leisurely bite, a coffee break, or a full dining experience, you can count on being well taken care of.

Wildly Delicious is a must-visit for anyone looking for thoughtfully crafted food in a warm and inviting setting. We’ll definitely be back for more!

name: Roozamoon Cafe
address: 398 Queen St E, Toronto, ON M5A 1T3, Canada
description: A downtown cafe with Persian-inspired touches and a warm, inviting atmosphere. Roozamoon is a distinctive spot that stands out from the usual options.

reviews.author: Ina Friedrich
reviews.text: My favourite cafe in the area! Like a cozy living room filled with warmth, art, board games and delicious food and drinks. Hamoon, the owner, is super kind and welcoming and so is everyone else I've encountered - whether it's people working at the cafe or other guests. I've been to one of their workshops (crochet) recently and it was such a unique experience being in the cafe after hours with a group of 10/12 people and just doing crafts in such a cozy environment. I love the Persian tea with rose and the spinach feta bakery item! They have free wifi as well. I've been when they play live music as well and it's another amazing layer of this place!

reviews.author: Bebsabe Duque
reviews.text: Roozamoom is a true gem in the city! The vibe is the perfect mix of eclectic hippy-chic and old-school Persian soul. Between the live music pop-ups, DJ sets, and the resident cat Juliet—she’s the absolute best! , the atmosphere is incredibly homey and community-oriented. The owner is always around making sure everyone feels welcome. My personal favorite is the Persian Tea, which is delicious and, best of all, refillable. 10/10 recommend!

reviews.author: Tahsin Rahman
reviews.text:
cozy, nice and warm cafe with a cute cat in the house! the barista was really nice and welcoming had a good chat! the drinks were sooooo good that i need to go every week for real! i wish it was near me aha!
saffron blossom iced tea and the saffron pistachio latte were amazing!! i’m still thinking about those haha😭vibes are chill with good music and good atmosphere.

reviews.author: Eren (The Hector)
reviews.text:
This café has a very unique and cozy atmosphere. The moment you walk in, you notice the carpets on the floor and the old chairs and tables, which give the place a warm, nostalgic character. It doesn’t feel like a typical coffee shop at all — it feels more like someone’s living room, in a good way.

The lighting is very low and intimate, with candles lit everywhere, creating a calm and relaxing vibe. It’s a great place to sit down, slow things down, and actually enjoy your coffee. The coffee itself was also quite good and well prepared.

The only downside is that the inside can feel a bit stuffy and not very well ventilated, especially when it’s busy. Other than that, the atmosphere, decor, and overall experience were really enjoyable.

I’d definitely recommend this place to anyone looking for a cozy, different, and atmospheric café experience.

reviews.author: Kitchen Patrol
reviews.text:
If I could spend every day at Roozamoon I would.  Having been in Toronto for 5 nights, this is my third visit...despite the 45 min commute. Sunday morning is the jazz jam. How blessed. Thank you. Transcendental.

The comments echo what this cafe is all about. High vibe low key, spiritual calm, a place to bring people together.  Thanks to Hamoon and Roxana for creating this space for us to encounter.

Wishing you all the very best in this experiment. Much love.

name: El Catrin Destileria
address: 18 Tank House Lane, Toronto, ON M5A 3C4, Canada
description: A Distillery District spot with Mexican flair and a beautiful space to match. El Catrin is a great stop for coffee before dinner or drinks after.

reviews.author: Michael Hill
reviews.text: A really great spot for a chill afternoon to enjoy food and drinks. The waitress was phenomenal and excellent. The food was very well prepared. But I will say the asada might be a touch over priced. Would like to see the drinks served in proper stem wear. A margarita in a 350 ml glass is not great. I am not a snob about many things but drinks in the correct glass wear is a hangup of mine. That bring said awesome place and I would go back

reviews.author: Ilia
reviews.text:
This is the fifth time we have been to this restaurant and when it starts from 10 out of 10 you never think it can go any higher but every time we show up they manage to do something better.

Pamela, Aldo and so many other incredible servers are doing a superb job. Even Earl the manager distinctly remembered an interaction from over a year ago, when we commended our server for doing an incredible job.

Great job offering the flight of margaritas and adding a vegetarian menu!

This place is going in the right direction, keep up the great work!

reviews.author: Netanel Nesher
reviews.text:
This is such a beautiful place! Designed so marvelously with such a great vibe!
The service was really really great, the waitress was sweet and lovely.
The food wasn’t the best, it lacked spices and some flavors. I didn’t try any fish.
Overall I had fun

reviews.author: Trupti jadhav
reviews.text:
Such a happening vibe here. I am a big fan of mexican cuisine, and I totally loved this place for its tacos and drinks.
We tried pollo pibil, roasted coliflour, fried chicken tacos and hongos- every plate had amazing flavours. If you like spicy food, dont miss adding the delicious chilli oil to your plates😋

reviews.author: Krissy P
reviews.text: Wow what a place! The atmosphere is so unique, loved looking around at the walls. The taste of the food was fabulous. Freshest guacamole I've ever had, "La Familia" was perfect for the table and the beef and pork tacos were the best I've ever had. The food service was quick!! The margarita flight offered new tastes to try that were delicious as well. Our server Pamela was such a delight. Her recommendations were exactly what we needed for our table.

name: Wallace Espresso
address: 848 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1P1, Canada
description: A King West coffee bar that keeps things focused and does it well. Wallace is for people who want a great espresso without any distraction.

reviews.author: Wes Inaba
reviews.text:
Wallace Espresso is a teeny hole in the wall establishment just east of Stanley Park in downtown Toronto.  They have high quality java options here and a small set of eats that are great.

The service was very kind and helpful!

The space is pretty basic.  White walls.  But the windows at the front in a bay setting offer a lot of light into the space!

5 x ☕☕☕☕☕

reviews.author: Cindy Hum
reviews.text:
Decent and fairly priced drinks - very smooth, good depth in flavour. Drinks were served very quickly. Good amount of seating despite the small space.

Although there wasn’t London Fog on the menu explicitly, I was able to request for it, and the barista at front was able to make it (and a good one, at that!). Definitely worth a stop if you’re passing by.

reviews.author: Anna Suki
reviews.text:
Such a cute little dainty coffee shop on King West. I ordered an iced latte with regular milk and also a ginger molasse cookie. The cookie was made fresh!

Both items were better than what you can get at Starbucks. Would totally come back to support this local shop!

Also, there’s free Wifi so if you want to work remotely here, you have seating inside with lots of natural lighting!

reviews.author: yun
reviews.text:
Cozy and peaceful atmosphere with healthy plants, Barista is friendly, Blackbird pastries and a good coffee…. I loved here totally!!!!
I had a lemon loaf and flat white. The flat white was nice! Beautiful latte art on the cup and mixing well between milk and espresso.

reviews.author: Negar Shahrokh
reviews.text: Lovely coffee shop with a very professional and friendly barista. I tried their iced cappuccino, almond croissant, and carrot cake (bread). The cappuccino was amazing and I liked the croissant better than the carrot cake even though it wasn’t that fresh. Both of the pastries had a strong cinnamon flavor which I liked but might be overpowering if you’re not a big fan of it.

name: Café Negin
address: 787 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1N4, Canada
description: A warmly personal King West cafe with over 890 reviews and a loyal following that shows up every morning. Cafe Negin keeps things small, consistent, and genuinely good.

reviews.author: btajro
reviews.text: We absolutely LOVE this cafe! The atmosphere, service, and food/drink quality is top notch. We walked by it one day and decided to drop by and had such a great time, we decided to come back every weekend. The servers are extremely polite and kind. Definitely recommend!

reviews.author: Nicole HR
reviews.text: What a gem! I stumbled upon this place after dropping off my dog to his groomers close by  and im upset I didn’t know of this place sooner. I got a very delicious bagel with cream cheese and chai latte. The owner was there, what a lovely lady and she offered us warm soup on the house to keep all customers warm during a very very cold day.

reviews.author: Mason La
reviews.text:
Just recently I heard through the grapevine that this place had undergone a change in ownership, so my girlfriend and I decided to go check it out.

To say the least we were thoroughly impressed. I got a chai latte and my other half got a regular latte. Forgot to take a picture of the food, but it was very good, no complaints there.

Overall pretty pleasant experience, and I’m glad this place is being revamped to its full potential under its new ownership.

reviews.author: Parmida Shahbakhti
reviews.text:
The ownership of this place changed last month and the new owner is so exceptionally warm and welcoming!

They have blankets for a cozy vibe, dogs are welcome, and their tables are spacious.

Their saffron tea and chocolate croissant was exceptional!

Great place to work or study.

reviews.author: Hiral Patel
reviews.text: Cute spot for chai & bites! Turkish inspired baked goods like pistachio croissant and cookies. Good space to work or study.

name: Jacked Up Coffee 225 King
address: 225 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 3M2, Canada
description: The King St location of Jacked Up Coffee, bringing the same Annex-loved coffee program to King West. Consistent, well-made, and easy to like.

reviews.author: Anna M
reviews.text:
Super cute vintage Citroen truck as a coffee kiosk inside an office building. This truck makes the whole boring nondescript lobby come alive with fun.

It’s worth coming to get coffee here even if it’s just for a change in routine coffee pick up in a normal regular cafe!

I decided to get coffee here after lunch and it was even cuter up close than from the outside. Service was professional and attentive. Beans were ground and coffee was made after a few quick minutes. Even their cap is on brand, the same blue as their truck color.

There are tables and high stools in the building lobby where one can take their coffee.

reviews.author: Olivia Polkowski
reviews.text: 1 star review for terrible service and pricing at Jacked Up Coffee in DT Toronto's PATH food court. Ordered an iced Americano with half a shot of caramel. Was told only one-size cup was available and asked to pay first. This is what I got for the $6 order: lukewarm coffee, no ice, no caramel. The cup literally has no chill. If this is how they handle a simple order with no other customers in line, I can’t imagine what it’s like when they’re actually busy. Avoid.

reviews.author: Parul Gupta
reviews.text:
Found my new favourite coffee spot in Toronto!
I tried Americano which was as fresh as it was brewed at home. Bonus points to this place for existing out of a truck, friendly Barista and cool branding.

Highly recommended to coffee lovers

reviews.author: Kevin Tan
reviews.text:
What a cute coffee bus inside a fancy modern building.
Had chance talk to the owner during buying a bag of beans and a cup of coffee, he shared his own story with coffee and founder of this Jacked Up Coffee, which the most joyful part is  how he transferred from a bartender to a barista and bringing a great cup of coffee to the costumers.
I Wish he having wonderful time in his coffee journey continuously.

reviews.author: U P
reviews.text: Ordered a Mocha, which I had to wait for about 15 minutes to order, since OOO message. That's fine, I can live with that, but the person making the drinks was rather unfriendly to where after you've walked 12 minutes to get there to try the place, waited, and are then acknowledged in such a 'what do you want' manner, its like why come back.

name: Forno Cultura
address: 609 King St W, Toronto, ON M5V 1M5, Canada
description: One of Toronto's best bakeries, with a coffee program that holds its own against the pastries. Forno Cultura on King West is worth the inevitable wait.

reviews.author: Yvonne Huang
reviews.text:
Being a big fan of Almond croissants for as long as I remember, I finally found something even better and can never go back now!
Forno Cultura’s Cornetto Mandolra is packed with roasted Almond and the pastry is so flakey and most importantly, just enough sweetness that doesn’t leave you feeling guilty and heavy. It is definitely superior and I’ll ditch almond croissants for this anytime! Please please please keep this recipe and keep making them fresh! Such a gem!

The bakery has a hip style seating and really appreciate the goods more when you can see the hardworking bakers put all their hearts and souls into their creations.

reviews.author: Rana Halaby
reviews.text:
I got a recommendation to try this place in Toronto and I have to say that the culinary experience was somewhat underwhelming. Thing is - we went in with high expectations given the buzz, and I was a bit disappointed with the food. Not that it’s not good - on the contrary, if you like bread and pastries, this place does it well - but when it comes to breakfast sandwiches, I felt they were bland.

Their coffee s good though - and they have
this open kitchen concept which I thought was cool.

Overall, it’s still is a place to try given the quality of the ingredients used - but if you like flavors - you might wanna stay away from  the egg sandwiches. It’s also on the pricey side - we paid 43$ for 2 sandwiches and 2 flat whites + 1 baba au rhum.

reviews.author: Christen
reviews.text: Everything I've tried has been delicious, from the cookies (my absolute favourite is amaretti di pistacchio) to the pizza slices which usually have seasonal toppings. You can tell the coffee is great quality. The staff is really lovely as well.

reviews.author: Priscilla Wong
reviews.text: I feel like for a bakery, it was disappointing for the worst part of the sandwiches to be the bread. Very hard and dry, I fear they gave us bread from days before. The much drool sandwich was really good in its fillings but the rest I just couldn’t finish. I’m not sure if I’d go back.

reviews.author: naghmeh sheikh hassani
reviews.text: Love this place! They have some of the best grab-and-go breakfast options in the city, and the cookies are a must — you really can’t go wrong with any of them. They’re a bit pricey for how small they are, but absolutely worth it for the quality and flavor. Not much outdoor seating, but there’s some available inside for when you want to enjoy your treats on the spot.

name: maman
address: 100 King St W, Toronto, ON M5X 1A9, Canada
description: The Toronto outpost of the beloved New York cafe. maman on King West is beautiful, the coffee is excellent, and the cookies are worth making a trip for.

reviews.author: Susan Hollander
reviews.text: I went to Maman for an afternoon snack and beverage. The smell of freshly baked goods greets you as you walk into the restaurant. The staff were all kind and most had french accents. I ordered the lavender hot chocolate and the cookie which advertised was Oprah's favorite. The cookie was stellar! Incredibly gooey, chocolatey and indulgent. The hot chocolate I found was not as rich in flavour as I was hoping, however overall, I would return.

reviews.author: Anita
reviews.text:
This is the cutest food stall in the First Canadian Place. Enjoyed my vanilla latte made with oat milk. ☕

My avocado tartine was artfully presented and delicious. 🥑

Service was swift and friendly.

reviews.author: Dana Shapoval
reviews.text:
I visited this place in Montreal during COVID, and it was amazing back then — the breakfasts, the coffee, the baked goods, everything was perfect. So I decided to try their Toronto location, and I think it’s popular mostly because it’s in an office area. Everyone comes for their morning coffee, so the line was pretty long (I arrived right when offices were opening).

I ordered the pumpkin spice matcha, and there was no pumpkin flavor at all, and barely any matcha. A proper cup should have about 4g of matcha, and this tasted like maybe 2g — very weak.

The croissants were also flavorless, not crispy, not buttery — just bland.

I did enjoy the lavender loaf — the flavor was lovely and the glaze was delicious.
But overall, I think everything here is overpriced for what you get. The baked goods simply don’t justify the cost; the taste feels like something that should be priced much lower.

reviews.author: Star View
reviews.text: Nice vibe and outdoor atmosphere. Very nice and friendly staff. Food below average. Latte had too much milk vs amount of coffee. Canele bottom was kind of burned. Almond croissant was only crispy at both ends and rest very doughy. Didn’t meet expectations for food.

reviews.author: Hannah T
reviews.text: If you like sweet coffee drinks do yourself a favor and try their salted tahini honeycomb latte. One of my top 3 coffee drinks I have ever tried. Such an interesting and well working combinations. Maman if you read this please expand to Seattle or Hamburg, Germany. Thank you!! 🙏🏼🙏🏼🙏🏼

name: NEO COFFEE BAR PATH
address: The Exchange Tower, 130 King St W CS12A, Toronto, ON M5X 1E1, Canada
description: NEO in the PATH brings specialty coffee underground. A genuinely good option for downtown workers who want quality without going outside.

reviews.author: J. A.
reviews.text: I love this spot. Their vanilla cream puffs are divine! And they make a great iced strawberry matcha as well!

reviews.author: Putri Naibaho
reviews.text:
stumbled upon neo while making my way through the path one morning. the sole barista was lovely - friendly and helpful with thoughtful recommendations.

the space is impeccably clean, conveniently situated in the middle of the path where it's easy to find. they offer a pleasant seating area perfect for checking emails or enjoying your drink, though i'm not certain about outlets for longer work sessions.

their drink selection is quite nice, with an impressive array of matcha options that keep me coming back. each variety has been consistently delightful.

a particularly nice touch is the customization station where you can add your own honey and sweeteners. there's something rather special about personalizing your drink with fresh honey, though milk isn't available at the station and must be requested at the counter.

this charming spot has become my weekday retreat - a peaceful moment in the bustling underground path.

reviews.author: Amy Zhu
reviews.text: Hands down the best matcha I’ve ever had! Both their espresso and matcha flavours are rich and smooth, and are never overpowered by sweeteners. They always make the perfect ratio between milk to espresso or milk to matcha. The baked goods are also delicious and fresh. Plus the service is always quick and friendly!

reviews.author: Munch
reviews.text: So I got a small ice coffee (left) and a medium hojicha latte (right), as you can see in the photo the cups are the same size and the hojicha latte is significantly less than the ice coffee. I’ve asked them twice if that’s a medium and they said yes. What a scam.

reviews.author: Michelle
reviews.text:
Eh... do not get me wrong nice interior and menu. I've always wanted to try Neo for their matcha since I see them on social media. Matcha berry latte just tasted very milky and yogurt like.

Service was really nice and fast. I didn't know they had a Path location til I came across it. There's a few seats! and they sell pastries, mochi, and sparkling drinks

name: Sam James Coffee Bar - PATH
address: 150 King St W, Toronto, ON M5H 3T9, Canada
description: Sam James underground in the PATH. The same focused, quality-driven approach that made the original famous, now available for the downtown commute.

reviews.author: Alexia B
reviews.text: I was very happy with my coffee and croissant. If you find yourself downtown and want to venture underground stop by SJCB. They have a nice sitting area across from the coffee bar.

reviews.author: Madison Ferrer
reviews.text: Cutest little grab and go shop in the path. First time trying their chai and it was delicious and actually super hot which was awesome. Staff was unbelievably kind and great to chat with. There’s a cute little seating just across the hall as well. Great vibes, affordable and yummy drinks n pastries

reviews.author: Shabibi Khan
reviews.text:
I went to Sam James for an afternoon indulgence. Tucked away in the PATH  downtown it's a charming coffee place.
The latte was great made with lactose free milk. Not every cafe or coffee shop has it which means Sam James will be my go to everytime I go to work.
I look forward to trying the other beverages.
There is no seating area but I believe they have a second location as well.
I recommend this cafe

reviews.author: Paul Nazareth
reviews.text:
THE espresso on-the-go PATH favourite for Bay streeters, St.Andrew station Tim’s is not the spot for real espresso - walk a few steps to Sam’s.

Use their nice little seating spot in the path too, but with Starbucks closing locations on Bay St and this is a local spot, it’s the best spot.

Good luck finding anything else in the PATH! I was born a few blocks from here and still need to come up every once to get my bearings….

reviews.author: Z Chowdhury
reviews.text: I’ve been coming to Sam James for many years, and the coffee has been consistently excellent every single time. I even use their beans at home – they’re the best I’ve found anywhere in North America. The roast is perfectly balanced, never overdone, and the quality really shines through in every sip. That said, the prices are starting to get a bit out of reach, which makes it harder to enjoy as often as I’d like.

name: Cosette Coffee
address: 1715 Bloor St W, Toronto, ON M6P 1B2, Canada
description: A downtown cafe with a soft, feminine aesthetic and a serious coffee program underneath it. Cosette is pretty and substantive in equal measure.

reviews.author: Michelin Eater
reviews.text:
I’ve been on the hunt for the perfect Dubai Chewy, and Cosette Coffee absolutely delivers. Their version stands out for its beautifully balanced filling — pleasantly nutty with a delicate kadif and pistachio profile, and most importantly, not overly sweet.

Another standout was the mushroom croissant. The mushrooms were incredibly fresh, flavorful, and perfectly prepared — I’d gladly come back just for this alone.

I also tried the yuzu soda, which completely exceeded my expectations. I typically avoid sodas like this, but it was exceptionally refreshing, with vibrant citrus notes and delicious bits of purée that remained consistent from the first sip to the last.

Cosette Coffee truly impressed me with both creativity and quality. Highly recommended — a well-deserved five stars.

Edit: i came back for all their Dubai Chewy flavours. The Mugwort and Injeolmi are worth a try!!!

reviews.author: Warren Chanansingh
reviews.text:
Fantastic service. Fantastic people. Fantastic food.

Cosette Coffee was top tier in terms of taste, fast service, and customer centricity.

I was able to order and receive my order exactly as I wanted in 2 minutes flat. And the place was bustling and busy at that!

I'd prefer a matcha latte and forgot to  tell them that I wanted it iced. They remade it for me with no issues.  The food on display looked extremely scrumptious. I also had the matcha Madeline and a brocelli and egg quiche. Both were extremely satisfying and not too sweet.

Will definitely visit again soon.

reviews.author: Duane Brown
reviews.text: Heard the walnut cookie was great. It was! Also tried their Yuzuade soda and it was good too. Nice little spot with a big table for working in the back.

reviews.author: Shelley Davis
reviews.text: Cosette is my reward after a jog around High Park. It really is a treat. Staff are friendly and the service is impeccable, even when they’re under pressure you never feel rushed. The little serving trays are like a switch saying it’s time to relax. The coffee is reliable and the pastries are perfectly flaky. I love it.

reviews.author: Divine
reviews.text: I love this café! Their iced matcha latte is my absolute favorite, and the chocolate cookie is a must-try. The staff is always welcoming, and the atmosphere is perfect for working on my laptop with relaxing music in the background. They also have lovely outdoor seating. Highly recommend!

name: The Brick Room (一期一会)
address: 9 Temperance St, Toronto, ON M5H 1Y6, Canada
description: The Brick Room is a minimalist Japanese-inspired cafe in downtown Toronto, known for exceptional matcha and espresso served in a beautifully considered space. One of the most distinctive cafes in the city.

reviews.author: T
reviews.text: Came here around 2pm on a Saturday. Quite busy but was able to get seating. Ordered from the seasonal menu, the sticky rice mango matcha and a pistachio croissant. You could taste the rice bits in the drink and the mango as well. Didnt feel it overpowered the matcha which was nice. I also enjoyed the dried mango piece on the drink. The matcha itself was about $9. I would come back to try their other seasonal drinks as they had many selections.

reviews.author: Zeta
reviews.text:
The Brick Room Cafe is such a cute spot! I tried their winter special First Bite Matcha and it was genuinely an experience drink. They instructed me to pour the matcha in and sip it without mixing, so every sip had slightly different notes 😋

The flavours were super unique and delicious, I picked up notes of apple, strawberry, cream, and matcha. It was really enjoyable and also so aesthetically pleasing. I’d love to come back and try more drinks here! 😋🍵

reviews.author: Shuxin C
reviews.text:
I visited both their locations - the one on Temperance St and the one located in the Path. The location on Temperance St has a much brighter, airier vibe and is good for chatting with friends. There’s a row of bar seating plus some 2 seaters throughout the store.

Trie “brown noise” from the winter specialty menu and it was very good! The genmaicha had a nice, roasted flavor but it wasn’t bitter, and the houjicha cream was creamy but not overwhelming. It was the perfect balance. Def recommend!!

Have also tried the houjicha latte here - it tasted good, but not sure if I want to pay close to $10 again.

Overall the prices at this place are pretty steep (I think one of the top in Toronto), but I think their specialty drinks are worth a try.

reviews.author: Yara Daou
reviews.text:
Loved their hazelwood drink!!! It’s on the sweeter side so if you don’t like sweet drinks you should try their brewed coffee is pretty good! But I love me a sweet drink!

Just wish they had pastries to try since it was our first time there but understandable since it was New Year’s Day and most probably they thought it wouldn’t be as busy. Will come back next time to try other things!

reviews.author: Eye No
reviews.text:
This café is a must-visit for serious coffee lovers and is conveniently located with two spots nearby.
My experience was exceptional: the owner personally made my drink, and my Flat White was absolute perfection. The quality is outstanding, the espresso was rich and they offer great varieties of espresso beans.

The staff is great ; the baristas are highly skilled in their craft and provide warm customer service.
Their space is very clean and, most importantly, is a refreshing laptop-free café. This policy creates a wonderful atmosphere focused entirely on enjoying your coffee and having good conversations.
Highly recommended if you want the best brew and a chance to truly connect.

name: Ninetails Coffee | Bloor
address: 651 Bloor St W, Toronto, ON M6G 1L1, Canada
description: A Bloor St cafe with an anime-inspired name and a coffee program that takes things seriously. Ninetails is a fun, well-made addition to the Annex.

reviews.author: Demi Wu
reviews.text:
Such a lovely and quiet Japanese cafe! Their matcha latte is excellent and served traditionally in a beautiful chawan when have it in store.

It's a very peaceful atmosphere, perfect for relaxing or getting some work done. A nice bonus: they have a few handy charging spots available.

reviews.author: Daria Kurovskaya
reviews.text: First visit today and I really liked it. The cappuccino was flavourful and rich without any bitterness, and the size was perfect. Honestly, it might be the best cappuccino in the neighbourhood, and I’ve tried plenty. Nice selection of fresh pastries too. The space is cozy with plenty of seating, and the atmosphere is relaxing and quiet even with place being packed, perfect for a morning coffee. Loved the design. The staff were friendly and quick. I’ll definitely be back.

reviews.author: Nhi Nguyen
reviews.text: This place is sooo good. I’m obsessed with their vanilla coffee—smooth, cozy, not too heavy! And don’t get me started on the Basque cheesecake… it’s AMAZING. The vanilla coffee + cheesecake combo is perfect. But if you’re not into super sweet, get a matcha latte with the cheesecake instead—omg, trust me. Everyone needs to try this spot 😌☕️🍰

reviews.author: Melissa PEREZ
reviews.text: A lovely spot to stop for a coffee or tea near Christie and Bloor. Every time I have visited there was a table available and our drinks were delicious. The baristas always present you with a beautiful creation.

reviews.author: Rebecca Sorgini
reviews.text: Literally the best coffee I’ve ever had - the almond milk latte changed my life. They also serve it in cute little green cups which was unique and fun. Even their takeout cups have a cool design. I camped out with my laptop for a few hours using their wifi, which a lot of other people were doing as well. There’s lots of natural light and it is bright and cheery inside the cafe. Only cons is that there is a lot of traffic and it tends to get busy on weekends the second it opens. Otherwise 10/10!

name: Typist Studios & Coffee
address: 390 Dupont St, Toronto, ON M5R 1V9, Canada
description: A creative studio and coffee bar in the Annex. Typist is a thoughtful space for working, meeting, or just enjoying a well-made espresso.

reviews.author: Graham Sanders
reviews.text: Our favourite neighborhood café. Great espresso and breakfast sandwiches and other treats. Sunny, large room with lots of tables and sofas, plus a vintage clothing boutique and an artist's studio in the back. Nicole (the owner) and Anna are fantastic too!

reviews.author: Su
reviews.text: The London fog was delicious. When we walked in, the place was full of people, so it had a really lively atmosphere. There were lots of different seating options, and many people were working on their laptops. On one side, they were selling vintage clothes and accessories. If I lived nearby, I think I’d come here often.

reviews.author: Niloofar
reviews.text: I really enjoyed my visit to Typist Studio. The space has such a cozy and charming vibe, with a cute and stylish design. The service was great too, making it a very pleasant experience overall

reviews.author: Laura Cline
reviews.text:
This coffee shop has the best and my favourite vibes in the city!! The 70s soft rock music is amazing with songs by Fleetwood Mac, Neil Young, and Led Zeppelin. This place is so calming and chill!

There’s lots of comfortable seating and tables, and there’s also a cute little vintage shop at the back. There’s also artwork on the walls by local artists for sale too!

I also really enjoyed the iced matcha and the food is delicious too!

If the owners are interested, I think this would be a really cool place for vinyl spinning.

reviews.author: Genie
reviews.text: Over the winter holidays, I stopped by this cafe on a Saturday evening. It wasn't busy like the other reviews mentioned; could also be because students were on a break. The cafe definitely gave me hipster vibes. I loved the artwork displayed throughout the cafe. They had vintage clothing and accessories for sale, which I found very cool. The staff members were just as cool. It was a nice place to converse over a hot drink. Their oat milk latte was good.

name: Pamenar
address: 307 Augusta Ave, Toronto, ON M5T 2M2, Canada
description: A Kensington Market cafe and bar with an eclectic, slightly chaotic energy that fits the neighbourhood perfectly. Great coffee, great vibe, no pretension.

reviews.author: Naya Snow
reviews.text:
Drink Quality: 4/5
Drink Selection: 3/5
Pastry Quality: 3/5
Pastry Selection: 2.5/5
Price: 3.5/5
Ambiance: 4.5/5
Service: 2.5/5
Cleanliness: 3/5
Location: 3.5/5

Located in Kensington Market, this café offers both front and back patios, with ample workspace and accessible outlets for electronic devices. A medium latte costs $5 (before tax and tip), and while the drink quality is solid, the selection is fairly average.

The interior has a minimalist look, with functional seats and a casual, unpretentious atmosphere. While this simplicity may appeal to some, others might find it lacking in character or intentional design. A "grenade" art piece (see picture) at the entrance seems to convey a political message, which could be potentially off-putting for some.

The back patio has a welcoming, homey atmosphere, enhanced by shade from apple trees. However, the lack of air conditioning can make the indoor space uncomfortably stuffy on hot days.

Pastry options are limited, and while the quality is decent, it's not a highlight. Service is functional but lacks warmth or engagement. Cleanliness is average overall. There are several washrooms in the basement, but they're only accessible via stairs, which may present a challenge for some guests.

reviews.author: Christa M
reviews.text: Charming little spot in Kensington! We came for drinks on a Friday night and sat out on the patio, which was super cozy with apple trees growing through it and twinkle lights overhead. The espresso martini was fantastic.

reviews.author: francis david
reviews.text: Love this place. The staff, the art, the music (not mall music :)) The 2 patio selection and the atmosphere. I’ve been coming here for years and finally giving them some love to let everyone know. Oh and the small music events they have as well are great. ThankYOU PAMENAR :)

reviews.author: April Tolosa
reviews.text: Love this hole in the wall cafe beside a bike store. They have a variety of coffee, tea, beer, and other alcoholic drinks. They encourage you to not bring your laptops but instead to be offline and talk to each other. They also have a patio at the back and washroom in the basement.

reviews.author: Adela Eve
reviews.text: A laid-back, indie-style café with excellent espresso and a super cosy interior. It’s the kind of spot where people come to read, work or just sit quietly for a while. The backyard patio is also a great place to relax on warmer days.

name: Woolshed Coffee
address: 850b Adelaide St W, Toronto, ON M6J 2S1, Canada
description: An Australian-inspired King West cafe that brings a laid-back energy to one of Toronto's busiest strips. The flat whites are very much the move.

reviews.author: Naya Snow
reviews.text:
Drink quality: 3.5/5
Drink selection: 3.5/5
Pastry quality: -
Pastry selection: 3.2/5
Price: 4.2/5
Ambiance: 3.8/5
Service: 5/5
Cleanliness: 3.5/5
Location: 3.7/5

The interior is small and cozy, with a well-designed layout. A central workstation features a large table with seating for 4-6 people. However, the chairs lack back support and are quite uncomfortable - I ended up standing for the two hours I was there.
The café offers Wi-Fi and has four outlets for charging devices. A small iced Americano costs $4.75 (tax included, no tip), though prices don't appear to be displayed anywhere in the store. The pastry selection is limited.
Music consists of slow, relaxing tracks that match the café's ambiance, but the volume is a bit too high for conversation or focused work.
Service was excellent - very friendly and polite. One staff member asked if the music was too loud and adjusted the volume when I said yes. Later, when I asked for a fork (despite not having ordered food), he kindly found one, washed and dried it in front of me, and handed it over.
The washroom was relatively clean, though the handwashing station outside the washroom is cluttered, with items like a fridge and extra tables stored in the area.

reviews.author: Leonard
reviews.text: Cool cafe ambiance. It's feels you're in like a small clubhouse or freight container it seems. I ordered a cortado with their housemade vanilla syrup and it was really good. The vanilla syrup cuts the coffee bitter well but isn't so sweet that it costs your teeth or tongue or anything. There is wifi here,  one table inside with 4 chairs and a slim standing table in the inside. Outside front there are alot of tables and chairs. Price is a bit steep here.

reviews.author: Laura Cline
reviews.text:
Delicious hidden coffee shop near trinity bellwoods! I loved the iced matcha latte! It was very rich and refreshing!!

It’s a take-out window so it’s a great place to stop by while on a walk! There’s a couple tables and chair outside during the warmer months.

reviews.author: Wes Inaba
reviews.text:
Super cute hole in the wall coffee shop sout of Trinity Bellwoods Park in downtown Toronto.

The space is super tiny.  Cozy.  They odder some coffee retail items as well.  Super decorations adorn the space with a hip video playing on the wall. Lots of character.

The service was kind and helpful.  But somewhat reserved.  Not overly friendly.

The Java was great. And the food was quite good.

5 x ☕☕☕☕☕

reviews.author: Austin Flynn
reviews.text: Awesome cozy coffee shop with excellent vibes. So much character for such a small space. Black coffee was excellent and full of flavor, plus the breakfast sandwich was simple and satisfying. Would definitely come again next time I’m in Toronto.
